### hellojtm — 2012-03-14T23:23:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/san-francisco-thermogenerator.1/post-1>

My name is Jesse and I live in San Francisco. I'm not quite 40 years old and have been immersing myself in diet/fitness for the past couple of years, although much more intensely since January 2011.

Definitely coming around to circadian/seasonal eating and living. Dr. Kruse is presenting the best thoughts on this subject that I have heard since reading Lights Out. My wheels started spinning the same way when I read that but Dr. Kruse has the knowledge and experience and also did the work to show the way. Looking forward to exchanging notes on here.

### Valorie33 — 2012-03-18T19:56:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-my-name-is-valorie.3/post-2428>

I am feeling really frustrated right now.

Thursday evening I was helping to move a couch up a very steep set of outside stairs in the cold and rain(which really didn't seem to bother me) and I think I may have broken or seriously sprained my wrist.

The only part of me that is getting CT for the last couple of days is my wrist, my food choices have been horrible, you never realize how much you use BOTH hands to do things. My bf is out of town for work, my son is not much for cooking, however he did go out and get me some free range eggs, so this morning I had eggs and oysters, so I am hoping that today things are better and I can get back on track.

I know I need to go to the clinic to get this wrist checked out. I just hate to go to any doctor since the time that I went in for pneumonia and the doctor said (in a room full of other patients) that I should just lose weight, that it wasn't helping me. He actually said it like I hadn't noticed that I needed to lose weight and he was telling me this great observation that only he could know. At the time I was so humiliated I couldn't speak, if I knew then what I know now... I could have told him that following doctors advice and eating low calorie low fat diets ALL my life got me where I was that day, and that until he really knew what he was talking about, he should shove it !!!

But still I let his words keep me from getting this wrist looked at.......until now !! Off I go !!

### Birdy — 2012-03-19T04:29:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-my-name-is-valorie.3/post-2584>

What a jerk! Hopefully you won't let it get you down for long.

# Thread: steaming in the shower?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-03-14T23:35:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-7>

So I've been building up my cold tolerance for a couple of weeks. I'm at the point now where I can do a 10-15 minute cold shower. I haven't used hot water in about two weeks and I've been splashing my face as much as possible. I also wear nothing but shorts and a t-shirt even though it's been as low as 45F and rainy during this period.

Last nite while I was cold showering I noticed water vapor rising off my skin. Also no shivering during the cold shower except a little in the legs.

Is this progress?

I've found that the showers are much easier to get into post workout. For example after playing a soccer game or doing some weight lifting.

Let's hear it.

jesse

### Mooringsman — 2012-03-15T00:31:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-77>

Well, I know it seems against the laws of physics, but lots of sportsman can be seen steaming in cold wet conditions - UK rugby players to name just one group.

### AKMan — 2012-03-15T00:33:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-84>

You should see what I look like when I get out of my 105 degree hot tub on a -20 day! I think the steam you are describing is more a function of air relative humidity, temperature and water evaporation rates. Good job, though!

### hellojtm — 2012-03-15T04:40:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-459>

Good answers. The humidity was probably fairly high, but no different than it was during the other showers where the water wasn't vaporizing. I'm not claiming it was steam  That would be absurd. Still curious if others passed through this phase or not, due to curiosity.

### ashryn — 2013-01-19T02:36:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steaming-in-the-shower.4/post-71560>

I know this is an old post.. But i fog up my glass shower walls in the cold shower too...

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-14T23:39:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-8>

Excited to see this forum starting! I've come over from MarksDailyApple.

I've been eating a primal diet for 10 weeks, 5 of those following the Leptin Rx to become LS, and have been practicing cold thermogenesis (CT) through either bath or spot-icing for the later two weeks.

Currently working 2 jobs, sometimes until 11pm which has made staying with circadian rhythms and light difficult, but hoping it's not much longer. Looking forward to learning more about these topics along with optimal health.

### aug19 — 2012-03-14T23:43:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-11>

I cant wait to start Leptin Reset once we are through moving this month. It is going to be great learning from each other!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-14T23:44:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-12>

Yes it is, and welcome to the board. Good luck with the move too (I've done it a few times and can be quite traumatic!)

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:24:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-56>

Welcome!! Souldancer, are those blue-blocking sunglasses on your emoticon or are you still midday?

### TrishaMariaPena — 2012-03-15T03:34:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-389>

Hi there! What part of TN are you in? I'm in Hermitage/Nashville. I too work a different schedule but more by choice. I unofficially have Delayed Sleep Pattern Syndrome, where you sleep prefectly fine but just not during the same hours as most people. My optimal time to sleep is about 4a to noon. Luckily I have worked nights most of my career (25+ years) and have made the most of it but right now I have to be at work at 10a which is not where I want to be. Hopefully I will be freelancing soon where I can set my schedule back to what's best for my body. (OK, I know Dr. K disagrees with not sleeping during normal night time but DSPS is real and I have been this way my whole life.) I actually DO have the blue-blocker sun glasses and wear them around the house when I need to get sleepy. The best thing though is the f.Lux software from [http://stereopsis.com/flux/](http://http://stereopsis.com/flux/) that makes the color of your computer's display adapt to the time of day, warm at night and like sunlight during the day. Cuts out all that excessive blue light. It's wonderful and free. Enjoy!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T15:12:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-714>

I had not heard of that particular sleep condition before - fascinating. I wonder if a friend of mine has that - she's been as you have all the time I've known her which is most of her adult life. I use f.lux at home and at my day job (although it doesn't affect much there because of the hours). Can't put it on my evening job's computer, unfortunately. But maybe that's a good thing because I still have to drive home after the shift. Oh, and I'm in Nashville also - Donelson! Very close to you indeed. We're right there next to Dr. Kruse.

### hazyjane — 2012-03-16T01:54:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-tn.5/post-1162>

Hey Clabbergirl! I didn't realize you were in Nashville. I'm here too!

# Thread: Greetings from Springfield, IL!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rojo — 2012-03-14T23:47:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/post-16>

Hello all,

I've been following Jack's blog for three months. I love his insights as well as his sometimes chaotic writing style. I teach composition at a community college so I thought I had seen it all, but Jack is one of the most original writers I've ever seen. His style is perfectly suited to his ideas: a bit wild and rough but with a good heart and brilliant ideas.

I was a paleo/crossfitter for 3 years, but have really cut back on the crossfitting recently. I'm still a big Robb Wolf fan. Now I do Olympic weightlifting 2 days/week and throw in a high intensity WOD every once in a while.

I'm following the Leptin RX (will probably be on it for a while) and doing CT (very slowly). I've got a mild case of Raynaud's and probably a bad 06/03 ratio because I don't tolerate cold well at all. Even with my mildly cool showers and baths, though, I've seen a difference.

But I'm in this for the long haul: I want to optimize longevity, health, and intelligence. I don't care about 6 pack abs anymore. I'd be interested to hear from other folks in central IL.

Keep chillin!

Rojo

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T00:08:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/post-36>

Hey Rojo,

I've been following the blog for about a month now or so and am still catching up on all the old posts. Like you, follow Robb Wolf fairly closely as well as Dr. Bernstein since I am T1D...anyway, I am near Belleville, IL which is not too far from you (like 30 min from STL).

Just thought I'd say hello as a fellow SoIL native!

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-springfield-il.7/post-45>

Welcome! Hey, do you still have snow????

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T01:12:30+0200

### Enkidu — 2012-03-15T01:17:24+0200

I only really started investigating Paleo a few months ago. Started with Mark Sisson's Primal Blueprint, since then I've become a huge Robb Wolf fan myself. I look forward to hearing more from you.

# Thread: Tomatoes/salsa, other nightshades
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rojo — 2012-03-14T23:53:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-17>

Hello all,

I'm following the LRx pretty strictly: BAB, high fat, less than 20g carbs/day. What is the consensus on the use of tomatoes or salsa. I REALLY like it on my grass-fed burgers. I've also heard good things about the lycopene in tomatoes, especially for prostate health.

AFAIK, I don't have any leaky gut problems, but I haven't been tested.

I could give them up if it would get me closer to optimal.

Keep chillin!

Rojo

### MonteD — 2012-03-15T00:09:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-37>

I'm interested in this as well since I make all sorts of salsa's daily. I even eat a spicy meal before jumping into the cold tub because of the thermogenic effects.

### JoeBranca — 2012-03-15T00:13:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-42>

Hello Rojo,

Here is a helpful ref to Loren Cordain's taxonomy of the amount of tomatine in various tomatoes

[http://www.rawpaleodietforum.com/infonews-items/cordain-newsletter-nightshades-part-2/](http://http://www.rawpaleodietforum.com/infonews-items/cordain-newsletter-nightshades-part-2/)

Essentially, tomatoes like all nightshades may be counter productive if you need to heal a leaky gut. But the tomatoes at the bottom of the list are least problematic (I try to stick with on-the-vine Roma tomatoes as much as I can):

> The primary tomato saponin which causes a leaky gut is the glycoalkaloid, ?-tomatine. Table 4 below shows the concentration of ?-tomatine in a variety of tomatoes and tomato food products. Note that smaller and unripe tomatoes have noticeably increased concentrations of ?-tomatine, whereas this compound is barely detectable in a standard ripe, red tomato. In contrast, ketchup, green salsa, pickled green tomatoes and cherry tomatoes are all concentrated sources of ?-tomatine. Although tomatoes typically maintain lower concentrations of glycoalkaloids than potatoes, they are more potent than potatoes in disrupting the intestinal membrane and promoting a "leaky gut"27.
>
>
>
> Table 4. ?-tomatine concentrations (mg/kg) in tomatoes and tomato food products. Adapted from reference 26.
>
> Type of Tomato or Tomato Product ?-tomatine concentration (mg/kg)
>
> 1\. Unripe, small immature green 548.0
>
> 2\. Unripe medium immature green 169.0
>
> 3\. Pickled green tomatoes (Brand A) 71.5
>
> 4\. Unripe pickled green 28.0
>
> 5\. Pickled green tomatoes (Brand B) 28.0
>
> 6\. Green salsa 27.5
>
> 7\. Sundried red tomatoes 21.0

### Phoenix — 2012-03-15T03:01:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-336>

I've also heard that any food from the nightshade family can cause inflammation in the joints. Coincidentally, since I started eating Paleo with no nightshades, the crackling noise in my right knee is almost gone! That's amazing for me, because it's been crackling for over 30 years. I'm not sure it's the nightshades, though, since I changed so much of the food that I eat.

> joe

Thanks for copying that chart. I'd just like to point out that since the chart references mg of tomatine or whatever per

kilogram of tomato product, that it is a bit unfair to have ketchup dead center LOL because I think we'd be hard-pressed to

find a normal person who would use a kilogram of ketchup at one sitting.

I have a mild allergic response to tomatoes; now this is after a very boring/clean diet for quite some time,

so I have had a lot of time to decipher sensitivities and such. Phenol/salicylate foods will give me a bit of

an itchy nose. Nothing serious but kind of annoying. Anyway, I try not to do tomato products anymore

but I am a bit stuck on my condiments, and so things that haven't left my diet yet are my

organic ketchup and some natural non-soy salad dressing, and butter.

Also thanks to Iceman for sharing that info about HDL and tomato products. I have a very good HDL so

I will not panic over my occasional ketchup use.  I do have a high CRP though so I do try to be mindful

of inflammatory things in general.

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-23T20:46:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-5099>

I still eat tomatoes sometimes, albeit the heirloom variety. With no noticeable affect.

I plan to get my labs done in June and now wonder how they can vary from winter to summer. I may have to get them in December too.

### L8F — 2012-03-23T23:22:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-5163>

Beware of thinking you don't have a leaky gut! I strutted around for a long time thinking that I had the most perfect gut in the universe, mostly because it seemingly worked like clockwork, and never made so much as a peep of of complaint or discomfort. Figured out later that it was a bit of a mess...all silently, expressed through my labs, later in life chub, as well as adult onset skin issues like eczema. (oh, and not so great HDL)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-01T03:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-9623>

> **Rojo;18 wrote: Hello all,
>
>
>
> I'm following the LRx pretty strictly: BAB, high fat, less than 20g carbs/day. What is the consensus on the use of tomatoes or salsa. I REALLY like it on my grass-fed burgers. I've also heard good things about the lycopene in tomatoes, especially for prostate health.
>
>
>
> AFAIK, I don't have any leaky gut problems, but I haven't been tested.**

**

Hi Rojo,

If you have any joint inflammation or unexplained gut pain you could have a sensitivity to Nightshades. The only way to really know is to remove all Nightshades from your diet for about six weeks and then have a Nightshade Family party. Here is a link to **eatkamloops.org with a recipe to replace hot peppers for those that are sensitive. There is a link to the Weston A Price Foundation** essay called Nightshades** which will give you a protocol for testing if you have a Nightshade problem:

[http://www.eatkamloops.org/lacto-fermentation-horseradish-condiment/](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org/lacto-fermentation-horseradish-condiment/)

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-01T03:58:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-9634>

Fwiw I'm able to eat tomatoes and potatoes (potatoes maybe 1-2x every 4-5 mths next time I have some will be in the fall) w/o any issues

But If I eat eggplant or peppers my joints swell, get stiff, turn red, & I get serious bloat

My HDL is >70 That said I've never liked peppers and I avoid them & now I'm doing the same w/eggplant

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-01T04:12:08+0300

> **Iceman;389 wrote:** Asked Jack about nightshades in private email. Response "nightshades ok if HDL is over 60 in women and over 70 in men."

or if you have leaky gut or autoimmune disease.

Makes my life miserable. It's so, so hard to make it without cayenne, simply because I can't eat anything at a restaurant anymore - almost anything could have cayenne or paprika in the seasoning.

I don't known if nightshades give me any issues - I've never noticed anything at all, except for I find tomato products (not tomatoes, just their products, from salsa to chili) completely additive - and my HDL is over 80, but I have leaky gut and likely AID, so none for me for now.

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-10T17:38:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-236852>

The main reason I see for using tomatoes or tomato products would be to get lycopene, which is an essential nutrient, and which is difficult to get otherwise in natural form.

Edit: Just found out that water melons have twice as much lycopene as tomatoes, and they should be ok to eat, at least in season.

### Da-mo — 2018-03-11T11:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-236940>

I avoid nightshades due to arthritis in my knees. |Previously my knees would balloon with sinovial fluid. Since eliminating tomatoes and potatoes I have not had any swelling.

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-11T17:26:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-236972>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=236967)
>
> And the highest amount of deuterium than most foods.

Thanks Jack for your reply. If you mean that watermelons have the highest amount of deuterium than most foods, I would be interested in where you find that information.

According to the paper below, watermelon has among the lowest amount of D compared to other fruits and vegetables:

[http://www.plantphysiol.org/content/plantphysiol/72/3/725.full.pdf](http://www.plantphysiol.org/content/plantphysiol/72/3/725.full.pdf)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-11T18:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-236983>

where the watermelon grows is the key. In the USA they grow in places with the highest amount of deuterium laced water.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-11T18:55:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tomatoes-salsa-other-nightshades.8/post-236984>

Watermelons grow by photosynthesis by consuming water and CO2. So this table above is operational for that one point in time in which it was made.......to know the content you need to understand the source of water. 99% of watermelons grow in the Southwest and California.

# Thread: Hi from Melbourne, Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kiera2 — 2012-03-14T23:59:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-20>

I'm so excited to see this new forum. Thanks Jack!

I've been following the Leptin Rx for 4 months, CT for 3.5 weeks. I lost 10Kg in 10 weeks, then started CT and stalled. But doing CT has _cured_ my degenerative disc disease! Yes, cured it. In 2 weeks. I had been severely physically limited since 2003; couldn't do any gardening or heavy housework; couldn't lift more than 2Kg.

Four weeks ago, without thinking, I moved a pot of broth to the back burner, and was laid out with a migraine for the rest of the day. That is what always happened if I lifted too much, or not close in to my body.

Yesterday I held 4Kg at arm's length. I felt no buzzing down my leg, and felt no after effects. I can't believe how strong I feel. I am eternally grateful for the work Jack has done, and his willingness to share it with anyone who wants to know how to live an optimal life.

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-15T00:26:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-61>

Hey! Coriander!!

I'm having similar results re: spine issues. It's so awesome I can now bend to my left again, which means so much better turns skiing

I'm now regularly submerging up to my chin... thinking it might help the chronic headaches

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:27:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-65>

Hi Coriander!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### Jane — 2012-03-15T00:53:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-120>

Hi Coriander and Souldanzer,

Fantastic! I have a herniated disc in my neck and one in my lower lumbar region that causes numbness and tingling in my left leg. You give me inspiration!

Jane

### fitness@home — 2012-03-15T03:46:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-400>

Hi Coriander. Your progress is great!

### Rojo — 2012-03-15T03:50:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-410>

Welcome, Coriander! It's good to see you over here!

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-15T03:59:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-420>

Jane, the first symptoms I noticed, in 2003, were numb patches on my legs; and in about 6 months I couldn't feel heat or cold on my left, from my bra down to my toes. I had such pain in my hip I was limping. But there was nothing wrong with my hip.

In only the second week of CT I noticed I could feel the cold in patches on my leg. Then in the next two days, it felt totally normal! I could feel the cold all over. Then I realised I no longer had the buzzing down my leg when I lifted.

I am stunned that CT has worked this fast. Maybe because I was following the Leptin Rx for months already? I had also been following the GAPS diet for 2 years. I can only guess that my O6:O3 ratio must be good; I can't get it tested in Australia.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T04:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-422>

Hey Coriander!!! good to see you over here from MDA

### caroline — 2012-03-15T04:56:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-497>

Hi Coriander - I have been following your progress - you are doing so well

### mumone — 2012-03-15T05:06:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-516>

Hi Coriander

ive followed your progress over at MDA, u r an inspiration

Im - Leptin Rx/ Keto paleo/ Leaky Gut Rx

and CTing every day and its doing something -hard to define just what yet but i can feel my body changing- Im trying to get my chin in to try and reduce the double chins i seem to have gained  its a very interesting non linear process.... no weight loss yet (just perpetual gain)

Anyway its grreat to be here with you all, you are a constant source of inspiration for me.

### Mindymoo — 2012-03-16T10:46:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-1347>

Hi all - great to see some fellow Aussies on here!

Have been following the MDA threads for a while, which is how i found Dr Kruse - amazing stuff! Have always followed a fairly clean diet, but have really struggled with an extra 20 pounds that doesn't want to let go! Have been crossfitting for the last 10 or so months (& am totally addicted) which is where i first started hearing about Paleo, although it wasn't that disimiliar to how i was already eating! Then i found Primal which really seemed to resonate with me.

Was already on the coconut oils etc, doing some IF and, while not following a low fat diet, after reading Primal Blueprint i really upped my fat intake. Within about 2 weeks, while the scales didn't move, my clothes got looser & about 3 people commented on how much weight i had lost! Then i found Dr Kruse (only about 2 months ago) and started applying some of his principles - upping my b'fast protein, no snacking etc. Lost 2 kilos in less than a month!!

Am now on holidays travelling for a month (home end of next week) so it has been next to impossible to follow, but i don't feel like i have put on much, if any weight. It's also given me heaps of time to read, read, read all this amazing info - can't wait to get home & really get stuck into it & trying the CT!

Have learnt so much from reading the threads & look forward to continuing on the journey with all of you

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-16T22:00:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-melbourne-australia.11/post-1670>

Hi Aussie friends. The Kiwi crowd seems decidedly smaller than the Australian contingency, but we should probably all stick together since our seasons are in sync. I'm really not looking forward to the dropping temps while doing CT! It was 7 degrees this morning in Christchurch!

Coriander, did you ever hear anything from Spectracell re: telomere testing? Or a doc referral from Drs M&M?

I'm really please we're heading into winter. It means the tap water temperature will drop down, so we can ease into colder and colder baths, and maybe won't need to buy ice.

I am just trying to get caught up on CT, Leptin Reset and after I finish Dr. K's blogs for March, I will be heading over to the MDA thread and begin going through that monster  I am excited that we are heading into colder weather which should help with the CT. # Thread: Hello from North Carolina!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### aug19 — 2012-03-15T00:01:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-26>

Hi, I am Michelle. I live in North Carolina. We are smack dab in the middle of moving from the Charlotte area to the Greensboro area. I found Dr. Kruse about a month ago and have been insatiably reading his materials. I am also in graduate school and it has been hard to concentrate on what I should be reading for what I want to be reading (and re-reading) Quilt papers. Some of his papers I have to read more than one time to absorb 100%. I need this leptin reset bad.

My story is long -

In 2008 I felt I was in the prime of my health. Recently losing 80 pounds (260-180's) by working out 2 hours a day and eating a low carb diet. After 7 months I found myself suffering from heart racing, exhaustion, strange sensations like waking up feeling like a lightening bolt was going through me at the first moment of waking up, any kind of adrenaline being uncomfortable. My marriage was great, kids were great doctors just wanted to tell me I was depressed. Couldn't help me in any other way so I began to read everything I could get my hands on.

Miraculously met a compounding pharmacist. He did saliva test on me and cortisol was wacked. Put me on some adrenal fatigue vitamins from Dr. Wilson (?). Introduced me to an open minded doctor in my area that did every test under the sun including spectracell to find out a lot of what I was deficient in. She worked with me for two years. At the same time a few of my family member's sons (13 of them on the Autism Spectrum) were showing great results from a GF/CF diet. With nothing to lose I experimented on myself with it and got some relief from whatever was going on.

I lived on the GAPS diet from 2010-2011 until in June I ran across the Paleo diet. I started reading everything I could get my hands on regarding this and was relieved that there were more people who ate like me in the world. Through MDA I found Jack Kruse.

We are in the middle of moving but come April 2nd I cant wait to start Leptin Reset with CT.

I know I have some leaky gut issues, some inflammation issues and who knows what else. Can't wait to get a full set of labs to show a before/after.

I will be watching this forum daily

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-49>

Hi Michelle!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### Jane — 2012-03-15T01:53:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-214>

Hi Michelle,

It's interesting how we have to take our health into our own hands while still paying ungodly premiums for CW health insurance. I hope to some day be so Optimal that I can cut back on everything but catastrophic emergency care.

### jreyn07@live.com — 2012-03-15T01:56:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-221>

go forward and succeed!

### Martin — 2012-03-15T13:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.15/post-661>

> **aug19;27 wrote:** Put me on some adrenal fatigue vitamins from Dr. Wilson (?).

Hi there! I grew up in Charlotte. I still think it's home sometimes.

all the best,

### miper70 — 2012-03-15T00:03:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-28>

Hi Dr. Kruse,

First of all, I just want to say thank you from the bottom of my heart for all that you do. I feel you have literally saved my life.

I have a quick question. I know you probably don't remember answering me a while back, but I am the one who just got diagnosed with addisons recently from using hydrocortisone for too long. I also have pretty severe back problems from it. I started taking a pretty high dose of vitamin k a few months ago (45mg) and am wondering if I should still take the high dose or if I can lower it. The back has gotten a little better.

Michele

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-03-15T00:06:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-31>

Hey, have you considered getting an osteocalcin level drawn?

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-15T00:28:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-68>

> **RiverNeighbor;32 wrote:** Hey, have you considered getting an osteocalcin level drawn?

Do you know what a good level would be?

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-03-15T00:35:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-88>

> **Souldanzer;69 wrote:** Do you know what a good level would be?

I don't even know if the reference range is standard amongst labs....

### healthnut — 2012-03-15T04:29:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-450>

Metametrix does underboxylated osteocalcin. The results give you a chart & percentile reference ranges.

### healthnut — 2012-03-15T04:31:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-451>

osteocalcin - - - should be in the teens at minimum.

K1 should be 2.0 or greater

Target ucoc to be 0.1 or the bottom end of the first quartile.

### Dextery — 2012-03-15T13:45:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-664>

> **miper70;29 wrote:** Hi Dr. Kruse,
>
>
>
> First of all, I just want to say thank you from the bottom of my heart for all that you do. I feel you have literally saved my life.
>
> I have a quick question. I know you probably don't remember answering me a while back, but I am the one who just got diagnosed with addisons recently from using hydrocortisone for too long. I also have pretty severe back problems from it. I started taking a pretty high dose of vitamin k a few months ago (45mg) and am wondering if I should still take the high dose or if I can lower it. The back has gotten a little better.
>
>
>
> Michele

Are you combining the K2 with Vit D3? They go hand in hand together in repairing the body. Also there are good reports that cold thermogenesis has repaired degenerative disk disease DDD over on the MDA monster thread as well as other back problems.

### miper70 — 2012-03-15T14:04:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-676>

I am...I just got a bunch of test results back this week and my d3 level is 97, so doing good there. In fact, I decided to back down from 10,000 to 5,000 a couple of days a week. I am slowly starting the CT and hope to be able to do the compression shirts with ice in the next month or so.

The high dose vitamin k has allowed me to be able to sleep through the night. I only get pain in the last couple of hours before waking now. I just don't know how long a person can take this high of an amount without negative side effects. I've thought of backing down to 15mg which is still a pretty high dose, but I am so scared of going backwards.

### Dextery — 2012-03-15T17:55:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-844>

> **August;802 wrote:** Which form of K2 is best . . MK4 . . or MK7? (or would it make more sense to supplement both)
>
>
>
> Is there a supplement that you could recommend for K2 (I guess possibly combined with other stuff like D3) that would be espeically well suited to following Dr Kruse's protocols . . and improving bone health?

[http://www.iherb.com/Thorne-Research-Vitamin-K2-1-fl-oz-30-ml/21592?at=0](http://http://www.iherb.com/Thorne-Research-Vitamin-K2-1-fl-oz-30-ml/21592?at=0) This is the MK4 that lots of people use. 1 ml per drop. It is available around the net...always $61.20 for the 1 oz bottle. If you use DEX004 code you will get $5 off your first order. Some use [http://www.vrp.com/bone-and-joint/ultra-k2](http://http://www.vrp.com/bone-and-joint/ultra-k2) VRP periodically has a two for one sale. Check their site frequently. Once you buy from them they send out emails announcing their sales. [http://www.vrp.com/bone-and-joint/ultra-k2](http://http://www.vrp.com/bone-and-joint/ultra-k2) See if code VES1746 works for the two for one. They currently have 40% off one bottle.

From iherb, I buy the Country Life D3 5000IU because the carrier oil is MCT oil instead of soybean or olive oil which most other brands use. [http://www.iherb.com/Country-Life-Gluten-Free-Vitamin-D3-5-000-IU-200-Softgels/18038?at=0](http://http://www.iherb.com/Country-Life-Gluten-Free-Vitamin-D3-5-000-IU-200-Softgels/18038?at=0)

### miper70 — 2012-03-16T17:15:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-1490>

Just bumping this because I'd really like to know how long I can continue to take high doses.

### Owl — 2012-03-16T17:49:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-1524>

I think 45mg is the regular daily dose for Japanese residents to treat and prevent osteoporosis.

From Wiki: "In the amount of 45 mg daily MK4 has been approved by the Ministry of Health in Japan since 1995 for the prevention and treatment of osteoporosis.\[14\]" So you're safe to stay on that dose.

### miper70 — 2012-03-17T15:18:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-1951>

Thank you so much for that answer.

### Joce_ — 2018-01-19T14:22:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-232902>

Liquid or capsule form K2? On jackkruse.com he recommends life extension super K

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-19T16:40:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-dose-vitamin-k.16/post-232919>

Jack only recommends this for those eating a paleo diet.......and Epi paleo template has tons of K2 in it and it is redundant.

> **Souldanzer;1236 wrote:** I just looked at the Boulder Res website and swimming is not allowed at this time of the year
>
>
>
> How'd you do it?

The Polar Bear Plunge is a special event held on January 1 every year and so isn't subject to the usual rules about no swimming. Though I wouldn't call what I did "swimming"! My first year I did a little bit of swimming back and forth in the hole they cut out of the ice because I had friends filming me so I needed to show off. In subsequent years, I got out as fast as my numb feet would let me. You could probably go in from the north shore right now or at nearby Coot Lake if you really wanted to get that ice cold water experience. I'll be sticking with the tub for now.

"The Cryosauna uses gasiform nitrogen to lower the client’s skin surface temperature from normal body temperature to 30-32 degrees in 30 seconds or less and keeps it that way for two-three minutes."

### Marsha — 2012-03-23T04:02:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-4801>

Now that would be fun! But the video with Dr.Oz on the belowzerocolorado.com site will give you an idea of how big the thing is and how it works. I bet the 3minutes is pretty specific, no matter what condition you're in -might come out too frozen to warm up if you stay in longer!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-17T01:12:59+0300

So area folks are invited to my house Saturday for Webinar. That means you. Pm me!

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-25T17:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27339>

What are you guys doing for Memorial Weekend and the Bolder Boulder 10K ?

Running the Bolder Boulder 10K was one of my \*very important\* goals for AN recovery... and I did it last year in under 53min!!!

I'm assuming running a 10k without training isn't the smartest thing to do for me right now (Dr K?). But it's really messing with me! I feel like I'm going backwards

Then I was planning on getting away with a friend to Shoshoni Ashram - until I realized that meant even higher altitude and veggie diet, which neither I'm supposed to do right now.

I'm tempted to run the Bolder Boulder anyways b/c of my stubborn mind in this case. Considering I also can't be part of the happily intoxicated crowd on the sidelines which I would have accepted as secondary choice . What else is there around here as a getaway that doesn't include exercise, alcohol, altitude, or vegetarian food?? I'm entirely out of the loop if it can't involve altitude and/or exercise.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-25T18:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27352>

I can sympathize about feeling like you are on the sidelines while everyone else is active. Would it work for you to walk the race? There are special waves were everyone is walking so you'd be less likely to feel like you weren't doing what you "should" be doing. Ah yes, my infamous "S" word - "should." Should do this, should do that. So much easier for "should" to rule my life rather than dig into the emotions and figure out what I really want. I, too, am struggling; I'm not able to ride much and can't keep up with my friends. I tell myself to think of the long-term and be patient (which sadly is not one of my strengths).

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-25T18:37:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27361>

Sorry to hear you're dealing with similar things, lyndra. I have a friend who has a horse up in Longmont and it's her life...

I'm not feeling a \*should\* in this case at all. I just remember how much fun it was last year and how motivated I was after the race to continue my journey to health. It proved to me that I was doing the right thing. I was so proud of myself when I crossed the finish line ahead of my friends. I know there are walking waves. Even that feels like a step backwards and giving in to "sick" status again.

I had to work hard for 7 years to be able to run the BolderBoulder last year. Yes, it took me that long to reach this particular goal  I realize there is not point to this if I am hurting myself even more

I'm trying to find something else to do instead that feels like an accomplishment to acknowledge all the work I have done since the last BB 10k.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-25T21:24:47+0300

Geez, I'm no help here!! Let's try this again... be gentle with yourself and back off if you need to. Once you're all fixed up you can ride all you want again. ... better? Still learning to let go of the Boulder mentality of finishing the race no matter what. The price might not be worth it.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-26T05:31:26+0300

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-27T03:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-27781>

I know what you mean about not knowing when/if you're going to be better... I definitely am having my moments with this. A good cry works wonders for me with this - and then I pick myself up and do something to help the issue, like making another phone call, or often I just look for someone inspirational on the web who's successfully healed what I am working on. I need those pictures of success in my mind or I wouldn't get out of bed somedays.

Just got back from getting some serious O2 at Tonic and hanging out with some friends. I stepped it up with super-charged O2 water and a Deer Antler Tonic  This is THE place for me to hang out these days!! That bar has a ridiculous oxygen level in the air b/c so many people are hooked up to O2. I'm feeling like a million bucks now  Well, almost. Much better than this morning. I told Michael to expect me back until my prescription O2 comes through.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-28T18:48:50+0300

> **Souldanzer;28146 wrote:** How'd it go?

I did get out for an easy ride with my husband. I am deliberately not gathering data so as not to be bummed out with how slow I am, but I can still tell I'm slower than a slug. But, I did enjoy it and that is what I am after these days.

What are you doing today?

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-28T19:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-28058>

Sorry about your cat being sick. I hope he feels better soon. I get so worried when my pup isn't feeling well that my whole world revolves around her until she's better.

Good to hear you are able to still enjoy the riding. I worked in my garden yesterday and couldn't do much either. An hour and I was done! But getting outside and soaking up some sun was good despite. It's all a matter of perspective.

Not sure about today. My friends took off for Shoshoni Ashram without me  Cried a little, then picked myself up and spent 1.5 hrs in a 50 degree bath, shivered for forever, snuggled up in bed and listened to some podcasts I had been wanting to until I fell asleep. I can't read much these days, which is a real bummer, as that is my favorite thing to do really.

Boulder traffic will be a disaster still, so I'll probably stay away from going into town for a while longer. Planning on going to a yin yoga class with a friend in the afternoon. It's supposed to be super gentle, and I'll skip the head stands  Think I might take a walk to Vic's in a bit.

### Hope — 2013-02-22T04:54:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-rockies.19/post-76985>

I am so jealous of all you guys...what a dream to live there eh?? I wish I could talk my hubby into it but he loves DC....serious electron depletion.

# Thread: Omega 3/6 ratio

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PattyLA — 2012-03-15T00:25:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-58>

I have followed the GAPS diet for over 2 years. I don't consume "vegetable oils". I eat wild caught oily fish 1-4x a week. I take fermented cold liver oil daily. I eat grassfed beef and pastured chicken. I started the leptin reset in late Nov of '11

I also happen to have chronic lyme disease. My latest cd57 was 29.

I am trying to do CT but just drinking ice water is giving me terrible brain freeze. My face turns white after 2 min in the ice water and I get brain freeze from that too.

Why is my omega 3/6 ratio off with this kind of diet? Does inflammation use up omega 3's rapidly? I'm going to add more fish oil to my regimen but is there something else that may be making it hard for me to absorb those omega 3's? OR am I probably just using them up much faster than I am taking them in.

I have terrible brain fog now that the weather is warming up here. I iced my belly and that helped the brain fog but now I have a bad headache.

Can anyone help?

### kkcarlton — 2012-03-15T00:29:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-70>

Hi Patty,

I have chronic Lyme too (CD57 was 20) and I am waiting on my O6/O3 test kit to come in. Did you actually get yours tested?

~k

### AKMan — 2012-03-15T00:50:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-115>

I have been trying for 2 years to get my doc to test mine, he just poo-poos me. I have heard it can take years to change (although CT may change that). If mine came back bad, I can't really think of anything I would change. I eat no veg oil or PUFA in general except krill and fish oil. Maybe one day I'll break down and do an on-line test.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-15T00:59:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-131>

You didn't mention seafood? Have you been eating a lot of it? It might help with the CT.

Are you overweight? I think that might make adjusting you ratios more difficult because of all the O6 that's likely trapped in the fat.

Best of luck to you on your journey to optimal!

### Martin — 2012-03-15T03:16:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-361>

PattyLA, what is your ratio? I would think the only way to know for sure is to start testing. I had no clue where I was even after eating mostly Weston Price-inspired foods for nearly five years - grass-fed, local, raw dairy, etc. Not much fish until recently. I was pleasantly surprised to finally test and get my ratio - 4.1 to 1!!!!! Where's that bucket o' cold water?!

I had bad illness episode three years ago and suspected Lyme, but did not present many of the neuro symptoms. My CD57 was 60. All I knew was that it nailed me BAD. I have not been the same since and it drove me to Kruse's site. I found Dr. Ritchie Shoemaker thru Kruse's site and tested my DNA susceptibility to mold, Lyme, etc and discover that I have mold and low alpha MSH genes. Mold, as it turns out, has been my nemesis. And the low aMSH explains my lifelong struggle. Makes me think about all those old houses I worked on with my restoration business in Atlanta!

My house tested off the charts and I'm in the middle of remediation as I write this. As soon as it is cleared of contamination, I'm starting CT and then seeing a great mold doc in Atlanta to start clearing the biotoxins. Finally! After so many years of suffering! A way through!

I don't like to spend money or go to doctors, but all that changed when I saw the pathway that Drs. Shoemaker and Kruse are showing us. I started testing. I see the way out now. I'm spending the kids inheritance to get optimal. No more guessing, wondering and wishing.

Martin

Good for you Martin. Best wishes getting to Optimal. I have been eating WAP style for 10 years, the past 2 Paleo (gave up even soaked grains). I'm better now. I don't like to go to doctors either but I am finally going to one I found on paleophysician network and hoping I'll get all the tests done to find out more about what's going on with me. Huh I replied but I don't see it now.

I am 15-20 lbs overweight. My health crashed 2 years ago. I was an ideal weight at that time but gained the weight over the next 6 months or so. It goes up or down by 5 lbs but doesn't move more than that.

I have not gotten my ratio tested. I probably should do that next. Is there another reason to be so sensitive to cold? I still have the headache that I got several hours ago from drinking ice water.

I eat salmon 1-2x a week. Sardines and shrimp a few times a month. I've got a MTHFR mutation that makes it hard to detox so I don't want to overload my body with mercury.

### healthnut — 2012-03-15T04:42:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-462>

I've heard some contraversy over the optimal 6 to 3 ratio. My last test was 2.7 to 1. According to Genova Labs NutrEval reference range, they would like to see a 6 to 1 ratio. According to Genova Labs NutrEval reference range, they would like to see a 6 to 1 ratio. I've heard in some circles 1:1 is optimal. Get's confusing! The body does need some inflammation to repair, so I think the 1:1 ratio is likely not the ticket.

I was thinking that this was something that was checked in the Genova NutrEval. I'm going to get that done soon so then I will know. Great.

### PattyLA — 2012-03-15T05:24:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-539>

### Szara.loring@gmail.com — 2012-03-18T17:11:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-2387>

So I hope I'm not introducing more confusion on this topic, but I have been a wee bit confused on the PUFA sections of the CT posts. I still need to go back and re-read to see if I can straighten myself out. Currently my thoughts are that more O6 in the membrane is the signal/cumulative result of the "harvest" and with the onset of "winter" the dark and cold triggers the reversal of O6 to O3 in the cell membranes. O3s are needed in the membrane because cold temps require more membrane flexibility. So when we CT we are trying to trigger the winter/hibernation biochemical pathway in the body that decreases O6 and increases O3. Kind of a seasonal garbage collection. Since the benefit of the switch to O3 was escaping me, so I googled and found this interesting article:

[http://jn.nutrition.org/content/133/6/1913.full](http://http://jn.nutrition.org/content/133/6/1913.full)

"....The data indicate that dietary (n-3) PUFA are capable of profoundly altering the composition of phospholipids that constitute lipid rafts in T-cell plasma membrane...reducing T cell signalling.....Dietary fish oil, containing (n-3) PUFA, has been shown to suppress human T-cell function via reductions in the secretion of interleukin-2, the primary autocrine and paracrine T-cell growth factor...."

Reduced immune activation is important for those of us with AI issues.

Any thoughts?

### shilohman — 2012-03-23T18:16:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-5031>

WeAreDevo,

Here is what Dr. K has to say about fish oil supplementation from his fish oil blog. To sum it up,

use your diet to bring your omega 6/3 ratio to where you want, then fine tune with fish oil.

> THE MOST IMPORTANT FACTOR IN THE O6/O3 ratio is CONTEXT!!!!! This blog was really motivated by a conversation I had yesterday on the Santa Monica Pier with two college students who were going to attend the Ancestral Health Symposium at UCLA later today. I felt after speaking with them that this blog needed to be written because I think there is much mischaracterization out there about O6 fatty acids. They should not be vilified indiscriminately. We need an small excess of O6 to O3 normally in a healthy human diet.
>
>
>
> The current SAD “grossly” exceeds that normal excess to a great degree. Therefore, we can not and should not vilify all O6 fats. The real problem we face is that certain chronic disease conditions such as obesity, arthritis and ischemic heart disease, is where the damage has already occurred. This situation results from a chronic stimulation of the inflammatory pathways of eicosanoids in response to this neolithic insults. This is evidenced by an elevated HS-CRP, IL-6 or TNF alpha on blood testing. This is not the fault of AA or DGLA in our diets, but it is the original cause of damage of the disease process.
>
>
>
> The key point is that we should strive for a diet that limits omega six consumption to the appropriate ratios for human optimization based upon an ancestral biochemistry. This ratio is 2 to 1 to 6 to 1 by most good studies I have read. So when you begin your own trek to get healthy and optimized I would strongly suggest you get your own omega 6/3 ratio drawn so you can see how far your past diet led you astray from what should be ideal………if that number is greater than 6 to 1 and you have active chronic inflammatory markers that are elevated, then your first move should be to bring down the omega 6 content of your diet and increase the dietary consumption of omega 3’s before you supplement with fish oil!
>
>
>
> My concern is that fish oil supplements are becoming the “proxy” for good dietary choices in our paleo/primal community. Once you get your diet repaired and back to ancestral balances of O6/O3 fats, then you can titrate the effective dose of supplemental O3 oils to your desired O6/O3 ratio based upon your own blood work. This is the context I use for myself and patients.
>
>
>
> If you overdo your fish oil supplementation and don’t test you could actually hurt yourself and your cause long term. You may even cause stalls of changes in your VAP and HS CRP levels. They may also confound you and your doctor because you forgot the context of why this has happened. Remember fish oil in supplement form is a “PUFA too” and is subject to serious peroxidation and ALE formation when it is taken improperly or when it is adulterated. So it too, can be a double edge sword like the O6 content has been vilified in our community. REMEMBER CONTEXT is critical for Optimal!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-6-ratio.25/post-12406>

It is probably mainly down to supplementation. High DHA fish oil in particular. My wife says we have more supplements in the house than actual food

I'm low, almost no-grain - having evolved that way gradually after first getting rid of wheat, then a period of gluten-free substitutes. I am wary of seeds. I eat nuts and nut butters. I eat meat, fish, eggs and so on. On reflection I am pretty careful about the oils I use, so that must help. At one time my diet seemed to me mainly canned tuna, mayonnaise and rye crispbreads  Once I recovered from that, I started looking at the oils I was using and changed those from canola, sunflower, safflower and all those to coconut oil for cooking, olive oil for cold stuff, butter or ghee if I am having a rare carb-fest with rice cakes.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-16T04:41:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-1269>

If it were me, I wouldn't push it until my cortisol was tamed, my sleep was deep and long, and I was dreaming about doing pull-ups. Even then I'd keep my exercise sessions short, with several days of recovery. I'd watch my cortisol levels carefully (and try not to stress about them ). Good luck.

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-17T13:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-1920>

Cold, if you are deep into the reset and are feeling as if you are LS. Starting to exercise is a good thing. Cortisol should be at its lowest in the the afternoon/evening so begin with exercising at those times. walking is great with the occasional sprint sesson.

LHT- think compound movements- squats, pushups, deadlifts, overhead presses.

The heaviest weight you can handle with good form. think under 10 reps range, if you can do more its too light. Do this 1 to 2 times per week.

Get good sleep for recovery and you will be well on your way........

Thanks for asking and answering - I ride about 11 miles to work and then home a few days a week weather depending. I'm definitlely not riding like the other "tour de france" peeps who fly by me, but did wonder....its all an even path, my pace is leisurely and I really enjoy it. Good to know!

### LightForTheNight — 2012-07-19T23:29:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-39480>

I'm gonna second the lady who mentioned HIIT on page one and emphasize the importance of HIIT training in regulating your cortisol level.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-20T05:29:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-39594>

I just started biking again after 3 months of absolutely nothing, nada for exercise pretty much. Couch bound.

I bike for leisure and transportation and I'm trying to figure out what it is exactly that I'm doing. I think I might get around 15 miles the days I bike but some I do not. I wouldn't be worried about it at all except for that I live in Boulder, CO - lots of hills, lots of up and down. I do exert myself when I go uphill. I enjoy the ride down hill. The flat stretches in between are great, no problem, I go at a leisurely pace and don't try to keep up with Boulder Crazy Cyclists

So, would this be chronic cardio? Or more like walking-style, with short bursts of HIIT for the hills? I really love it and I feel great doing it again. It energizes me, doesn't make me feel tired at all. I enjoy the views on the way.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-20T15:42:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-39653>

> **Souldanzer;39781 wrote:** I just started biking again after 3 months of absolutely nothing, nada for exercise pretty much. Couch bound.

I wouldn't be worried about it at all except for that I live in Boulder, CO - lots of hills, lots of up and down. I do exert myself when I go uphill. I enjoy the ride down hill. The flat stretches in between are great, no problem, I go at a leisurely pace and don't try to keep up with Boulder Crazy Cyclists
>
>
>
> So, would this be chronic cardio? Or more like walking-style, with short bursts of HIIT for the hills? I really love it and I feel great doing it again. It energizes me, doesn't make me feel tired at all. I enjoy the views on the way.

I think it counts as moving slow IMO... and yes the hills could be considered a HIIT I think.... follow your body's ques

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-20T21:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-39750>

Here's my take... and I've had parasites (though not from fish)

I'd eat raw seafood before I'd even consider eating undercooked pork. I've also had steak tartar (raw) from grassfed/finsihed beef.

If I were to go after raw fish -I'd be going to a fish monger I trusted... and go from there...

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-20T22:48:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-39790>

> **Shijin13;39840 wrote:** I think it counts as moving slow IMO... and yes the hills could be considered a HIIT I think.... follow your body's ques

Thx. I think most of it is slowmo  The hills can be longer than what would be considered HIIT technically.... but I figure grok didn't always get exactly 40yards? I make sure to take it real easy once I reach the top.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-20T22:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-39793>

> **Souldanzer;39977 wrote:** Thx. I think most of it is slowmo  The hills can be longer than what would be considered HIIT technically.... but I figure grok didn't always get exactly 40yards? I make sure to take it real easy once I reach the top.

IMO A HIIT counts when you go fast for any specific time, w/short rest in btwn, and no HIIT should last more than 20mins MAX!

I love BRTV b/c most of the exercise are HIITS and are all completed w/in 12mins! I've been swimming several times/week and it feels great. now when I get in the pool I'm just doing it to feel good I'm shooting for a killer work out or times like when I was training at the national level... I figure its a good de-stress thing...

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-20T23:04:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-39797>

I like BRTV, too. But I do have to go back and do the old ones with Suzanna. The new girls freak me out  I'm not doing that right now anyways. I'm at odds with my docs whether or not I can lift (and not sure I'm ready according to LS requirements) b/c of PFO. They keep telling me nothing can possibly be due to the PFO but then they tell me I shouldn't lift or do anything basically! wtf. Either it's a problem or it's not!

I'd say most of the HIIT is just a few min or so. And then it's often down hill after. I swim, too. But that's for relaxation and getting a bit of cold. I'm just starting to move a bit again  Going slow with it.

### LouTheNavi — 2012-07-22T07:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-40187>

I average at least 7 hours weekly cycling, weight lifting with 11 kg dumbbells and yoga. I use my bicycle for all my transportation. In addition to the 7 hours of weekly exercise I do some water aerobics.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-27T05:57:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-47435>

Am I breaking the no cardio rule yet?

2hr dance class in the morning, 30min bike ride half of that straight uphill, 2hrs hike with I think 800ft elevation gain. Somebody throw the red flag my way if I am. I primarily had a lot of fun

I really only do this on the weekends. For now

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-27T06:26:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-47443>

> **Souldanzer;47882 wrote:** Am I breaking the no cardio rule yet?

>

I dont mean this to be flip - but it sounds binge like. If you have a big down from it - might think about that.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-27T14:36:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-47463>

> **Cavemam;47890 wrote:** I dont mean this to be flip - but it sounds binge like. If you have a big down from it - might think about that.

Nope, makes me feel terrific. It's really not that much in my book..... I'm used to other. Last four months have been mostly horizontal. But before that I'd ski 6hrs a day on weekends, in summer I'm on my bike for transportation anywhere up to 30mi a day, and the last 6 years I've been seasonally working on local organic farms.... a workout in itself for several hours a day. Weekend dance workshops. I love to be ouside, I love to dance, I love to move. Now if I had been on the treadmill in the gym for 4 hrs, that would've been different and entirely qualified for your suggestion.

I don't have a down from it. But my body is complaining tonight  But then it does that some days after being active. Not sure what it is except for my bones hurting real bad. Not my muscles. My bones.

Does anybody in Boulder not binge hike, bike, run, and dance on weekends?

### Lyndra — 2012-08-27T20:05:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-47506>

> **Souldanzer;47913 wrote:**
>
>
>
> Does anybody in Boulder not binge hike, bike, run, and dance on weekends?

Sounds like everybody I know in Boulder

I wouldn't call it a binge, but it is a pretty dramatic increase over what you have been doing. Not surprising that you're feeling some of the effects, but sometime you just have to break loose and have fun moving.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-27T20:35:38+0300

Ah, thanks Lyndra! Yes, I needed to go have some fun. I paid for it, so be it. I'm a bit sore this morning, not bad at all though.

You are allowed to call it a binge - a binge on LIFE  Got some catching up to do.

### Ariel8100 — 2013-04-12T20:02:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exercises.26/post-83920>

Regular swimming builds endurance, muscle strength and cardiovascular health fitness. Swimming does burn fat at a rate of about 3 calorie a mile per pound of bodyweight. It is great for general fitness, just not a great way to drop pounds.

### rbu5@sssnet.com — 2012-03-15T00:31:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.29/post-74>

It's great that Dr Kruse has a forum all set up for us. This is awesome! I came over from the Marks Daily Apple Leptin Thread and it is wonderful to see so many of the posters here.

I've been low carb for a couple years, then gained weight from hypothyroid (Hashi's) and of course not getting everything working well, got sicker and heavier over time. A cycle of getting despondant and binging and losing over and over was wreaking havoc. After being so ill over the past holidays, I knew I had to find out a better way to get healthy and live a full life again and that search led me from the Hcg forums to the leptin forums and Dr. Kruse. So I have been doing the Leptin Reset, Paleo since Jan 2. I am now also working on leaky gut and doing one last round of hcg.

Glad to be here.

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:32:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ohio.29/post-79>

# Thread: Hello from Cambridge, UK.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-uk.34/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lamree — 2012-03-15T00:35:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-uk.34/post-89>

Good evening everybody,

My name is Alex and I'm a junior doctor working in Cambridge. As soon as I saw the email about the forum I signed up immediately.

Over the past year I've lost a great deal of weight and improved my lifestyle thanks to people like Martin Berkhan, Robb Wolf and now Dr. Kruse.

Everyday I struggle to spread what I have learned to my patients and to my colleagues. It can be a slow and often frustrating endeavour, but more often than not it is worth it in the end.

I hope to see and be involved in a great many interesting discussions and insights into optimised living, as well as seeing this community blossom!

Kind regards,

Dr. Alex Alamri

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T00:36:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-uk.34/post-91>

Welcome Doc!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### Jane — 2012-03-15T01:20:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-uk.34/post-168>

It's fantastic to see a member of the medical community get on board the Optimal Train. There's hope that it will spread out into quality patient care. Good for you!

### rbu5@sssnet.com — 2012-03-15T01:47:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-uk.34/post-204>

A hearty welcome to you Doc! It is wonderful to see you and I'm thrilled you are educating your peers in the medical community. We sure need you and so many others on board.

All the best!

### fitness@home — 2012-03-15T02:04:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-uk.34/post-237>

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RiverNeighbor — 2012-03-15T00:38:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-96>

Has anyone researched brands and associated processing methods, quality, bang for buck?

### Hurd — 2012-03-15T02:59:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-334>

[http://www.drrons.com/](http://http://www.drrons.com/)

This company is reccomended by the Weston A Price foundation. I have been buying cod liver oil from them and been well pleased.

### nonchalant — 2012-03-15T03:53:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-412>

I've been buying NOW brand. I know if I get a headache, taking one of their krill oil capsules will fix the headache within an hour. So I think it is effective against inflammation, like Omega 3 should be. I don't care for their 1000g capsules, however. They smell sweet, like the MegaRed brand. Yuk. I've gone back to ordering the 500g size from NOW.

### sage_ — 2012-03-15T04:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-471>

> **Sally;413 wrote:** I don't care for their 1000g capsules, however. They smell sweet, like the MegaRed brand. Yuk. I've gone back to ordering the 500g size from NOW.

mg maybe??

### healthnut — 2012-03-15T05:08:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-519>

I've been using Vitacost Superior Krill. I was using Now brand, but discovered it produced FISHY burps! ConsumersLab did a product review and Now Brand had some spoilage toxicity issues and also fell short on their label claims of DHA & total omega 3's.

[https://www.consumerlab.com/reviews/fish\_oil\_supplements\_review/omega3/](http://https://www.consumerlab.com/reviews/fish_oil_supplements_review/omega3/)

### EatingPretty — 2012-03-15T06:04:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-576>

I have read that all krill oil available here is produced by the same Canadian company, so the only difference should be what happens to it after the processing. This means I would choose a distributor that has a high turnover rate.

### nonchalant — 2012-03-15T16:21:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-759>

Good idea, EatingPretty. You also might want to puncture the capsule and smell the contents occasionally. I do that for cod liver oil as well as krill oil.

### Dineen — 2012-03-15T17:24:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-816>

What kind of dosing are you all using on your krill? Are you finding that you use less compared to "just" fish oil? I am asking as someone who has regularly taken 10-12 grams of fish oil for the past 7 years.

Getting more bang for my buck would be great. So would being able to add the benefit of the astaxanthins.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T17:26:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-818>

> **Dineen;817 wrote:** What kind of dosing are you all using on your krill? Are you finding that you use less compared to "just" fish oil? I am asking as someone who has regularly taken 10-12 grams of fish oil for the past 7 years.
>
> Getting more bang for my buck would be great. So would being able to add the benefit of the astaxanthins.

I'm interested in knowing this too. I'm not taking that high a dose of fish oil, but only because of cost/consumption issues.

### zoebarry@yahoo.com — 2012-03-15T20:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-958>

I've been using Dr. Mercola's brand for a year now, since it was recommended by doctor. Incidentally, my Dr charged me $89.00 for a 3-month supply, but on Dr. Mercola's website, I purchased same exact bottle for under $60.00!!

### IceBarbie — 2012-03-15T20:46:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-975>

> **luvmyzoe;959 wrote:** I've been using Dr. Mercola's brand for a year now, since it was recommended by doctor. Incidentally, my Dr charged me $89.00 for a 3-month supply, but on Dr. Mercola's website, I purchased same exact bottle for under $60.00!!

I was also using Mercola's Krill Oil UNTIL Kruse said to use Omega 3's to get the 6/3 balance back. Krill contains Omega 6's. So then I switched to Omega 3 only such as Salmon Oil. Now Kruse says HE uses Krill. Go figure!

### ASHO — 2012-03-15T21:02:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-986>

Source Naturals ArcticPure Krill Oil has no Omega 6's.

It is the most potent Krill oil I have found (much more potent than Vitacost's Krill).

### IceBarbie — 2012-03-15T21:13:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-991>

Kruse said in a recent comment that he thinks "Krill is fine" and he has his Krill Oil compounded. Not sure what he meant by that!

#897 (Cold Therm blog) Jack Says:

March 12th, 2012 at 6:58 pm

@Chris…..I think Krill is fine. I have my own compounded. I should start selling my own personal recipes but they are not cheap and most people complain about the cost…..so i just tell them to buy retail But you get what you pay for.

### JC2K — 2012-03-16T19:15:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-1602>

> **IceBarbie;992 wrote:** Kruse said in a recent comment that he thinks "Krill is fine" and he has his Krill Oil compounded. Not sure what he meant by that!

I think he's referring to a compounding pharmacy. [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Compounding](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Compounding)

I'm guessing they get a source of krill oil in jugs or something fresh from the manufacturer, and are putting into gelcaps for him. So he can have freshly made gelcaps vs something that's been sitting on the shelf for a while, or in questionable storage conditions.

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-20T09:57:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-3171>

I used omega 3s @ 2 gm/day when I lost all my weight. Now that the weight is stabilized (BMI 23), I just eat cold water wild caught fish both canned and fresh/frozen. I am happy with this and eat them a few times a week. I may try krill but am finding my body seems more healthy and resistant to things like pathogens and other stressors. Maybe its all in my mind, but it sure feels good and is reassuring.

### Sherry — 2012-03-21T18:28:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-3923>

I have been using krill oil from this company:[http://www.bulknaturaloils.com/Category/766-virgin-krill-oil.aspx](http://http://www.bulknaturaloils.com/Category/766-virgin-krill-oil.aspx). They have bulk supplies and I have been pleased with the quality. They also have coconut oil and palm oil in bulk quantities.

### zoebarry@yahoo.com — 2012-03-21T19:28:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-3950>

Wow, thanks for the link Sherry! Those prices are so reasonable!

### nonchalant — 2012-03-22T21:53:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-4624>

I injured my shoulder recently. Ice was just fine yesterday, but today I was actually considering taking ibuprophen. Gasp! Just in time, however, I remembered to take a couple krill oil capsules. They really work for inflammation. Before the hour was up, complete relief. Ahhh. Thank you, little shrimpies!

Dosage - I only had my 300 mg capsules with me, so I took 2 of them. They really seem to work exactly like ibuprophen, on muscle pain, headaches, joint pain, etc.

### Sarah115 — 2012-08-17T11:54:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-45311>

Krill Oil - [evolution-slimming.com](http://http://track.moreniche.com/hit.php?w=168081&s=171&a=11446) Just wanted to write and say that I'm VERY satisfied with my purchase.

### Huck — 2012-08-17T15:08:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-45325>

My doc (this week) said that according to a recent study, krill oil isn't effective and I should switch back to fish oil. I didn't ask for references because that was a low priority item for me and needed to spend my time talking about more important things. Did a quick search and didn't find any confirming studies.

Please post a link if anyone finds a current study on effectiveness/ineffectiveness of krill oil.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-17T15:30:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-45330>

your doc is not correct.......

### Huck — 2012-08-17T16:46:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-45347>

> **Jack Kruse;45685 wrote:** your doc is not correct.......

She may be wrong, but she has been favorable to krill oil in the past. I know she recently attended a medical conference, so maybe that is where she heard it.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-17T16:48:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-45348>

I agree it is best to get in seafood.......but krill is better than fish oil becuase it has phospholipids that fish oil does not. But seafood has the phospolipids and the iodine that both do not......and this is the ultimate protection of the PUFA that is DHA.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-17T16:49:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-45349>

this is also why raw seafood is best......cooking destroys the evolutionary package protection.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-17T19:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-45369>

> **Jack Kruse;45707 wrote:** I agree it is best to get in seafood.......but krill is better than fish oil becuase it has phospholipids that fish oil does not. But seafood has the phospolipids and the iodine that both do not......and this is the ultimate protection of the PUFA that is DHA.

When one is very malnourished and taking high-dose DHA/EPA while still eating very little..... a former doc told me my body was just burning the omega 3s I was attempting to get into my body despite. I felt like it was helping my depression tons at the time... in fact, I started calling it my "Happy Oil". I kept taking it despite my doc telling me I was wasting my money on rx-grade high dose fish oil.

Was he right? It was the start to me getting a little grip on things years ago.... slowly.....more in my state of mind than my eating really. I stopped crashing so low with my depression. Just... stabilized a bit. Still not eating, though. Did any of that DHA actually go to my brain or did my body just use it for energy?

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-17T20:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil.37/post-45385>

> **Jack Kruse;45737 wrote:** Yes he was......

Well at least the placebo effect got me to swallow tablespoons full of oil .... That nutrition alone probably made me feel better lol. Expensive calories....

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-15T00:56:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/product-ideas-reviews-sources-for-implementing-cold-thermogenesis.38/post-125>

I went on Ebay and got a cold water circulating machine (think igloo cooler with a water pump) and a 22 x 13 pad for 40 bucks used including shipping. Normally runs 150 -200 dollars new. It runs 40 degree water through the pad. I place it on my torso against my skin, underneath a compression shirt to hold it in place. Twice a day for 30-1 hr. works great!
