# Thread: Die Off Symptoms?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 278
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-15T01:12:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-149>

Just curious what sort of die off/detox symptoms others have experienced.

I'm a little over a week into the Leptin Rx and CT. Before that, 2 years primal lowcarb, with IFing regularly.

I'm experiencing poor sleep (though that could be due to the time change), and loose stools.

Just curious what others are experiencing.

### mycrazycartoons@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T01:18:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-163>

Hi Evo.. I've had sinus drainage, intermittent sleep due to being a raging furnace to freezing off and on all night. I've been on the leptin rx for a month and CT for over 2 weeks... the sinuses started with the face dunking and is just now starting to improve. Digestion has been off too... hoping it will all find a new normal soon.

### jodi.wibel@yahoo.com — 2012-03-15T01:19:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-165>

I've been Paleo for a year, but just started CT a week ago. Neither of the symptoms you're experiencing, but nasal allergies are really bad (having to take allergy meds nearly every day) and feeling fatigued in the afternoons. Jack says the latter is from an increase in my DHEA and toxin & estrogen release. Not sure if the allergies should be attributed to the season, or if CT is exacerbating this? Since I've been on Paleo, I NEVER feel fatigued during the day. So far, my CT protocol consists of daily face dunks in ice water and every other day for 30-45 minutes in a pool below 65 degrees. I have hypothryroidism so I'm highly sensitive to cold. This is going to take a LONG time for me to adapt!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T01:39:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-192>

I'm 4 weeks into CT, been Paleo for 9.5 months.

I have had 2 colds (mostly sinus-issues), lots of pimples on face and chest (never ever get these normally), nausea and lots of it, excess estrogen creating chaos with my mood, sex-drive and will to live. Ita lls ettled down after about 2 weeks. My sleep has been poor on and off, but I blame that more on other issues. CT has mostly improved my sleep.

### sisterclare — 2012-03-15T01:50:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-209>

I am just starting out on the paleo diet but I hear you about the cold. I also have hypothyroidism, and I feel like I have to wear, and do wear, thermies, when it is below 60 outside. Even a blast of cold air makes me shiver!

### kalibex — 2012-03-15T01:54:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-215>

> **Glamorama;193 wrote:** I'm 4 weeks into CT, been Paleo for 9.5 months.
>
>
>
> I have had 2 colds (mostly sinus-issues), lots of pimples on face and chest (never ever get these normally), nausea and lots of it, excess estrogen creating chaos with my mood, sex-drive and will to live. Ita lls ettled down after about 2 weeks. My sleep has been poor on and off, but I blame that more on other issues. CT has mostly improved my sleep.

Yikes! Heh, yeah, it's reports like this that have me a little nervous as I prepare to get more intense with the CT...

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T01:58:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-228>

> **kalibex;216 wrote:** Yikes! Heh, yeah, it's reports like this that have me a little nervous as I prepare to get more intense with the CT...

The positives far outweighs a couple of weeks of detox though.

### Bee — 2012-03-15T02:23:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-277>

I'm 3 weeks into Paleo & LR and 2 into CT.

I've had bacne, which I think I've only had maybe once or twice before. But go figure, my face has cleared up completely. I'll take the bacne over the facial breakouts though--not like I can walk around with a paper bag over my head. Also constipation, insomnia (which went away once I started face dunking, but then I took my first cold bath 3 nights ago and the insomnia is back. Also the slight runny nose. Waiting on the improvements in my moods and libido (what libido?), but though I am still irritable with my kids, my depression is completely gone. Brain fog and focus also improved exponentially, but they are also back today.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-15T02:32:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-289>

I think we all have some "die-off" to experience. I am 11 weeks into Leptin Rx and 4 weeks into CT. I experienced nausea after the first week that was horrific. Nausea stuck around for 2 weeks and then itching took has taken it's place for the last week or so. But that seems to be getting better too. Not sure if there will be more but who knows if it's getting rid of crap that shouldn't be there, I'm all for it.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T02:58:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-333>

My husband and I did the face dunks and then started to progressively cool our showers at the same time. We are on day 4 of the baths. The first couple were around 58ish and the last couple have been about 53-55. My husband was extremely lethargic and tired for a couple of days but does seem to be feeling some better today. He was wondering if it was the Leptin RX diet. I told him I doubted it, that I suspected probably the CT. I'm a bit worried, because so far I don't feel like I've really experienced any side effects.

### Claudia — 2012-03-15T03:11:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-356>

Hi Jodi, What does hypothyroidism make you highly sensitive to cold? I ask because I recently got cold urticaria (hives) from a CT session. The hives are almost gone now and I'm back to trying it with cold showers and a cold bath (my first today since hives) for 13 minutes today. Is this what you mean by sensitive? I am undiagnosed but suspect hypot. Getting tested soon, doc. appt is March 29. I am seeing a doc I found in paleophysician network. I've been paleo for 2 years, minus the fruit, although did have some last year when I did hcg diet. I gained all the weight I lost on hcg back very slowly, not because of eating the wrong food, but hormonal (gain weight with my TOM and it doesn't come back off). I have a very clean diet and I have been taking krill oil for years. I doubt I have a bad o6/o3 ratio as my diet consists of grass-fed meats, wild salmon, eggs, veggies, coconut oil, raw butter, and some raw cream (tiny amounts in my herbal tea).

### Iceman — 2012-03-15T03:30:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-384>

6' 165 8-9% bf. Paleo for about 3 years. Intermittent fasting for 1 yr( leangains). Im in tub now at 55 degrees. Cold showers every am (as cold as it can get in the south).

Baths arent as shocking anymore. Sleeping a little more soundly, vivid dreams. Slightly irritable lately, seem to get exhausted 4-5 pm. Just stopped my 2pm coffee though. Had to skip my work out I was so lethargic. Still waiting for some good magic. Will give it a few months. Wife thinks between cold baths and no tv and bright lights after sunset i have gone off deep end....

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T03:58:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-417>

Detox is going to vary depending on what you're working with. My Detox has been quite unique - but then I hit the hormones first - 2wks into CT I got a massive out break of acne on my face - I never get acne on my face - only cystic acne on my scalp. then when I started targeting the parasites and candida I've got rolling around... I've had everything from the shakes, nausea, vomiting and loose bowels...

I've also had different variations of sensitivity to cold...

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T04:32:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-453>

I haven't been doing CT full-swing, just face-dunks and getting used to the cold. After my first day in the tub, I had the runs. Weird, though, I didn't feel sick at all. I also got a swollen gland under my armpit.

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-03-15T04:41:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-461>

My detox has been interesting....i had extreme fatigue immediately after starting CT (mostly 1 hour baths at 54-60 degrees for an hour...along with or in place of spot CT-ing)...this fatigue lasted until last week (doc said it is detox or increased DHEA...got a big zit (mostly have clear skin which, along with paleo eating and the leptin rx has been looking even better than usual), and NOW, have the WORST period i have had in years....my breasts are so much bigger and swollen than normal, my cramps are horrible and my bleeding is heaver than normal....I am thinking it is my body dumping out excess estrogen...it's interesting bc in the last couple of years (with the onset of my hashis) my period has been extremely light, with very few if any side effects (besides cravings)....interesting to say the least...hope the next month is better!!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T04:48:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-476>

> **Iceman;385 wrote:** 6' 165 8-9% bf. Paleo for about 3 years. Intermittent fasting for 1 yr( leangains). Im in tub now at 55 degrees. Cold showers every am (as cold as it can get in the south).
>
> Baths arent as shocking anymore. Sleeping a little more soundly, vivid dreams. Slightly irritable lately, seem to get exhausted 4-5 pm. Just stopped my 2pm coffee though. Had to skip my work out I was so lethargic. Still waiting for some good magic. Will give it a few months. Wife thinks between cold baths and no tv and bright lights after sunset i have gone off deep end....

The first thing I noticed what that I was looking leaner. With BF% as low as yours I'd expect you to notice other positives like improved sleep, massive strenght-gains in the gym and possibly less apetite.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-15T14:15:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-680>

> **kalibex;216 wrote: Yikes! Heh, yeah, it's reports like this that have me a little nervous as I prepare to get more intense with the CT...
>
>
> Glamorama;229 wrote:** The positives far outweighs a couple of weeks of detox though.

Couldn't agree more. I've experienced lots of positive effects, as well, including improved mood and energy, weightloss (1+ week in and I've lost about 5-6 pounds), and much better tolerance for cold. And, this morning, my brain is firing on all cylinders. I didn't realize I was experiencing any brain fog, but comparing my usual to thus far this morning, I think my fog is clearing.

I started the thread because I had seen on Dr. K's blog and the MDA monster thread that others were experiencing detox symptoms and I thought it might help me and others to share what we are going through, so (1) we understand this is normal adaptation as our bodies release all the badness in our fat cells, and (2) that none of us feel alone or like we are doing the wrong thing as we experience these die off symptoms.

### BenG — 2012-03-15T14:34:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-691>

I had a bit of acne, not feeling as refreshed in the morning, headaches, and nasal drainage. Also those cherry red spots on the skin on my arms from the increased Estrogen. Dr. K advised to eat mushrooms to block T converting to E2.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-15T14:46:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-697>

I've been doing CT abt 2 weeks now, had been doing cold showers for a almost a year, but only 2-3 min after a warm shower. Now its cold for 5-6 min. 51 deg out of the tap, yikes. 35-45 baths, alternating between legs/feet up and torso down, and legs/feet in, sitting upright. Also I sit on large ice pad during 25 drive to work.

Die off: ungodly amount of mucus from sinuses and lungs. Oddly, no allergies, and this time of year I am usually sneezing my heqad off. GI upset/runs when I upped time on bath significantly from 20 to 30 and from 30 to 45. When I do the baths in the am or afternoon, I seem to warm up quicker. When baths are at night, I am huddled under the covers, feel like I cannot warm up. Beginning of this week, my skin was itchy, in an unusual, almost painful way.

Facial skin looks amazing. Pores are practically invisible, skin is tight, wrinkles around my eyes are greatly minimized. No more dark circles under eyes.

Belly skin, seems to be more droopy (?) like the fat underneath is more squishy

Glutes, what in god's name is happening here. I feel like my butt looks awful, like it is spongier, has more cellulite than ever. DH says both my belly and glutes are tighter, smaller so maybe what is happening is the fat that seems "hard" softens up a bit before it gets "melted off" (for lack of a better word) anyone else experience this?

I'm going to have DH take measurements, he took them 2 weeks ago.

### mycrazycartoons@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T15:19:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-718>

> **Evo Mama;681 wrote:** I've experienced lots of positive effects, as well, including improved mood and energy, weightloss (1+ week in and I've lost about 5-6 pounds), and much better tolerance for cold.

Along with some die-off effects, I've also experienced positive effects, but the scale sure doesn't want to budge! My clothes are getting looser.. just not sure why the scale won't move. I just want to ditch the last 15-20lbs. What's your secret Evo Mama? Back to the "cold tub" I guess.

### kalibex — 2012-03-15T15:28:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-726>

> **BenG;692 wrote:** I had a bit of acne, not feeling as refreshed in the morning, headaches, and nasal drainage. Also those cherry red spots on the skin on my arms from the increased Estrogen. Dr. K advised to eat mushrooms to block T converting to E2.

Hmm, I like the 'shrooms...time to stock up!

### kalibex — 2012-03-15T15:32:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-727>

> **PaleoNana;719 wrote:** "... My clothes are getting looser.. just not sure why the scale won't move..."

I wonder if this is likely to be a redistribution (perhaps more internally) of temporary water weight....

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-15T15:37:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-729>

> **kalibex;728 wrote:** I wonder if this is likely to be a redistribution (perhaps more internally) of temporary water weight....

I think that is the case but then the question becomes why do some have the ability to shed that fluid quickly whereas others do not??

any number of people have reported that they have instead gained fluid weight that won\`t seem to shed. We know it\`s hormones but it\`d be nice to know if there was a way to encourage the shedding while doing CT

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-15T15:42:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-734>

> **PaleoNana;719 wrote:** Along with some die-off effects, I've also experienced positive effects, but the scale sure doesn't want to budge! My clothes are getting looser.. just not sure why the scale won't move. I just want to ditch the last 15-20lbs. What's your secret Evo Mama? Back to the "cold tub" I guess.

No secret. We are all different, and will respond to CT in different ways. There could be a ton of reasons why you are not losing weight just yet, but more importantly YOUR CLOTHES ARE GETTING LOOSER. If your clothes are looser AND you are maintaining weight, THAT IS FREAKIN' GREAT! That means you are likely burning fat, and gaining a little muscle. That's pretty much the only way that happens.

I'm MUCH more pleased that I can tighten my belt at the next notch, than the 5-6 pounds I've lost (in fact, since I moved last week from LC to VLC, some of this weight loss could just be water). It's so hard to give up the Neolithic dogma about "ideal weight." If you keep getting slimmer, who cares what your weight is. I'm trying myself to shift to this perspective, as well.

Anyway, I'd say you're making progress, so jump in that cold tub, PaleoNana!

### mycrazycartoons@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T16:00:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-743>

Good point... it could be water retention-- then cutting salt intake might help.

### mycrazycartoons@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T16:01:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-744>

Good point... it could be water retention-- then cutting salt intake might help.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T16:27:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-765>

We are on the cutting edge of this, a large beta-test, _ifyouwill_, and that any odd or out of the ordinary symptoms we experience can be shared here for the greater good. It sounds like Dr. Kruse has already done preliminary testing on himself and individuals in his practice, so many of the symptoms we're reporting, he is already familiar with. But sharing these symptoms here is quite useful as it helps us to not feel alone/crazy/psychosomatic as we embark on this new process. We may have some similar and some very different experiences. Even so, I find it interesting to learn about these things as not all of them may be happening because of CT - what with all the factors each of us has in our lives every day that might affect our symptoms.

Don't usually run the AC in my car unless it's crazy hot. I am in NY. I prefer fresh air and open windows in the car. At home, we have the AC going but to not spend too much money it is set at around 72. I'm pretty sure my H won't agree too lower it. I have been gaining weight since August of last year, after ending hcg round. I have gained about 12 lbs. but eat completely clean, never cheat, no processed foods, totally paleo. I gain weight with TOM and it doesn't come back off. I kept thinking my hormones are all messed up, but now I am wondering if it's thyroid. I eat plenty of coconut oil, which is supposed to be good for thyroid, and I put it on my skin so my skin isn't very dry, but it used to be. No nausea with CT. I seem to tolerate it ok and I don't shiver either. I am feeling fatigued just doing my normal exercise this week, even walking .

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-03-15T21:12:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-989>

It's been interesting and helpful to read everyone's experiences. I have been following Leptin Reset Rx, adrenal Rx, leaky gut Rx and doing AI protocol for about a week. Added CT last week. Did face dunks twice and my sinuses couldn't take it. Now I have bad congestion - haven't had a sinuse attack in over two years. I moved to ice packs and since I was tolerating that I add cold tub before bed - 55 degrees for 20 min. My Raynauds flares up a bit and it takes me a long time to warm up. I do ice packs in the morning and cold tub at night. I'm sure my O6s are high and I know my hormones are a mess. (no TOM since doing hcg in July 2011) I gained three pounds since starting leptin Rx. I am so tired everyday. End up going to bed early and have weird dreams. One dream I was in a plane and Dr K was pilot! Lol

On the positive, i have noticed i can wear heels all day when usually I only last a few hours. And, this could be coincidental, but last week I had my annual appt and the doc felt something on my ovary. I started CT right after that. A week later I had the ultrasound and nothing was there!

### Sherry — 2012-03-15T21:49:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-1022>

My detox symptoms have been varied. I've been on Leptin Rx since Jan 1 and started CT when Dr. Kruse blogged about the process. My symptoms have basically been severe menopausal-type symptoms (heat, weight gain, insomnia, foggy brain) with my body heating up and insomnia being the worst over the last 12 years. I've not been to a Western doc in 25 years and have used alternative medicine but nothing ever worked long term. My prior diet included unrefined grains since I was a vegetarian for over 20 years. So glad to have been enlightened by Dr. Kruse. Once I started the reset I had my symptoms worsen. I had to do a lot of self-talk to keep at it because I was pretty miserable. But now, although I've still got some symptoms they have lessened and I am able to tolerate them. Nothing is free. And if I look at this in comparison to other therapies I've tried over the years this is nothing short of a miracle. I can live with whatever arises during this process. Thanks everyone for all of your input. I've been learning a lot on the blog although rarely took part in the discussion.

> And like you Teenie Leek my belly and thighs seem to have turned kind of spongy - I do hope it's a prelude to losing the fat - it looks ODD! How have you all got on with loose skin after losing fat - does the cold help tighten things up?

I've lost almost 50lb and have no loose skin whatsoever. I've also seen a lot of success stories at MDA where people have lost well over a hundred pounds and have no loose skin. I think that it's because the diet is solid, not only removing fat, but giving the skin what it needs to recover. Also, on the B/A pic thread there, people have mentioned that most of what is considered "loose skin" is actually just fat still there. If you can pinch it and get a thick pinch (instead of the thinness you get on your ankle or wrist bone), it's fat.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-23T18:07:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-5021>

this week I have had to cut back on CT because I ended up with a fever. got up to 101F which is high for me because my basal body temp is 96.5F

At first I thought it was detox from CT and in some ways I still do. the fever has lasted 3 days but what has accompanied the fever has been serious draining of the sinuses and a weird rash.

it's the rash that makes me think this might be connected to CT as it only showed up in 3 places and only after my longest (40 min) and coldest (48F) soak yet.

under my chest to the left (so over the liver) and a bit between the breast area (duodenum?), in the bikini area (over the ovaries and uterus) and over my left kidney in the small of my back. none of these rashes were connected to each other but rose separately with the chest first, then the groin and kidney areas after.

this wasn't raised bumps but a red flush like a niacin flush and was very discernable but did not itch at all. it lasted 2 1/2 days and then subsided. The fever finally left yesterday but the draining is still going on.

very odd, particularly the placement. anyone run into anything like that or have any ideas of what might cause this?

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-25T18:00:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-5828>

> **Susan M.;5583 wrote:** What does CRON mean? Thanks.

Calorie reduction somethingsomething. Calorie reduction has been the only proven method to extending longevity, but the problem is, almost no one can be successful at just deciding to reduce calories (and ignoring hunger for life). What Dr. Kruse proposes, and I have personally seen happening to me, is a way to cause the body to reduce hunger naturally, so you don't want all those gratuitous calories - your body becomes efficient with the nutrient-dense wonder food you give it, as it heals hormonal/neurotransmitter/receptor problems that were requiring superfluous calories to stay functioning.

I needed lunch for the first time in five months yesterday - \*needed\* it. I don't think it was CT,, though; it felt related to the tachycardia/muscle weakness I've been having that seems to be a result of overdoing the sodium/potassium balance.

### Sherry — 2012-03-26T01:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-6090>

Dr. K said that our symptoms can be in reverse order of those we've had previously that could have been associated with a disease process. But, I've got a rash all over the bottom part of my head and it itches. I've never had anything like that before so I'm not sure what it could be associated with from previously in my life. It's been there for a month since starting CT. I did have an outbreak of a psoriasis-type patch on my elbow that I remember having about 10 years ago. It's almost gone now. And, I had a small, dry skin scaling-type thing that I thought of having checked by my dermatologist except that it's gone now.

I'm very thankful to have this forum though. It feels as if I have kindred souls helping me along the path. I am having some of my initial CT symptoms reappear (loose stools, nausea, hot flashes, sleep interruption) after they have been gone for a while and that gets frustrating but it does help to hear about everyone else's experiences.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-06T23:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-12281>

I'm not much of a paranoia person, but to me, tooth pain a few weeks after an extraction means I better entertain the possibility of infection and head it off, b/c the way the dental profession addresses it is not pretty, and ignoring it can literally lead to a brain infection - BUT, it is very easy to head it off.

If vit C gives you the runs, that means you've taken more than you need. That's why it's so easy to self-administer - it's water-soluble and any excess gets excreted.

Some people hit "bowel tolerance" (bubbly tummy, cramps, runs, depends) at just 4gm (4,000mg), even with an acute infection, and never need another dose. Others (me!) need to start at 5gm with any immune challenge and head up from there. I'd take 2,000mg (like two packets of Emergen-C) just to see what happens. (Unless your normal tolerance is far less than even that)

If nothing happens, take two more a few hours later. Repeat. If you hit bowel tolerance below about 8gm, you probably don't have an infection ... but you might. It doesn't hurt to keep taking it to just below BT for a few days and see if your tolerance goes down (meaning you've fought and conquered something).

When I have an infection, I usually can take 18gm (spread throughout the day, spread WELL!), but have at times taken 40+ and not hit BT. I'm sadly deficient.

> **Maggie;12336 wrote:** Thanks, MamaGrok, I'll give it a try. Vit C in large doses gives me terrible runs, but it's worth a shot. Maybe I can space doses out throughout the day. But what did you mean by "could save your face"? Might a need a face transplant?!! Seriously what are you thinking of?

I believe I have a very sick liver. My 2.5 years paleo came with very high FBG numbers and heavy lifting just made it worse. I am doing this to see if I can correct that, let's hope it works.
>
>
>
> so yo mean to tell me I may get rashes too, ugh..........

Definitely take it slow on the CT. That sounds like a fairly extreme detox, and there could also be something else going on. I would make sure you feel recovered from this before CTing again.

I wanted to echo sore muscles, super dry skin, loose stools on and off, as well as sinus headache/drip/congestion. That said, I'm still generally feeling amazing!

Always great to hear I'm not alone.

\---

I am here: [http://tapatalk.com/map.php?n1r1hc](http://http://tapatalk.com/map.php?n1r1hc)

### Inger — 2012-04-11T13:11:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-13679>

I got a boil a few days a go.. so many years since I had these.. but not too big and on my shoulder so thats good. It is healing up.

Then I had so strange detox last 1,5 weeks. Such a anxious feeling in my stomach, without reason. Actualy, when I think about it, it started out with heart-palpations! Then.. the anxiuosness followed or kind of developed under the surface.. So awful! It felt frozen and hard in there.. literally in my stomach-area. I was easily irritated.. I did not felt happy and peaceful like I normally do!! I was scared it will always stay that way but now it is gone...!!! I am SO thankful for that. I am sure it was a hormone-thing. It makes me feel sad for all women that have messed up hormones - to feel that way every day must be real bad.

Huh.. what will come the next... (it started out with sinus infection the day after my first CT... the sinusitis was not as bad as this "anxoius" thing!)

I take milkthistle and B and K vitamins religiously now! I drink detox tees too. I did not do that in the beginning. I recommend every women to do it and take it slowly - it looks like otherwise you got to suffer quite a lot..hmm.

I have cut back on my CT too, I just do something every day, but not too much! I WANT do do really much, but... I know it is better for my body to gradually increase - just like Jack tells us to do.

Inger

### Mei — 2012-04-16T17:30:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-15580>

Ealachan: Hmm! Depending on what you've been eating up until now, could be that the lettuce was too high in fiber for your recovering gut. As you get healthier, I find, you get more sensitive to foods you thought were fine - perhaps because you know what good feels like, and then start to notice when your body sends you clear "NOT GOOD" signals. I know Dr. Natasha Campbell MacBride recommends a super low-fiber diet when healing your insides, and moving slowly into moderate amounts of fiber only after symptoms clear up. Something about how the fiber accumulates in the intestine instead of passing through, and then it's a nice airy home for opportunistic bacteria to live while they produce their gasses and bloat you up. This is my non-scientist interpretation of reading I've done, sorry for the layman-isms. I would suggest low to no fiber for a few days, extra fat, and broth if you make it, and I've also read that kefir made of heavy cream is good for constipation, kefir made from milk is better for diarrhea.

Interesting. Up until the salad on Friday, I hadn't been eating very much fiber at all, really. I've been doing the Leptin Reset, so I've been sticking pretty closely to fat and protein with a little bit of veg, and as long as I remembered to take my magnesium at night I had no problems at all.

I was able to eat dinner last night and breakfast this morning with no problems - my stomach itself seems fine. It's just the lower level plumbing that seems to have gone on the fritz.

### Mei — 2012-04-16T17:37:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-15583>

While I'm here....I wept, uncontrollably, for like two hours last night. It was like a PMS nightmare. It came on slow, and then all of a sudden I was crouched in the living room weeping. I took the dog out for a pee, and ended up literally laying on my neighbour's front lawn with the pooch in the middle of the night, crying like nobody's business. (Dog was all, "this is nice. we never cuddle anymore.") When I came back inside, my boyfriend was playing hockey talk radio while doing the dishes, and I had to beg him to turn it off because the meaninglessness of it all was further breaking my heart. Poor guy had no idea what was going on. I feel like CT and circadian timing and the paleo diet are all intensifying my Great Concern that things are not as they should be...but only a CT detox dump could have created that soggy monster last night.

### Mei — 2012-04-16T17:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-15584>

Yeah it's those lower levels that get all the work done... there's the GAPS intro diet, which fits in with Leptin/paleo/etc, but which recommends meat broths and boiled meat/veg until you can tolerate adding more things in. I try to do it when I have any bug, intestinal upset, or what have you. I just read that fresh-pressed veggie juices can help bile production: "When there is not enough bile, the fats in the food do not digest well; instead they react with salts and form soap in the gut, causing constipation."

But these things can take more time than we want to give them, and it sounds like you're slowly improving! Always remember: a good tummy rub can't hurt, and it can often actually help to get things moving...

Dr K recommends magnesium, you might want to up your magnesium intake. Magnesium draws water into the intestinal track.

As far as the lettuce making you sick, if was iceberg lettuce, you need to completely avoid iceberg lettuce. I have read that

Iceberg lettuce in the US is contaminated with ammonium perchlorate. Don't know how much truth there is to that, but

I have learned to listen to my body and my body does not like iceberg lettuce. When I go out, I either get a spinach salad or

none at all. YMMV

### MartiD — 2012-05-01T17:44:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-20411>

I had two very similar outbreaks, first in January(pre CT) when I was doing alot of hot baths with epson salt. I thought I was having a reaction to Sulfates so I eliminated all in body washs, shampoos, etc. I had another outbreak in mid February (post CT)and determined that sulfates was not at all my issues. I decided to go see a friend who was a very well know Allergist. He tested me for food and airborne allergens and I had no food (thank goodness) but 8 reactions to airborne, with 4 being severe. One was dog (boo), others were cedar trees (which start blooming first of year), dust mites, mold and few others. Basically everything I breathe. I don't have severe drainage issues in Spring but I have enough to take an over the counter pill. The Dr. explained what was happening with the rash was I was having such a severe reaction that instead of having the head drainage like most people do, my body was trying to expell the toxins through a skin reaction. He has started me on injections which will take some time to build up but I have not had any more issues, feel much better (although I attribute most of that the new eating lifestyle) and was able to keep the dog (not that it was every an option not to.)

My skin reaction was exactly like a allergic reaction to medication or food. It started out on legs and torso, red bumps that blended later and became somewhat itchy. First one was bad and traveled over both legs, torso, arms and back. Second time was not a severe but still very annoying and took over two weeks to clear up. Hope you figure out what your might be but wanted to let you know about my situation in case you think it might help! Good luck!

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-04T06:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-21264>

Ealachan... Coconut oil!!! I'm sure you already even have some!! Awesome for skin. And eventually it will soften from the diet & ct (this happened to hubby, his elbows looked dirty and scaly and now they are like velvet, and so are his feet and hands!)

Also w respect to allergies, they are just more inflammation and immune issues.. Paleo & ct will help them, I'd keep on keeping on. Hubby just started eating paleo and his horrible dog and cat allergies are no more.... 8 weeks in and he showed all the reset signs, since he didn't have that much to lose. Allergies gone is amazing because normally within a couple minutes of being in the presence of a cat his eyes would water, puff up, and itch, and his head would stuff up. A couple weeks ago he sat in a friends house for 2 hrs w their animals and didn't realize til he got home that he had ZERO issues!! (and the only ct he had done was some face dunking!)

### curves64 — 2012-05-05T20:10:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-21642>

Wow. Last Tuesday, Rosekelly and I (she's on this forum too) and I drove to Santa Cruz to CT in the ocean. It was awesome. We were the only two crazy people in the water and laughed the entire time. The only reason we decided to get out of the water after 25 minutes was because the big waves kept knocking us on our asses lol. I've been doing CT and the LR for 5 weeks now. In addition to my increased mental clarity, the constant gnawing hunger I used to have is gone. My only concern was my weight which hadn't budged. Anyway, after our dip in the ocean, all hell seemed to break lose on the detox front. I even experienced a stuffy nose for the first time in years. I decided to try something different last night and lay off the CT and decided to take a warm epsom salt bath instead. When I woke up this morning, I was excited and surprised to see that my stomach (where I hold all my weight) was visibly smaller, and I had lost over a pound! Fellow CTers, thank you all for your contributions on this forum. It is much appreciated. We are definitely on the leading edge here and I'm having fun playing in this arena.

### SeaHorse — 2012-05-12T18:13:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-23406>

I've been doing face dunks, some cold tubs and a few pond dips for the last three week. I have to say I feel much better after reading through this thread...checking off so many symptoms of detox. Dry and itchy skin...a line of three cold sores on my face, perhaps the virus activating under my skin due to cold...swelling hands and feet which are also a food allergy symptom, but I'm not eating anything I'm allergic to now...fatigue at first which is turning into energy...teeth hurting and metallic taste in my mouth (I think it's time to get the fillings out)...sleeping ranges from fantastic to non-existant. I'm still playing around with different supplements and going to look into adrenal fatigue and hormones testing to update what I already know.

Benefits...I'm actually feeling stronger without doing anything different. I do the same chores everyday, so it's clear that when the hay bales are feeling lighter I must be getting stronger....lost five pounds and then nothing since.

I do believe this detox is a long unwinding process from years of suboptimal living...healing crisis kind of stuff that needs to be managed safely.

### imdreaming — 2012-05-17T02:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-24739>

Anybody having problems with their eyes? Mine have been giving me issues since about December. (I've been working the reset since July and started CT in March) At night I experience extremely dry eyes- so much that I often wake up to put lubricant eye drops in. My eyes are frequently very bloodshot during the day, though they seem to have been looking better lately. However, this morning I woke up and my right eye felt like there was something in it- I looked in the mirror and the cornea was swollen and red and my eye was tearing really bad. This happened a couple of weeks ago as well- both times it happened the day after a soak in the cold tub. My vision is ok, and the swelling went down a lot through the morning. I spend at least 10 mins in a cold shower every morning, so that helped it look much better.

### angieonthegogo — 2012-05-19T17:34:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-25492>

Waves of detox, ugh! More mystery bloating or something. Up almost 9 lbs over the last 3+ weeks. WTH? I'm taking colostrum for LG, DIM, sipping detox tea and continuing my normal routine (incl CT) so nothing has changed except weight gain. So irriating. Sugar cravings are awakening but I've resolved myself to ignore them - I'm pretty sure it is this warm weather. I'm craving starchy/fruity things but if I'm gaining without them I can't imagine how much I might be gaining with them. Sheesh!

One positive note: Recently got my hairs did and my stylist noticed how much new hair growth I have all over ~3+ inches. I've had thinning hair for ~half my life so this is wonderful news that someone else knowing nothing about my healing journey noticed it without prompting. She commented on it 4 times or more and used words like "amazing".

Can't wait to learn what Factor X is (I have my suspicions) so that perhaps that is something else in the arsenal I can use to deal with weight gain or to tweak the routine.

### angieonthegogo — 2012-05-24T20:16:02+0300

> **primat;27151 wrote:** Your tap water is following the seasons - maybe just go with it and get real hardcore next winter?

I know. I'm loathing summer because it is interfereing.  I'm too impatient and have too much healing to do. I've gotta come up w/something, even if it isn't something I can impliment every single CT session but at least a portion of them.

### kathylu — 2012-05-24T20:22:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-27087>

> **angieonthegogo;27180 wrote:** I know. I'm loathing summer because it is interfereing.  I'm too impatient and have too much healing to do. I've gotta come up w/something, even if it isn't something I can impliment every single CT session but at least a portion of them.

I have 10 one liter water bottles (drank the seltzer water first...lol) that I freeze and dump in the tub with me. It's equivalent to 20 lbs of ice (yes, I weighed it). I take the caps off and pour the melting icewater over anything that is not submerged. Then I refill the bottles by pushing them under water. At the end of the bath I re cap them and pop them back in the freezer. A little bit of a hassle, but it's free.

### neaderthal — 2012-05-26T20:30:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-27683>

My last CT session was 8 days ago--50 min bath at 40 - 42 degrees, including a few short face dunks during the last few mins. This was my 1st 40 degree CT. The coldest previously was 50 degrees. Within 24 hrs of my last CT, I got a sore throat, rapidly resulting in a lot of sinus drainage and respiratory infection (mucus, coughing) and minor fatigue. I've taken a couple of warm baths and a warm shower this week. As much as I previously preferred my cold showers and baths; they do not appeal to me this week. I have a history of chronic sinus infections; although, with a "clean" diet (like Paleo), I am usually not bothered by them. I haven't had this severe of a respiratory illness for over 2 years; so, I'm wondering if the 40 degree CT session contributed to this. I would expect some sinus drainage due to CTs anti-inflammatory effects; however, I'm a little shocked by how rapidly the infection moved into my lungs with lingering effects without any improvement in 7 days. Any opinions or similar experiences?

### shah78 — 2012-05-26T22:14:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-27701>

I got a sinus issue from my second ct experience, a whopping 70 degree bath three months ago.After three months I still get pretty hefty detox whenever I do something "colder" than normal. I'm down into the low forties as well. I'm sure it was your bath that stirred this infection up. Good times!

### neaderthal — 2012-05-26T22:21:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-27704>

I initially thought it was from experimenting with the Malbecs for a couple of weeks prior to the onset of my illness. I'm really not suppose to drink any wine due to my allergies to molds & sulfites, but I wouldn't expect a lingering respiratory infection from the wine alone. The wine could have precipitated these issues, but I think the CT contributed to it.

### RespectTheProcess — 2012-08-16T03:03:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-45119>

Time to revive this thread, since it saved me when I thought I was absolutely dying. Consistently been doing CT in a horse trough (creativity is called for sometimes) for ~2 weeks now with water temps 55-60 (good ole Bali'more heat of >90 during the day) despite 100 lb of homemade ice. However, definitely seeing progress and had been feeling better until about two days ago when...

WHAM. I got my TOM, horrible lethargy, sharp headaches, nausea/lack of appetite, and mood swings GALORE. I had been feeling almost great up until this point, except for mood swings and slight increase of hunger. However, those were nothing compared to these detox symptoms. Thank goodness for you all who provided your insight and encouraged me to keep jumping in that tub. However, like many have said, having to go through these symptoms to rid myself of the torment I put my body through in my youth is worth it!

### CoolSwede — 2012-10-12T11:17:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-57719>

One interesting side effect appeared when I did my first "proper" CT in the sea last week after only having had cold showers at home until then. I noticed that the 13C temperature didn't feel that much on my body except in my armpits where the sea water felt freezing cold. The reason was that my cold shower routine did not include my armpits.

The day after I noticed stinging in my armpits when I put my daily dose Magnesium Oil there. When I looked in the mirror, I noticed several small red blisters in both armpits. Some kind of detox/die off obviously, but it would be interesting to find the root cause. Cold urticaria or something else?

### BeFearless — 2013-02-18T23:49:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/die-off-symptoms.48/post-76370>

**Hives--a good thing?**

> [vwaggs said:](/goto/post?id=4839)
>
> I also have an itchy rash now 3 weeks into CT. It started on my upper arms and is now on my face, neck, legs and lower arms. They are not hives at all just small little raised itchy bumps. Is this a detox thing? I don't have any other allergies so I guess that must be it. I really don't want to use steroid cream if I don't have to. My face is looking pretty bad though. Any luck with your rash?

I'm a newbie and I'm having rashes too. I've been combing the forums all day seeking some sort of confirmation that this is a sign that my body is detoxing. If this is a good sign, then I must be doing something right! I got my first rash on day 3. Definitely hives. Benadryl helped. If you don't want to use topical hydrocortisone, you can go with the old fashioned calamine lotion.

But here's my concern-- estrogen? Please tell me I'm not producing more estrogen. I'm a 50+ woman and I don't need MORE estrogen at this point. Menopause is no fun, and prior to starting Leptin Rx, I would get very irregular but super heavy periods. I don't want to go through that again.

If the hives are a good sign, I achieved that with 3 days of proper eating alone (no CT). This tells me I might hold off on the CT for awhile. I think I have about all the detox symptoms I can handle right now!

At first I thought the hives were from a food allergy (like I OD'd on eggs), but today I didn't have any eggs or coconut oil and right after lunch I started breaking out again. So, it wasn't eggs or coconut oil.

Question: how long will these "dying off" symptoms last?

### Marsha — 2012-03-15T01:14:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-colorado.49/post-153>

My name is Marsha, I live in Broomfield, CO (between Denver and Boulder).

A convoluted story on how I got here: My husband Steve has Secondary Progressive MS and tried Betaseron and a 4year treatment of mitazantrone...used in MS treatments. He reached his limit, but we still visit his hematologist, Dr. William E. Lee, every 6 months or so to visit and check on leukemia etc. Anyway, Dr. Lee a second generation Chinese and rice connoisseur was quite a 'round' man the last time I saw him- but the smartest doctor my husband has seen. Very into research on the latest methods of treatment etc. At our last visit, on Jan 6, 2012, I was flabbergasted by Dr. Lee's appearance! He'd lost at least 60 lbs. and when asked what he had done, he blushed and said he'd join the cult of Crossfit and adopted a lean paleo diet!

The next day I googled crossfit and paleo, settled with Mark's site and Rob's site and finally came to Dr. Kruse! Since then I've tried to digest all this new information and implement as best I can the leptin RX and CT (face dunks so far).

My husband is the hedonist cynic sitting on the sidelines in his wheelchair just watching and laughing at me (face dunks with a kids snorkel must be pretty funny!). He's also diabetic and does watch his sugars. However, he's not interested in giving up his tequila, his English muffins nor rice/potatoes etc. Does love his prime rib, though!

I'm not even here 'cause of him, but for Me! I am going to gain back my energy, my creativity, my slimmer body and maybe change a few lives along the way as I set an example of what a 65 year old can do when she sets her mind to it!

More as we continue, that's enough for today.

Here's something to celebrate along with Jack's sunrises, since it is 3/14 - International pi day (March 14):

[http://www.newscientist.com/blogs/nstv/2011/03/a-musical-interpretation-of-pi.html](http://http://www.newscientist.com/blogs/nstv/2011/03/a-musical-interpretation-of-pi.html)

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T01:19:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-colorado.49/post-166>

Welcome Marsha! Glad you are hear working on yourself. You deserve to be optimal!

# Thread: Type 1 Diabetics
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T01:16:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-158>

Hi all,

Just wondering if there are any Type 1 Diabetics here on the board. I know we get a lot of T2s and T1.5s but I feel like sometimes us T1s get forgotten about

I'm a 29 y/o male, diagnosed as T1 at age 3, so been dealing with it (in many ways!!!) for a while. Anyone else out there?

### shawnacardin@charter.net — 2012-03-15T04:53:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-490>

@Michael--hi there. I've got type 1 too. I'm 49 and have had it 23 years. Where are you in this process or are you?

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-16T01:27:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-1138>

Holy cats...short question with a long answer....here goes:

Basically, over the past 2 years I've bounced around a bit diet-wise from low carb to paleo then I started running...a lot. Got up to about 30-35 miles/week (plus weight lifting and some cycling) and the more I ran, the more I strayed from dieting until I eventually was back to just a normal "healthy" grains + lean meat diet. Blood sugars went to crap and eventually my legs just got....tired. I was only slightly leaner than I am now (maybe 10lbs), but realized that what I was doing was obviously not healthy, despite me being able to run 10 consecutive miles at a 7.30 pace (which is pretty good for a big guy like me). So, I figured something was up with me since I was running so much and really wasn't eating all that much, but I still wasn't losing weight, so I started looking for answers.... I really like diets with science behind them and somehow came across this site. I've pretty much gone back to straight up paleo (occasionally I will have some heavy cream or cheese as a garnish on a pre-made salad) and have been doing the big protein breakfast, no lunch, veggie/protein dinner and NO snacks at all. I eat a ~6T coconut oil daily and waaaay too many nuts (working on that  ). I still lift weights maybe 2-3x/wk and do an occasional run (maybe once per week), and maybe 1-2 crossfit type workouts per week. Oh and I'll cycle on the weekends sometimes for an hour or 2. It sounds like a lot, but my workouts are short and I work a desk job, so really not all that much exercise in the big picture. I started with the cold baths and showers about 2 weeks ago and am just now really getting used to it...I only shiver after I get out now...lol.

But even after all of this, sadly, I'm still at about the same weight as when I started. I'm 6'1" and about 230lbs +/- 3lbs on any given day. I carry a lot of muscle and have a generally large build, so I'm really not that fat but def have some to lose. Prob 16 percent BF I'd estimate. Wish I could figure out how to get to about 10-12 percent BF. Running a lot got me the closest, but like I said, I was falling apart doing that and my lifts in the gym were WEAK...once I stopped running, I gained that 10lbs back in water in less than a week and got my strength back...go figure.

For diabetes, I used the pump for about 10 yrs but am back on lantus/regular/humalog per Dr. Bernstein's book.

How about you?

Hi all I'm 28 been a T1D for 15yrs now, and Paleo for about a year, Jack Kruse groupie for a little under a month. I started the Leptin RX and cold training two weeks ago so I still have a ways to go. i jumped ahead a bit and just went straight to the cold tub, not quite ice baths yet, but I would like to begin soon. I'm not really over weight, but have a significant amount of body fat to lose I'm 5'4 around 140, but I think something like 29% body fat yikes. I'm really glad this thread was started because there's a ton of info for the T2D crowd and even other autoimmune issues, but not as much specifically geared to T1. I get the feeling that at least from where I'm coming from things are going to take a little longer (or maybe a lot longer) than the average \*sigh\*. I am also not exercising yet because I'm not leptin sensitive and before i knew this and was exercising was experiencing all of the symptoms Dr. K described, poor recovery, felt crappy, now weight loss etc. I was wondering if there were any T1's who have successfully come out the other end for the Leptin RX and what there experiences were? I was also on a pump for about 5 yrs, but since have switched back to injections Lantus/NPH/humalog. @Micheal I have heard of and read some excerpts from Dr. Bernstein's book, but out of curiosity why does he not like the pump?

### shawnacardin@charter.net — 2012-03-16T18:54:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-1585>

> How about you?

Before I started the leptin reset and ct I was working out like 3-4 days a week doing functional exercise like my TRX and running with my dogs for a few miles. I realized a long time ago that massive amounts of exercise weren't the answer. I didn't know what was but with a metabolic disease when things aren't going right it pretty much is par for the course. I use insulin pens-Levemir for basal and Novolog for bolus. I've eaten well for years but in the last 18 months quit grains and legumes because they give me too much insulin resistance. I've got secondary hypothyroid issues due to adrenal fatigue. Since starting the reset/ct, which was 7 days ago, my blood sugars have been much higher-lots of resistance-and I've felt lousy. It's so great to actually converse with someone who has type 1. I know that you understand. This disease takes every part of your life that would normally involve no thought whatsoever and makes you have to account for it. Let's keep each other posted and encourage each other.

### shawnacardin@charter.net — 2012-03-16T18:58:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-1588>

@Cara-Well it looks like you're in the right place. I hear the part about it taking longer. I'm still dunking my face in water. I'm going to follow the doc's protocol so that I can be sure of my results. I'm just going to trust my body. Just be patient. I know that is the hardest thing for me. We can do this!!

### Dextery — 2012-03-16T19:14:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-1601>

In CT 6 Dr Kruse said this

> 67\. Jack Says:
>
>
>
> March 3rd, 2012 at 2:44 pm
>
> @Maggie I have a lot of diabetics in my practice becoming un diabetic while the rest of the world believes it is a disease……its not a disease. it is a metabolic derangement that the cold fixes. Anyone can rid them selves of this disease if they cold adapt at home. Your doc wont believe it…….it took me two years to believe in my office. Regarding the rest of your questions……without testing Im shooting in the dark.

Now he did not specify I or II so am assuming he means all types of diabetics. He has talked about 1.5 types still having some beta cells but not enough to control BS and they can operate just fine without insulin on a totally zero carb ketogenic diet. But as for Type I people, I don't know....even cold adapted.

I would think an in home deep bathtub connected to an aquarium chiller that will take the temp of water down to 37 F would be worth the investment for a type I if there is a hope of the type I abating.

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-16T22:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-1696>

> **Cara;1517 wrote:** @Micheal I have heard of and read some excerpts from Dr. Bernstein's book, but out of curiosity why does he not like the pump?

He doesn't like it because its too hard to control blood sugars using the pump on the small doses of insulin you use on the low-carb diet he recommends...and he is right. I tried it, and it does not work well because of absorption issues and air bubbles. When your basal rate is 2/u per hour, an air bubble works its way thru pretty quick, no big deal. When its .5/u per hour, an air bubble can really screw up your basal for quite a while.

Also, if you are T1D, you need to own a copy of his book. He was on the Jimmy Moore show a few times, I think. Go search for those podcasts and give them a listen.

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-16T22:57:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-1698>

> **Shawna;1587 wrote:** Since starting the reset/ct, which was 7 days ago, my blood sugars have been much higher-lots of resistance-and I've felt lousy.

I'm kind of surprised by that. Typically, you'd use a lot less insulin on a protein/fat diet. What are you eating, how often, and what are your insulin doses (basal and at meals)?

### clzem1@yahoo.com — 2012-03-17T18:03:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-2027>

> **Michael W;1700 wrote:** I'm kind of surprised by that. Typically, you'd use a lot less insulin on a protein/fat diet. What are you eating, how often, and what are your insulin doses (basal and at meals)?

You know for awhile I was having the same issue, and though I've managed to get my bs down it's still a work in progress. I noticed a couple of things though, some of this may just be speculation, but my thoughts were. 1. for the time being I can't handle nuts of any kind, though they are fairly high in fat the carb load is enough to throw me off. 2.( and this sorta ties into 3) There are a good number of diabetics who need to bolus with protein consumption I'm something like 1u humalog per 10grams protein, timing is also an issue especially when you're eating high fat meals coupled with protein, and/or have digestive issues, and injecting fast acting insulin you can see spikes on the back end. 3. I think for me not being Leptin sensitive, nor yet a fat burner, it's a stressor on the body at first being ketogenic, body basically thinks it's starving and is pumping out glucose from the liver screwing with bs. Then you get a chronically elevated insulin level which promotes fat storage... That's my theory anyways I would love if Dr. K would write a geeked out blog post specifically for T1s because I'm just making it up as I go. I've gotten fairly strict on the Leptin RX in the meantime supplements and all because I think fixing that will go a long way in overall glucose regulation.

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-17T18:42:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-2043>

> **Cara;2033 wrote: 1. for the time being I can't handle nuts of any kind, though they are fairly high in fat the carb load is enough to throw me off.
>
>
> Agree. I eat way too many and simply need to stop buying them. Once my current stash of almonds and hazelnuts is gone, that is it. Jeez, that was even painful to say....ouch.
>
>
>
>
> > **Cara;2033 wrote: 2.( and this sorta ties into 3) There are a good number of diabetics who need to bolus with protein consumption I'm something like 1u humalog per 10grams protein, timing is also an issue especially when you're eating high fat meals coupled with protein, and/or have digestive issues, and injecting fast acting insulin you can see spikes on the back end.
> >
> >
> > This is precisely why I use Regular instead of Humalog/Novolog. I tried it with Humalog because I had a whole bunch of it in my fridge, but its nearly impossible to get the absorption of the food and insulin to time correctly. The best luck I had was doing the shot in the fattiest spots I could, but even then, it still wasn't as good as using Regular instead. The other thing is that Regular insulin is not as potent and once you get on super small doses, you don't have to do 1/2 units or 1/4 units like you might have to with Humalog. The only thing I used Humalog for is correcting a high blood sugar. This is all in the Bernstein book, btw. You need to get a copy!!! Heck, I already have a copy and he just came out with an updated version, which I will also probably buy!
> >
> >
> >
> > Cara;2033 wrote:** 3. I think for me not being Leptin sensitive, nor yet a fat burner, it's a stressor on the body at first being ketogenic, body basically thinks it's starving and is pumping out glucose from the liver screwing with bs. Then you get a chronically elevated insulin level which promotes fat storage...
>
>
>
> That's possible and may be part of it. Another thing you need to keep in mind is that insulin lasts a lot longer in your body than you probably think it does. If you look at graphs of insulin's active working time, there is always a fairly long tail-end on the graph that we often forget about. Also keep in mind that the larger the injection into a single site, the slower it will absorb (and the longer it will remain active in your body). So on a high carb diet, you're constantly doing larger injections that are hanging around in your body a lot longer than you think...your previous meal's injection could (and likely would) still be lowering your blood sugar the next time you go to eat/inject and this cycle constantly repeats. So in that case, after time it may \*seem\* like you are more sensitive to the insulin, but really you just have a lot of "old" insulin still working in the background. The converse is also true...so you start doing small doses, you have less of the "old" insulin from your last dose, and therefore it seems like you need more than you should for protein/fat when you compare it to what you know you were doing before on carbs. So when you change your diet, your insulin dose might not be affected quite as proportionally as you think it should. One thing you could try is playing with your basal does a little bit to see if you can even things out. Another good trick with the basal insulin is to split the dose between morning and evening...so instead of doing 10/u of Lantus each night, take 5/u in the morning and 5/u 12 hours later. Lantus doesn't always make it the full 24 hours its advertised to, esp if you are on a lower dose, so doing this helps to keep things more even. Plus, you are always going to get better absorption with smaller injections at a single site. Per unit, a 5/u dose will be more effective than a 10/u dose in a single injection site. In Bernstein's book, he goes as far to say that any time you do injections larger than 7/u, you should split them up to multiple injection sites so they absorb better.
>
>
>
> Cara;2033 wrote:** I would love if Dr. K would write a geeked out blog post specifically for T1s because I'm just making it up as I go.

I will second this. I would love such a post as well. I've been thinking about asking him, but the poor guy has to be so busy already, lol. Maybe he'll get a glance at this sometime and find it in his heart to throw us T1Ds a bone!

### shawnacardin@charter.net — 2012-03-17T18:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-2044>

> **Michael W;1700 wrote:** I'm kind of surprised by that. Typically, you'd use a lot less insulin on a protein/fat diet. What are you eating, how often, and what are your insulin doses (basal and at meals)?

Well for starters I do 5 units of basal 12 hours apart and that remains unchanged. My bolus changes usually at night. I have had for maybe 4-5 years experienced more resistance in the evening. Typically I don't do more than 15 units of bolus per day. I know this isn't typical for people with type 1 but I've never really been the square peg in the square hole in the way this disease manifests in my body. I'm eating completely ketogenic at this point according to Dr K's protocol. This isn't my first season eating like this either. I would imagine that it will take me much longer than most to see results just because. I did the primal diet 18 months ago w/o any of the frills that Jack adds and had excellent results for about 6 weeks and then it all started crumbling. I continued but wasn't able to achieve any of the effortless results that I had been achieving prior. Now that I know what I know it would appear that I truly need the full meal deal approach to get the results I should be getting. So if you understand and follow Jack's process about the plasticity of the brain then this starts making sense. For me I see that I'm going to have to think myself into this before my body is going to be willing to follow; enter in the resistance that I'm experiencing. "What a man thinks so he is." Every thought you think is in each of your cells. Now of course not everyone thinks like I do, hence why I experience what I do. I hope I'm making sense Michael. That's why Jack keeps hammering about what we think. He's so right on the money.

### shawnacardin@charter.net — 2012-03-17T19:14:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-2059>

@Cara

I do know that the protein has to break down into glucose thru gluconeogenesis. So the more protein the more you'll need to inject. That totally makes sense. Also the fact that you brought up re the spikes at the end has validity too. I have to agree we need the doc to geek us out. I would love to hear what he has to say. In fact I volunteer for a bio hack if he hasn't done one already!!

The converse is also true...so you start doing small doses, you have less of the "old" insulin from your last dose, and therefore it seems like you need more than you should for protein/fat when you compare it to what you know you were doing before on carbs. It looks like I really need a copy of Dr. Bernstein's diabetes bible, and some tinkering with my basals will hopefully lower my correction boluses, prob would do well to add regular too. Thanks for the info.

I was wondering if anyone has any thoughts on the artificial pancreas? It's only approved in Europe, but it seems like JDRF is making a push to get it approved more quickly in the US. I wish VLC and CT was standard practice of care here, but unfortunately it's not and unlike the pump this would be a closed loop, but with very tiny basals I don't know if there would be similar issues.

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-17T22:05:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-2115>

> **Cara;2109 wrote:**
>
> I was wondering if anyone has any thoughts on the artificial pancreas? It's only approved in Europe, but it seems like JDRF is making a push to get it approved more quickly in the US. I wish VLC and CT was standard practice of care here, but unfortunately it's not and unlike the pump this would be a closed loop, but with very tiny basals I don't know if there would be similar issues.

I tried medtronic's CGMS and didn't have much luck with it. The margin of error on that thing was so large, it pretty much made the readings useless. My understanding is that in order for the artificial pancreas to work, we need accurate real-time blood glucose monitoring but I'm not sure whether that exists yet. We'll get there eventually, but I'm not getting excited about anything just yet

### clzem1@yahoo.com — 2012-03-21T19:56:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-3962>

@Micheal, since you seem pretty in tune with Dr. Bernstein and his recommendations, do you have any thoughts on low dose naltrexone? This is something I wanted to ask Dr. K too, but it seems like it lowers carb cravings and binges by blocking opiod receptors, and is used in treatment for autoimmune diseases. With the Leptin RX cravings are way more manageable, but since CT(and I'm not even using ice yet) My appetite is huge, and I don't want to upset the apple cart too much, I eat like a monster, but still strictly keto paleo, and not gaining, but not losing either.

### mmuskopf@gmail.com — 2012-03-27T00:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetics.50/post-6639>

> **Cara;3987 wrote:** @Micheal, since you seem pretty in tune with Dr. Bernstein and his recommendations, do you have any thoughts on low dose naltrexone?

It's not something I personally have tried, so I really can't comment as to whether I think it would be effective for anything, etc. The only med I've tried related to diabetes (other than insulin) is injectable amelyn (symlin) b/c my doctor gave me some samples. The only effect I could tell that the symlin had was that it made me feel slightly nauseated and, therefore, i did not want to eat. So, I guess you could say it "worked", though it wasn't something I wanted to be on long term...plus it is really expensive. There are probably cheaper ways to make me feel sick so that I don't want to eat

Currently, the only things I take besides insulin are a multi vit, fish oil, vit d, and magnesium.

# Thread: Brown Adipose Tissue
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brown-adipose-tissue.56/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-03-15T01:41:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brown-adipose-tissue.56/post-195>

BAT is the holy grail for CT. It is activated by cold, when present, and increases the metabolism by turning energy stores into heat, bypassing normal cellular ATP functions.

BAT is mentioned a lot in the Quilt and the CT series. It seems there are conflicting viewpoints on who has BAT. All babies have it, but it disappears as we age. Some studies suggest everyone still has a stockpile of BAT and that cold can regenerate it and even recruit new BAT from WAT. It seems to be accepted that obese people all have less BAT than thin people.

What I'm wondering, and I've never seen a study on this, when obese people lose weight, do they end up with a normal amount of BAT or are they always deficient? Also, is there a way to assess if you have BAT and how much?

### David — 2012-03-15T02:22:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brown-adipose-tissue.56/post-274>

I read an article on line recently: "_Brown Fat, Triggered by Cold or Exercise, May Yield a Key to Weight Control_, NYT 1/24/12

I copied a part of it, that says:

"… reports that brown fat can burn ordinary fat and that glucose is not a major source of fuel for these cells. When the cells run out of their own small repositories of fat, they suck fat out of the rest of the body.

In the study, a number of men were kept chilled, but not to the point of shivering, which itself burns calories. Their metabolic rates increased by 80 percent, all from the actions of a few ounces of cells. The brown fat also kept its subjects warm. The more brown fat a man had, the colder he could get before he started to shiver. On average the brown fat burned about 250 calories over three hours. But there is another type of brown fat. It has been harder to study because it often is interspersed in the white fat and does not occur in large masses. Investigators discovered it years ago. A recent article reports that in mice exercise can make it appear by turning ordinary white fat brown. ... With exercise, mice muscle cells release a hormone that the researchers named irisin, which converts white fat cells into brown cells. Those brown fat cells burn extra calories. Irisin is induced with exercise in mice and humans..."

I thought that looked quite promising.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-16T16:41:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brown-adipose-tissue.56/post-1468>

I'd love to be able to recruit some more BAT from all of my WAT!

### ealachan — 2012-03-16T16:46:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brown-adipose-tissue.56/post-1471>

> **AKMan;196 wrote:**
>
>
>
> What I'm wondering, and I've never seen a study on this, when obese people lose weight, do they end up with a normal amount of BAT or are they always deficient? Also, is there a way to assess if you have BAT and how much?

I've been wondering the exact same thing since reading all these articles about BAT and WAT and BRITE, etc.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-22T03:43:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brown-adipose-tissue.56/post-153575>

[http://www.the-scientist.com/?artic...912/title/Brain-s-Role-in-Browning-White-Fat/](http://www.the-scientist.com/?articles.view/articleNo/41912/title/Brain-s-Role-in-Browning-White-Fat/)

Leptin and BAT.

### angie-s — 2015-01-22T20:32:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brown-adipose-tissue.56/post-153616>

How can we reduce the two phosphate enzymes in POMC, as mentioned in the article, to promote the sensitivity of Leptin and insulin and increase BAT. Will some kind of supplements help ?

### CTforlife — 2015-01-22T20:43:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brown-adipose-tissue.56/post-153617>

> [angie-s said:](/goto/post?id=153616)
>
> How can we reduce the two phosphate enzymes in POMC, as mentioned in the article, to promote the sensitivity of Leptin and insulin and increase BAT. Will some kind of supplements help ?

Electrons

### Penny — 2015-01-22T22:48:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brown-adipose-tissue.56/post-153628>

# Thread: If I can inspire someone, I'd happily go first!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-can-inspire-someone-id-happily-go-first.60/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 46
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T01:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-can-inspire-someone-id-happily-go-first.60/post-212>

To make it short and sweet, I was a massive hormonal mess after the birth of my 3rd child in late 2010. When the baby was 7 months old I decided it was time for change, and I went primal. Its been 9.5 months, and I have uncovered and fixed many health-issues since then, but many things are still a work in progress. I know we all appreciate a good visual in order to really see the success I'm talking about!

_ETA: I followed the Leptin-rx from July 2011, was LS (except liver) from Sep 2011, started post-Rx protocol 2 months ago, and CT 4 weeks ago. I focus on protein/fats, heavy lifting with multiple reps, 1-2 sprints weekly. Average calories pr day 1800-2200, 70 percent being from fat like coconut oil.

_

My before photos, at 190 lbs:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

After 5.5 months, about 40 pounds down:

After about 8 months (7 months of Leptin-rx eating, 1 month of post-rx protocol)

This photo was taken a few days ago, after 3 weeks of daily CT-sessions. My weight remains 47 pounds down from starting-weight, but the fat is going, and the muscle is slowly packing on. I expect my next set of photos (a few months from now) to be totally awesome!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T04:43:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-can-inspire-someone-id-happily-go-first.60/post-463>

> **Lexi;384 wrote:** fantastic! did you take stuff to help fix the hormones or did you do it all thru diet?

I have been using a range of supplements on and off. Didn't know I had hormonal issues until 8.5 months into this WOE... turns out I'm quite high in estradiol/E2. And my liver=function is below average... so I'm taking EstroFactors (metagenics-brand) to promote healthy estragen-metabolism, and have done so for the last 4 weeks. Its really helping, I can tell by mood/sex drive/breast tenderness. Most of my results on the outside were due to Leptin-rx, clean eating, good sleep and HIIT/slow walking.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T04:44:45+0200

### healthnut — 2012-03-15T04:53:43+0200

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:09:31+0200

I had all the signs of LS within 6 weeks, but I stuck to the protocol for 7 months because I just loved it and it worked so well for me. I should probably add that my 50 grams of protein in the mornings were down to 35 gr within 3 weeks. I simply couldn't handle the full amount. Most days I ate 25-35 grams of protein in my BAB.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:10:45+0200

Sometimes it feels like its been forever... and I hit stalls and mental walls and uncover new issues... thats when its good to look back to old photos and remember just how far I've come. I was a mess, both mentally and physically. I'm partly a mess now thanks to all the estrogen the CT has released from my fat, but I can see the end of this...

Its really helping, I can tell by mood/sex drive/breast tenderness. Most of my results on the outside were due to Leptin-rx, clean eating, good sleep and HIIT/slow walking.

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-16T01:00:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-can-inspire-someone-id-happily-go-first.60/post-1120>
