# Thread: Any performance increases using CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-performance-increases-using-ct.101/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### codys182 — 2012-03-15T04:56:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-performance-increases-using-ct.101/post-495>

I was previously an avid weight lifter, taking sometime off to recover from some adrenal problems. I figured I'd use this down time to couple a ketogenic diet with CT as Dr. K prescribes. I was wonderingÃ¢â‚¬Â¦has anyone seen improvements in their performance, specifically in regards to lean mass gains? If so, what type of workout regimen are you using? I am looking to start hitting the weights again soon, but in a safe way so that I don't screw up my hormones(mainly cortisol). It seems I have been losing weight (only a week in) because my clothes are fitting better, but I am also clinging onto stomach weight and also gaining sensitivity/losing firmness in my chest. I am 5'10, 185 lbs, and about 15-16% body fat.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:03:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-performance-increases-using-ct.101/post-507>

I'm a girl with relatively high BF% (still... tipping 27-28%), and I started lifting in the gym 4 weeks ago, around the time I started CT. It might be partly due to beginner's growth, but I have doubled my squats from 15 kg to 30 kg in less than 4 weeks (3 sessions). I do the major ones, like squat, deadlift, bench, hamstring curls, pull-ups (working on them...) and complimentary triceps/biceps-work with R60-equip. I'm all about barbells and dumbells. My fat is going, scales remains the same, but strenght is very steadily increasing, and quicker than I can recall from my past experiences in the gym. I fuel my WOs with fat and protein, and so far it seems to be going well. I let each msucle-group rest for 7 days before I hit it again.

### codys182 — 2012-03-15T05:17:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-performance-increases-using-ct.101/post-531>

Wow, very impressive. What rep ranges do you use? And how long are your workouts typically? I am worried about tapping into my glycogen stores a bit too much without having the carbs to replete them.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:21:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-performance-increases-using-ct.101/post-534>

> **codys182;532 wrote:** Wow, very impressive. What rep ranges do you use? And how long are your workouts typically? I am worried about tapping into my glycogen stores a bit too much without having the carbs to replete them.

I do a quick 5 min incline-walk on the treadmill, then I hit the weights. I do 2-3 exersices pr session, each at 3x 12-9 (12, then 10, then 9- till it burns) reps, as I want to tone up, not bulk. I'm normally done within 25-30 min. Like you, I don't want to trigger cortisol-issues or adrenal fatigue, so I make sure I never over-do it. Once or twice a week myw arm-up will be a sprint-session on the treadmill or elliptical.

I also do leg-raises for my lower abs... I do 3 or 4 sets with 17-20 reps.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:24:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-performance-increases-using-ct.101/post-537>

I actually drink a can of pure coconut water straight after WOs. 320 ml, contains the good stuff from young coconut, and 13 grams of coconut sugar/carbs. The recovery with this has been amazing, I seriously hardly ever feel sore. I leave the gym with jelly-legs and jelly-arms, but 2 min after drinking this, I'm back to my normal self. Its works super-fast, is very Paleo and tasty. I love the Ayam-brand. Could be too high in sugar in the winter-months, but its so effective.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-15T23:39:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-performance-increases-using-ct.101/post-1083>

I feel like it is a little too soon to tell, but my pull ups have gotten easier, able to do more of them, and my deadlift, which has always been my "iffy" lift, never reliable, has been steadily going up. I am 40 yo female competitive powerlifter. Tomorrow I will go for maxes, in a conservative manner, I have a meet in 4 weeks that I don't want to screw up! After the meet I will experiment more with CT and my training, right now I am doing cold baths after training (and protein/fat heavy meal)

I love coconut water too Glam! I was wondering about the carbs. after a taxing session I do find myself going for one, about 1 x week. I train hard 3 x week, lifting only. No conditioning ATM, so I'm not concerned with adrenal/cortisol issues. sessions run about 75-90 min tops

I'm not lifting to "look good" I'm lifting to get strong and I think I look good as a by-product

My current stats @ 152# 5'5"

squat 220#

bench 135#

deadlift 285#

I was hoping to hit 305 on DL by year's end, also a 240 squat. My bench is so low, I don't even have aspirations, I'd be thrilled to have bodyweight bench. These kind of gains happen slowly...or at least, they used to happen slowly... We shall see!

### eva — 2012-03-20T16:00:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-performance-increases-using-ct.101/post-3233>

another powerlifter here. atleast used to be. i havent been competitive for a few years but i train almostthe same anyhow. i have also noted the biggest improvement on my pullups. i have increased the added poundage with double in my 5 x 5s

### KA24 — 2012-04-25T22:03:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-performance-increases-using-ct.101/post-18822>

I've seen subtle strength growth in pull-ups and handstand push-ups over just about a month of CT. Not sure it's directly correlative, but I can definitely feel more strength.

# Thread: share lunch box ideas for kids!?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### smithangels@comcast.net — 2012-03-15T05:07:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-518>

My girls are in K and 1st. My 1st grader gets made fun of, regarding her (my) lunch choices. Tuna is too "smelly" apparently.

I am having a hard time finding great ideas for LR (or at least paleo) type food (and snacks for mid-morning) for school.

I will post the few things I can think of in the morning - brain is not working too well tonight, it is late

Oh, and I use F.lux blue-blocking addon to my Firefox browser (free) to block blue light at night (if you are wondering what I am doing at the laptop this late at night, LOL).

Warm regards,

Barbie

### fabbecky — 2012-03-15T12:01:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-643>

I would love to get some more lunch box ideas myself

For my 4yr old who goes to an all day pre-school/headstart program that includes breakfast, lunch, snack I have started packing some of the following:

Coconut milk berry smoothie w/ egg (raw, pasteurized, to make sure he gets protein). He loves his breakfast milkshakes

grapes, baby carrots, cucumber slices, ranch dip (homemade with no extra junk) hot dogs, sausages, yogurt (this is a big one for him, he loves Greek yogurt and I pack one for him every day) cocoa covered almonds (again, homemade so I control ingredients). I am still having trouble getting it all eaten every day, but he does really love this stuff. I am waiting on pins and needles for the Paleo Parents Eat Like A Dinosaur to come out later this month to get more ideas for lunches and dinners and everything else. I am planning on read it with my monkey boy and have him start helping me more in the kitchen.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T14:58:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-703>

salami and cream cheese roll ups! salami, yesterday afternoon my kids clipped off the remainder of some carrots. I make lamb liver/ground beef burgers and the kids can't get enough of them! THey've had 2 servings of liver this week and dd asked why we didn't have mommy's burgers more often!!!

hard boiled eggs, sardines, oysters etc... my kids will pretty much eat anything we put infront of them... the favorite veggies are KAle, collards, broccoli greens and broccoli - 1/2 the time dh and I don't get any!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T16:25:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-762>

My 7 year old LOVES salads loaded with meat and hard boiled eggs and cucumbers. I have started making all of our dressing homemade. It's so easy, cheap, and much healthier!

I make my own yogurt with full fat milk and strain the whey off, so it's like greek yogurt. I add berries to it and my kids love it.

Wish I could come up with more snack and treat ideas!

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T16:54:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-794>

@Trisha, save the whey and use it to make your own pickled foods (asparagus, pickles, green beans) -- it's very easy!

My girls are 6 and 8, in K and 2nd grade, and I'm getting, "Mom, it's not COOL to bring your lunch." Aargh! My little daughter makes decent choices most of the time, but my older girl will eat chicken nuggets all day, every day if given the option.

Great ideas here, now working on them to mitigate the peer pressure!

### nyika — 2012-03-16T04:27:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-1263>

> **PaleoBarbie;519 wrote:** My girls are in K and 1st. My 1st grader gets made fun of, regarding her (my) lunch choices. Tuna is too "smelly" apparently.
>
>
>
> I am having a hard time finding great ideas for LR (or at least paleo) type food (and snacks for mid-morning) for school.

I don't give tuna for the kids (for their rank on the food chain), but mini omelette's are easy to fold up with whatever filling, I also love making egg cups/mini frittatas in a muffin tray, they are great and very easy to pack in lunch boxes. Can be made with anything really...veggies, meat, whatnot.

Mini omelettes?

Egg cups/vegetable/fish/meat muffins?

What about oven baked/or fried almond crusted chicken popcorns?

Grilled/almond crusted fish and carrot chips?

Avocado dip and sliced veggies?

Burgers?

Kebabs rolled in ham/letture ? Anything rolled in ham/lettuce?

Paleo nut balls made with coconut oil? (nut free schools might don't allow these)

Coconut manna for desert flavoured with chocolate/cinnamon/vanilla/berries?

I made Paleo bread the other day (we don't really eat bread, but if it makes life easier for kids when everyone has sandwiches around it might be the thing) of heaps of eggwhites and almond meal and inedible fibre (for the texture) I used few whole eggs iand few whites only. You can put banana into the batter with coconut cream (for banana bread) or chopped olives and herbs.

Lightly toasted and so easy to pack for lunch.

A lot of schools are nut free, so you might won't be able to make it with almond meal, but the other day I made banana bread with coconut flour and it turned out fine, though a bit too light in texture. SO I guess any banana bread recipe with no banana in it could be turned into a plain bread recipe and then you can make a sandwich?

This is something I didn't do for a while, but now I might do it once in a while.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-16T04:42:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-1271>

> **jeepifer;795 wrote:** @Trisha, save the whey and use it to make your own pickled foods (asparagus, pickles, green beans) -- it's very easy!
>
>
>
> My girls are 6 and 8, in K and 2nd grade, and I'm getting, "Mom, it's not COOL to bring your lunch." Aargh! My little daughter makes decent choices most of the time, but my older girl will eat chicken nuggets all day, every day if given the option.
>
>
>
> Great ideas here, now working on them to mitigate the peer pressure!

I've made pickles before, but never used whey. Does it enhance the pickles somehow?

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-03-16T05:02:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-1278>

> **Trisha;1272 wrote:** I've made pickles before, but never used whey. Does it enhance the pickles somehow?
>
>
> From the Weston A Price website (they word things so much better than I could!):
>
> Salt inhibits putrefying bacteria for several days until enough lactic acid is produced to preserve the vegetables for many months. The amount of salt can be reduced or even eliminated if whey is added to the pickling solution. Rich in lactic acid and lactic-acid-producing bacteria, whey acts as an inoculant, reducing the time needed for sufficient lactic acid to be produced to ensure preservation. Use of whey will result in consistently successful pickling; it is essential for pickling fruits. During the first few days of fermentation, the vegetables are kept at room temperature; afterwards, they must be placed in a cool, dark place for long-term preservation.

I typically strain the whey out of greek yogurt, and use it with my veggies. I think I'm going to pickle some asparagus and maybe some green beans this weekend, using this recipe: [http://www.healthhomehappy.com/2011/03/homemade-pickled-asparagus.html](http://http://www.healthhomehappy.com/2011/03/homemade-pickled-asparagus.html)

### fabbecky — 2012-03-16T15:31:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-1421>

> **Trisha;763 wrote:** My 7 year old LOVES salads loaded with meat and hard boiled eggs and cucumbers. I have started making all of our dressing homemade. It's so easy, cheap, and much healthier!
>
>
>
> I make my own yogurt with full fat milk and strain the whey off, so it's like greek yogurt. I add berries to it and my kids love it.
>
>
>
> Wish I could come up with more snack and treat ideas!

I love making homemade Greek yogurt! So very very good... I need to figure out how to make it with coconut milk now, because I need to cut out dairy.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-16T19:51:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-1621>

> **fabbecky;1423 wrote:** I love making homemade Greek yogurt! So very very good... I need to figure out how to make it with coconut milk now, because I need to cut out dairy.

If you find out, let me know! I'd like to do that too. But, I'd still have to make the dairy yogurt for my 7 year old because he doesn't seem to like anything coconut

### Mud Flinger — 2012-03-16T21:47:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-1662>

Don't forget the cool lunch boxes from lunchbots for kids that are tired of bringing home food. Sometimes the package can make the sale!

My son loves meat roll ups with cheese inside and fruit in his lunch (he still wont eat many veges). If your kids are begging for lunchables (luckily my son has no idea what these are), maybe add a few rice crackers as they have some baked ones out there that don't contain other bad stuff (if you occasionally allow rice). Sometimes it's about limiting the worse choices and allowing a bit of less than perfect (don't let perfect be the enemy of good).

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-18T15:27:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-2362>

I don't make a packed lunch for my children any more as they are at boarding school, but when I did make them, I would include some of the following: steamed asparagus spears wrapped in prosciutto or smoked salmon (I used to live near a place that smoked their own salmon, so I'd get trimmings cheaply and freeze them!), tuna mayo with home-made mayo and sliced spring onions/scallions, with slices of red pepper to dip into it, meatballs, mini burgers, Scotch eggs made with quail's eggs and rolled in sesame seeds instead of breadcrumbs, chicken kebabs, chicken in pimento mayonnaise (home-made mayo with pureed roast red peppers and roast garlic mixed in) in little gem lettuce boats, frittata, smoked salmon roulade (smoked salmon wrapped around home-made smoked salmon pate), home-made vegetable crisps/chips, angels on horseback (prunes wrapped in bacon then baked until the bacon is crispy), etc .... a thermos filled with home-made vegetable soup was also generally a winner!

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-19T19:38:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-2836>

First rule of a great lunch for kids is a BAB! If you really push the breakfast, the lunch becomes less important. Ditto with a well done dinner at home. If you have those two bookends, you have great insulation for that lunch you can't supervise so closely.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-20T17:45:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-3285>

I agree. . .we even were so abusive as to make a few dinners in a row he wasn't that thrilled with (some with new veggies or something) so he was super hungry in the a.m., and started loading him up on the BAB! I've noticed, although I pack the required 2 snacks, many days he doesn't touch them. Of course they are things he's not that thrilled with like coconut flakes with some dark chocolate.

My son is 5, almost 6 - and he makes his own scrambled eggs now. That's been so much fun.

My lunch tricks - I ordered a boatload of fun toothpicks with sparkles, monkeys, umbrellas etc on the end. I then make little kebobs of whatever meats are around, and serve it with "ranch dip", also in a little 2 ounce plastic cup. Presentation is everything I think.

Our ranch dip consists of Ranch Dip spices from Penzy's Spices mixed with either yogurt mixed with oil, or mixed right in the raw heavy cream.

The other thing is I can make a boatload of meatballs in advance. . meat + processed spinach and it's a green meatball.

I loved sushi from way back, and made a point of taking him as soon as he could eat - so he's old hat at raw fish. In time emergencies I pick up a sushi lunch and figure a little white rice now and then will have to be dealt with.

My biggest frustration is his school has "pizza friday" every other week, so ALLLLLL the kids get to eat Dominoes pizza. I have not had the heart to deny him that, so he's getting wheat once every couple of weeks. I'm really looking forward to this summer to try and break that one.

When "treats" are needed (rarely) we do the almond flour cookies and stuff. It's still got too much sugar, but I figure a little honey or maple syrup can be processed by a few hours outside.

Love your tips/tricks. I have been making ranch dressing with Paleo mayo, coconut milk, and dried herbs/spices (garlic, onion, parsley, sea salt, dash of cayenne pepper). My 2 yr old said about it last night "yummy, so good!"

For my 2yr old, he would eat "eggies" at every single meal if I let him. And yogurt, with almond butter. Or last night, he grabbed a stick of butter out of the fridge, unwrapped it, and started eating it like a candy bar. And cried when I took it away.

### MJ* — 2012-03-23T14:45:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-4919>

I agree with this, I have noticed in the last couple of days since upping the protein in the morning, that some things are coming home uneaten from lunch...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-23T15:43:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-4947>

bacon......1/2 pound. its a staple. Beef jerky home made......then left over meat in a glass container and heat it up.......its what my kids eat.

### MJ* — 2012-03-23T18:36:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-5041>

> **Jack Kruse;4979 wrote:** bacon......1/2 pound. its a staple. Beef jerky home made......then left over meat in a glass container and heat it up.......its what my kids eat.

We'll get there but it will be slow with my daughter, just a couple of years ago she'd chew her chicken (would not evan eat red meat), suck out the juice, store it in her cheeks and then spit it out 20 minutes later, our man child on the other hand loves meat, for him it should be much easier! I do see increased protein in the morning helping...

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-28T03:38:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-7483>

> **Jack Kruse;4979 wrote:** bacon......1/2 pound. its a staple. Beef jerky home made......then left over meat in a glass container and heat it up.......its what my kids eat.

Yes. Cook bacon for breakfast one day and send the left overs in the lunch the next day. I actually bought a bacon press today so those annoying curls go away!

Thanks for the reminder that I really need to demand a dehydrator for Mother's Day.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-28T03:41:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-7486>

> **MJ\*;5073 wrote:** We'll get there but it will be slow with my daughter, just a couple of years ago she'd chew her chicken (would not evan eat red meat), suck out the juice, store it in her cheeks and then spit it out 20 minutes later, our man child on the other hand loves meat, for him it should be much easier! I do see increased protein in the morning helping...

When my now 6 year old wasn't good about eating meat... I bought her tenderloin. Sounds expensive but it did help her get past the chewing issues.

The other thing when she was really small, I would cook the ground beef and then puree it in a food processor 4parts meat to 1 part well cooked rice. And then I rolled her little meatballs. It only lasted a few months... once they really like the meat, they will start eating it better on their own.

### Shelley — 2012-03-29T01:14:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-lunch-box-ideas-for-kids.104/post-7987>

Great ideas Cavemam! A suggestion you might like is that we make sushi with cauliflower as a rice substitute - I just chop it up small in the food processor (or you can grate by hand) and lightly steam it - add some vinegar and salt to make it taste like the real mccoy too!

For pizza - we use the cauliflower (or can sub out zucchini), cheese, egg recipe for the base. It also passes the kid-test well!

I wrote a blog post on school lunches which you might find helpful..

[http://shelleybelcourt.com/school-lunch-ideas/](http://http://shelleybelcourt.com/school-lunch-ideas/)

HTH

Shelley

# Thread: Start Your Optimal Journal Today
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/start-your-optimal-journal-today.105/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### admin — 2012-03-15T05:09:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/start-your-optimal-journal-today.105/post-522>

Journal your way to optimal!

How do you do it? It's easy!

\- Start a new post

\- Title it with your name. For example: My Optimal Journal - Dr. Jack Kruse

\- Keep a log day to day updating your progress

It's a neat way for you to look back on all you've accomplished and all

the hurdles you've leaped to get to where you are...optimal.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:14:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-528>

...continuing on from our big post on MDA, anyone who has graduated from the Leptin-Rx and moved onto the post-RX protocol, feel free to join us here.

We refer to our breakfast as HAB: Half Arsed Breakfast, as we eat less than when we did the Leptin-rx. LOL

Hoping to see some old faces in here as well as new ones.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-15T05:30:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-542>

Hello Glamorama!

I didn't know this forum was here until I read your comment about it over on MDA. Not sure if I am LS or not. I only need to lose about five pounds, but I have switched over to the HAB and am CT'ing as well. I need to correct some osteopenia in the lower spine. STill, it would be nice to lose that last five pounds that have been so difficult to get rid of. I like your new picture here , BTW.

I am 58 yo 5'4 and 136 lbs. With huge upper arms. Worried that if I lose much more fat on the ole arms that the hanging skin (and there is already a lot of it from losing weight) will turn into empty skin wings. Lifting weights is only going to do so much, I am afraid.

Susan M.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:38:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-546>

STill, it would be nice to lose that last five pounds that have been so difficult to get rid of. I like your new picture here , BTW.

> I am 58 yo 5'4 and 136 lbs. With huge upper arms. Worried that if I lose much more fat on the ole arms that the hanging skin (and there is already a lot of it from losing weight) will turn into empty skin wings. Lifting weights is only going to do so much, I am afraid.
>
>
>
> Susan M.

Are you supplementing with MSM (powder-form)? 10 grams a day (in 2 separate doses, every 12 hours) will do amazing things for your skin, I saw my loose tummy-flab gone in 2 weeks when I started MSM 3-4 months ago! Take it along with Vit C as it helps absorbtion.

The last 5 are hard to lose. I'm down to my last 10 and finding it stubborn, but going by my clothes I've gooten a lot smaller lately, so I think I'm stacking on muscle. Give it time, focus on eating clean, walk daily, and do CT daily as much as possible. Maybe do measures and photos now, then you can come back and compare in 12 weeks? It often feels like we don't see results, but a photo and measurements don't lie!

### Susan M. — 2012-03-15T05:45:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-555>

Glamorama,

How did you do your last post with my post showing within yours. Am I supposed to use the "Reply with Quote" button? I have not figured out how to do that yet. I haven't a clue of how to really using a forum. Tying to keep up technically while aging is tough on me. Ugh! But, I can't let myself get left behind in that area either.

I used to use MSM for bursitis years ago, but it did not agree with my digestive system. I might give it a whirl again, but in a much smaller dose. Thanks for the tip. How much MSM do you take? Also, how much vitamin C ?

Thanks.

Susan M.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-15T05:47:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-558>

Sorry about that. I see you already stated how much MSM you take. I was getting just a little too focused!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:49:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-562>

"Reply with Quote" is the one you want.

I worked my way from 1 gram of MSM twice daily, till 5 grams twice daily over about a month. I was fine on it for nearly 4 months, but lately I connected it to be part of the reason my sleep has gone really bad lately. It makes me jittery at night and wakes me up... Odd.

Anyway, I'm having a break from it for now, but I seem to be unaffected if I take it 2 days on, then 2 days off. I take 2000 mg Vit C when I'm well, but much higher doses if I feel a cold brewing.

Lots of coconut oil is also meant to be great for your skin, both topically and orally.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-15T06:04:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-577>

Thanks for the information on the MSM and the vitamin C. I am taking most of the recommended supplements, except for the PQQ, which I save for my husband, Steve. He has lost 30 lbs. on the reset since Feb 1st and I can tell you he is very motivated -- just not ready to jump in a cold tub just yet. He needs to lose about 70 more pounds before he can have knee surgery via the VA. I know he will get there. This is probably the only way he can do it, too, since he cannot exercise. He has had so many darned surgeries that I just think of him as my awesome, good-looking, wonderful Frankenstein.

I love coconut oil. I use the expeller expressed for cooking, put use the full-flavored for making my chocolate bombs. That would be just good cooking cocoa and coconut oil, wrapped in waxed paper and frozen. No sweetener in these babies. Man, has my tastebuds evolved. Ummm!

Well, time for this old girl to go read in a low light before heading off to bed. Sleep well. Thanks again.

Susan M.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T08:06:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-621>

> **KiwiLauren;619 wrote:** Thanks. So do you think MSM (with Vit C) is a good basic supplement for any of us? I've never used it. But you think it's what contributed to your sleeplessness, too? I get overwhelmed with the supplement thing sometimes!

MSM worked extremely quickly for the purpose I used it for (lose skin). Yes, it did affect my sleep, but only after being at a certain level in my blood for a while. I was on it for a good 6 weeks before I started getting jittery and experience insomnia. Quitting MSM nad introducing CT ensures I sleep soundly most nights, but I think I have a lot of hormonal stuff going on (period 3 days late... not pregnant), and that affects my sleep too.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-15T23:54:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-1095>

Good afternoon!

I have a skylight in the bathroom, and the daytime light is more soothing for me than the artificial light I rely on in the evenings. ### Susan M. — 2012-03-17T01:57:45+0200

### L8F — 2012-03-17T02:28:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-1797>

PaleoFx was cool, and amazing learning. JK was the big attraction. What I got from it was some of the docs are interested, but skeptical of his stuff, but they don't really know that much about it, and certainly haven't tried any of it out. That being said, JK got mobbed at all times with eager people wanting to learn more. That was cool.

Lots of differing opinions there, but it was civil. Cool doc here in TX is a guy named Lane Sebring who has been a paleo doc for TWELVE years. Wow. He does a lot of BHRT, and knows his stuff. I am going to check out his website. If anyone is in TX and needs a doc, he and several others practice in Austin general area, and I wish I lived here!!!

What else, Erwan LeCorre from MovNat is possibly the most perfect male specimen I have ever seen (other than my husband of course!) His stuff is really cool. I need to decompress and then will report more factual stuff that I took away.

Lots of fit attractive folks in a big room together...kinda different from a medical conference for sure!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-17T10:28:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-1893>

Didn't have time for full submerge today, but did it for 3 consecutive days. So today I only did spot-CT. Normally the skin on my tummy/lower back/waist stays insanely frozen and old for a whole hour after CT, the cold for another hour. Today, funnily enough, my skin didn't even feel cool when I took off the ice wraps. It was like my body was heating itself during the entire time I was CTing in order to keep warm??? So weird,but brand new. Maybe I'm finally cold adapted?

Craving HWC today... So I went and got it for dessert after dinner. Not sure why I crave fat/dairy today, maybe because I worked really hard in the gym this morning (squat/ dead lifts/ hamstring curls). Lunch was tiny... I think my body was craving more calories.

One thing I've noticed I now no longer have die off symptoms when I take iodine

### Glamorama — 2012-03-18T02:20:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-2192>

My TOM arrived today, 5 days late. I'm actually a bit crampy, haven't had that since before I started the reset in July. My cycles have gotten longer and longer ever since Dec!

Slept like a log last night. Def think I've hit cold adapted status. Hubby couldn't sleep because he said the bedroom was so cold. He ended up under 2 covers, one being our thick winter-quilt! I was toasty warm under just a thin summer-quilt! Both bedroom-windows were open, and I think it was 13 degrees last night.

I think my hormonal craziness is settling down, I'm feel good, but still keeping a lot of water weight that hasn't budged since I started CT a month ago.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-18T10:54:37+0200

News of late is that I find myself craving the cold baths and spot-CT. I used to always dread it a bit, but last 2 days I have really craved it and felt fantastic while doing it. Took just short of 4 weeks to get to this point. I suspect it would have happens in 2 weeks or less if I submerged daily instead of just spot-CT.

So friggin tired and fatigued today, even though night-sleep has been deep and long. Detox? TOM? Hormones? Bit of everything?

### Glamorama — 2012-03-19T00:29:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-2481>

> **PaleoCowgirl;2349 wrote:** The CT got very tough for me around week 2-3, but now it's like I NEED the cold. It is a stress-reliever, and it soothes my achey muscles. I've been so hot lately, too, that it's just such a nice relief.

I'm slowly slowly starting to feel right, like a normal person again. The excess estrogen must be cleared, because my boobs are teeny tiny now (!!!!), my mood is nice and happy, I'm not snappy anymore and I feel like having sex! I'm finally tolerating the cold really well too.

No changes on scales, but I can tell I've lost a lot of weight off the breasts and across upper tummy. Seeing naturopath tomorrow, will get a full measure then, of lean mass and body fat. Should be interesting!

My skin constantly feels sticky and hot, sticky as if I'm producing too much sebum, Been happening ever since I started CT!

### L8F — 2012-03-19T17:44:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-2766>

Shijin, that is very very cool on the bikini!! Not so much on the TOTM stuff. Evil cramps. Do you chart? ### L8F — 2012-03-19T17:53:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-2774>

Colleen, I have no help on the pain in the side thing for now. Does it hurt when you press? Any swelling visible under the skin? Can you palpate any structures or edges under the skin? Does it get better with the cold?

Ok, thoughts needed on this topic: GI TMI ahead, skip if squeamish-

Before leaving Austin I ate a HUGE ribeye grilled, with raw kale salad and a couple of pieces of beets. First starch in a long while. I went to bed shortly after (bad I know) and felt stomach pain in the night. Woke up early AM, ate some bacon that wasn't that great tasting to me, and then black coffee. Got on a plane. Felt weird, but not hungry or weak. Did not eat or drink much during travel, felt fine. Then on second leg of plane trip, during descent I rapidly felt VERY ill, and knew I was going to throw up. I have not thrown up in many years, and am not pregnant. It came on suddenly, and I could not stop it. I think it was undigested meat and some bacon. Gross. Felt ok after, but had a hard time finding anything other than chicken broth and an avocado yesterday that I wanted to eat. Today I am fine. Any thoughts on this? I have NEVER had GI issues that I could _FEEL_, but it seems that I now am having them. I feel odd after eating and am not sure if it is too much or too little Betaine. Also, do I need a digestive enzyme? Will also check my pancreatic enzyme tests just to make sure things are normal. Mamma G had mentioned non-digesting and I think that is a good thought, and is bearing out. Why?? What to do? Don't want to go to MD who will not have any clue either. New functional MD visit in early April...Thank YOU!!!

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-19T17:53:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-2775>

> **L8F;2774 wrote:** Shijin, that is very very cool on the bikini!! Not so much on the TOTM stuff. Evil cramps. Do you chart? I just started a new thread for some charting stuff cause I am really interested in how all this stuff pulls together...Cramps, E to P ratios, charts, symptoms. I think the more data we gather it could be a really great way to look at an diagnose some subtler hormone issues via chart, sx and labs.
>
>
>
> When I was in Austin, KerryGold was ON SALE for 4 59, at WFM!! Then, at the WFM here in CT, it was 2 something. Weird regional variances. BTW the WFM in Austin is worth moving there for. They have a smoked meat station, and you can order ahead and have them smoke whatever you like, however you like. My cousin ordered some triple smoked bacon for infusing vodka!! They also have bulk sausage area where it is not in casings, as well as pastured poultry. Jealousy. Sadly it was so crowded cause of the South By Southwest festival that I couldn't wander around as much as I would have liked. I live for good grocery stores.

YEs I've charted for over 6yrs... I've got a total history of my charts! its funny to go back and look at them... Cramps are gone now but I think that has to do w/CT this morning... One things for sure I was definitely more sensitive to CT this morning! I'll stop by the charting thread!

WFM set up an East Coast Flag-ship store in FFX county - its something like 7000sqft!. Smoked meats, oyster bar, cheese and wine bar, huge butcher section!, then the deli and bakery section (Yuck) not to mention the veggie section... Got to go to the local organic market - to pick up diaper washing stuff so I'll probably pick up the kerry gold from there... rather than make 2 separate trips to separate stores!

>
> !

It's sore to press on it. You can sort of feel something hard under there. I thought it might be a tightened muscle or something but I couldn't tell for sure. I've had it since mid January. It was some better in Hawaii then got way worse on the way home and has been bad all week. It's never totally left since it started. It's actually on my side, not the front, not my back. It's just about exactly halfway between my waist and where my leg meets my torso. Very strange spot.

It's actually a little better today after sitting in the cold pool yesterday. Haven't been out there today yet.

### L8F — 2012-03-19T20:52:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-2881>

> It's actually a little better today after sitting in the cold pool yesterday. Haven't been out there today yet.

Hmm, have you tried massage for it? Is it nodular with clear edges? Can you wiggle it around, or is it fixed to the tissue underneath? Does it cause pain to breath in and out as the area stretches? I might head to my regular doc to look at it to get thoughts. Often they see certain things regularly and know exactly what it feels like on exam, and with the clinical history you give (a regular, good doc is often a great thing!!). They can rule out anything of concern and hopefully give you a diagnosis which you can treat thru them, or on your own with alternate methods...depending on what they feel it is. It sounds like it is sticking around and causing discomfort.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-19T21:00:17+0200

I'm heading home from AZ in about 10 days so I will go see my doc. It's very puzzling for sure. It does sort of feel nodular now that you mention it. It's hard to know about the breathing pain today because it is some better. The next time it worsens, I'll check that. Thanks, Lucy!

Maybe CT will fix it before I even get home. Now that I'm back to Kara's cold pool, I can do better with that. There wasn't even a tub in Hawaii, only a shower. The water temp in the pool there was around 60 and I swam for 1/2 hour in it every day but I know it really wasn't cold enough.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T02:35:01+0200

I haven't lost any on the scales since I last went, but I've had some major changes in strength, sleep, hormonal balance and body comp lately, so I'm very keen to find out.

Feeling really good today! Maybe something is shifting and finally "working". Thats how it feels to me, anyway. I'm in a really good mood, feeling content, happy and incredibly, not hungry! (6 hours since HAB... and I went to the gym this morning).

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T06:59:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3157>

A bit disappointed. BF percentage was still 29, same as last month. Good news is that I've gained 600 grams of muscle, which has beena dded to my weight, which explains why I'm now heavier. No idea why I haven't burnt any fat (with the amount of detox and excess estrogen I've gone through I must have burnt some fat off through CT), but I'm tipping a closer look at my thyorid-panel might explain the stall. Will do testing next week.

ETA: Liver and gall-bladder are now looking awesome according to naturopath, so I was right when is aid that something has shifted- my body feels cleaner and elss "congested"/toxic.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T16:37:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3245>

I'm still not sure if I'm LS or not.

**Positive Signs

Cravings: gone unless I have something sweet due to a brain-fart, then they're still manageable

Hunger: no desire to snack AT ALL. No desire to eat lunch 99% of the time, and easily ignorable the 1%

Sleep: solid again, after total chaos at the beginning of the LR

Skin: SUPER soft (with the occasional weird slightly bumpier day) after a lifetime of dry, dry bumpy sandpapery skin.

**Inconclusive Signs**

Heat: definitely warmer at night, but not kicking covers off except for once in a blue moon

Fat loss: still losing about 1-2lb/month (yeah, I'm gonna win a prize for rapid weight loss for sure)

Sweating: Not much opportunity to sweat, but I am sweating profusely every week during Mass (standing in warm balcony singing the whole hour) and did not used to. Not stinking, just sweating. In fact, stinking seems to be pretty much eliminated altogether!

Negative Signs**

Waking: still feel like death, or death warmed over

Energy: low, but slowly moving upward

Muscle recovery: climbed cliffs, hiked, & rode horses last Friday, and could hardly walk Sat & Sun. Not so good.

I've needed to reduce BAB from 65g to 50g lately in order to maintain an appetite for dinner. Still feel like eating the full 50g + broth + ferment + carbs every morning.

Shall we vote?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-20T21:29:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3451>

I had pretty similar signs/symptoms (with even better muscle recovery and I'd started HIIT/LHT) but as soon as I brought my BAB down I got really hungry for lunch. So for weeks I ended up eating a smaller BAB, then lunch, then the same sized dinner. I felt confused as to whether I'd moved too early or if this was OK or if I should make dinner much bigger, etc. I'm still in that confusion, actually. But from what you're posting, it may be worth a try to go to a HAB and see what happens. (I still can't quite figure out what is the better option: play it 'safe' and stay with the Reset or move to the Post Leptin living if you're not ready.)

I was LS everywhere except liver for nearly 6 months! So some areas are slower at recovering depending on what damage we're trying to repair.

When I had all signs of LS I was 22 pounds more than I am today. And still have 10 to lose.

Gretchen, where are the bikini-pics?? I wanna see!

Did bikini-shots yesterday to compare from the ones I did in early Jan. I've only lost a pound in that time, but from the photos I could see the following: thighs/hips much smaller, boobs very much smaller, arms same, shoulders same. Tummy looks the same too but more tucked in at the waist.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-21T00:21:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3575>

> **meyoolia;3585 wrote:** Glamorama, did you stay on the LR protocol for 6 months until your liver was resensitized? How did you know you were "there"? I've been on it 3 months and I'm improved but still not sure (see my post earlier today)...  ... though after nearly 2 weeks of CT, for the first time, I'm kinda looking forward to the cool of a nice frigid tub, too... yeeshk, no wonder my family's convinced I'm nuts, lol

I stayed on protocol for 7 months although I had all signs of LS by week 6. I didn't change protocol because I got comfortable on the reset. When my weight loss stalled I knew it was time to post-Rx. The liver? I learnt through experience with high-fructose fruit like banana and alcohol. Up until Dec I just didn't handle it well, and I would turn from fat burner to carb burner and get cravings and hunger every 3 hours, all due to half a banana daily! That showed me my liver wasn't there yet. So icut out the quick carbs, and stuck to my fat and protein instead. After Christmas something shifted but the liver was still slow at metabolizing.

In the last week I can actually feel that my liver is closer to optimal than it's ever been in the past. I feel different, in a good way.

### L8F — 2012-03-21T01:32:04+0200

Very good news, glad to hear it. You have had such a tough time with this liver stuff!!

On another note: After I had food poisoning or something like it, I ate a handful of Mike N Ikes candy almost like I was being propelled. Then I wanted more and ate a snickers bar. That was two days ago. Now today my B12 lozenge tasted REALLY good to me. Wanted more more, fruit etc. Craving. Ugh. I caved and ate a whole chocolate almond bar, 55% dark. Also had tomato broth with meatballs today, first nightshade in many months on purpose. I am sliding. (No CT baths in a few days, just spot treatment)

I need to get back on the regular keto horse. So annoying. Gonna do another set of fasting labs tomorrow AM to see where I am in terms of E & P, and sort out what to do. Waiting for my new functional doc appt on April 5th. Eating chocolate as I type. Not good. Maybe all this hot weather is getting to me. ITS TOO EARLY FOR SUMMER!!!!

I've been having troubles with this, too. The other night, I ate like 2 cups of coconut manna! And, now I've been "savoring my b12 lozenge," too. It's like I need sweet all of a sudden. I can't figure out what triggered it, but I'm definitely holding my ground now and cutting everything sweet. Right now, even cinnamon gets me to craving. Terrible...

### L8F — 2012-03-21T01:55:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3638>

Isn't this terrible!!?? Glad I am not alone. Brave ones who didn't give in. I think it was my vomiting episode that made me so ill that I couldn't eat much, then just ate what I thought didn't sound disgusting. Then all hell broke loose. DONT DO IT if you are craving. It gets worse! now feel sick, and wish I didn't eat what I did. Is it our primal selves sensing warmer climates?? Actually, I guess it is not warm everywhere.

I just filled in a lab sheet for myself, all with legit coding for true things I have going on... I used the diagnoses of: Hashi's, Anemia, Recurrent Pregnancy Loss, Fatigue. I think it would be hard to argue that given I am only getting hormones, Thyroid Panel, HS CRP, Vitamin D and Ferritin/irons. Hope they pay cause I am going for other $$ tests also that you all know about when I see my new non-covered doc.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-21T02:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3645>

> **KiwiLauren;3652 wrote:** I have had such a massive return of cravings since doing CT. I have not caved in to them (except to eat some 85% dark chocolate which only made the cravings stronger) but I am back to thinking about carbs & sugar all day (where I have not had this AT ALL for months... it's only since starting CT.) I'm doing baths one day and ice packs the next, alternating all week. I am not losing weight, I am not cold adapting and I'm craving carbs. Is factor X insanity?

I think I know what's going on, as it happens to me too. CT makes you burn fat. Body loses fat, body panicks, body asks for food; read: body ensures carb cravings to refuel. I had the exact same thing! Best advice is to do CT after a big fat/protein-rich meal, and load up on good fat like CO with your next meal. That helped. I've even found myself eating slices of pure butter after dinner because I still crave more! CT really drives appetite.

It has taken me exactly 4 weeks to be cold adapted. I know that I am for sure now, no doubt about it. You'll know when it happens. 4 weeks is twice as long as the 2 weeks Doc said, but I put it down to be mostly spot-CTing. I still have annoying detox symthomps but I'm cold adapted.

Thanks, Glam. I hate being hungry and having cravings all the time. Unfortunately, I feel FAR from being cold adapted. I've been at this for 4 weeks too but I'm getting less cold tolerant not more. Granted, temps are falling here in Christchurch so maybe I'm just staying the same. I've had to add back in lunch. I'm going to go eat more butter now  I seriously hope CT doesn't make me gain weight and get cranky all at the same time.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-21T02:39:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3683>

> **PaleoCowgirl;3696 wrote:** I'm definitely cold adapted, too. I leave the windows open, and it's 62 in the house. For the first time, I'm HOT and the DH is complaining about how cold it is. Dr. K says if you're hungry, something is wrong. Glamorama, do you still get hungry now that you're cold adapted, or has it receded? I find even though I'm able to tolerate cold, I'm still super hungry and wanting to eat all the time.

I tend to believe Ray Cronin (sp?) more on this one... I know Doc says telomeres are either really long or really short if CT makes you hungry, but like Ray said, CT burns fat, so naturally it evokes hunger in the body. While CTing you don't feel hungry, but I find I feel hungry within an hour or two after a session. That's why I prefer to do it at night, so I'm asleep when the hunger kicks in. And hopefully, that way, I'll burn more WAT! You can only really apply n=1 here, but Doc encourages us to think for ourselves. To me it's looming obvious that anything that burns fat and increase muscle at the rate CT does, will increase hunger.

Personally I have no idea how I'm ever going to be able to IF. I simply have no interest in skipping my meals.

I feel a bit lost. I'm not sure if I need to IF in order to drop the last few pounds, and break through this stall, on the other hand I think the best way is to do some testing and see where things aren't working. My bet is on hormonal issues. I can't for the life of me understand why my body thinks 29 percent BF is acceptable as a set point. Happens every time I lose weight. It seems impossible for me to get lean. I'm determined to change that. Look at any Indian woman in her 40s and older, and you can see the sort of epigenetic I'm fighting here.

### L8F — 2012-03-21T02:55:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3698>

I think for you Ivy it is def. a hormonal thing. Also wouldn't put too much stock in those body fat percents. I feel like you are getting leaner, every few days/week you note small gains in strength and leaning out. Stick with that. From the look of you you don't look exactly 29 percent. If you don't feel like IFng, don't. It might lead you to caving in on a craving. I think you have _**very**_ **recently** gotten over a huge hump in hormones/liver issues. Give your body time to adjust to the new good state, and let it do its thing. It hasn't failed you yet. (I swear, the middle aged Armenian ladies scare the pants off me too. I am NOT ready to have a bad dye job, and flesh colored nylons, and puffy feet) You are in a different stratosphere, and will not end up stuck in middle aged Indian lady land. Plus you have the Norweigan winters in your epigenetics too.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-21T05:11:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3780>

> **KiwiLauren;3802 wrote:** Can you explain this a bit please?

if you take hcl and don\`t get a burning feeling then you need it. hcl is taken when you have low stomach acid levels. basic protocol is to start with 1 per meal and then keep adding one more hcl capsule each time you eat until you feel the burn and then back off by 1.

for me the number is 7 before I feel a burn

the ones I use are hcl and digestive enzymes combined, I tried the Thorne ones and did not have as good a reaction as I have had with these

### L8F — 2012-03-21T15:53:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-3873>

Ok, so brekkie is now a few thick cut slices of Wellshire Farms Black Forest Bacon (has sugar, tiny amount) and then sometimes a half avocado. Then I am full. I am happy with this. I still eat lunch, and reading the first part of CT 7 I am happy to think my gut is doing its thing getting me full after being quite hungry when I wake. Go Post LRx! After brief perusal of beginning I added on IL-6 to the labs I had drawn this AM, not sure if they have the right tube and blood amount to add it on. On another note, my veins were super easy to get this AM, and pumped it out like an artery. Was happy about this small win too. Made the whole thing less awful, and no huge mark on my arm, and no need to go to the pipes in my hands.

Really liking CT 7 so far, reads smoothly. Wish I had had it before...but I guess spoon feeding is not the M.O. of JK and he tells us why.

Heading to Boston area today to see the parents who have been 100% converted to gluten free Primal (some dairy still). Will order them up a giant slew of labs, and get cracking. Their doctor be damned. I used to believe in prof. courtesy of discussing things with a doc when I order labs on their patients, but once she circled their HA1Cs of 5.9 (matching) and said "pre-diabetic, not to worry for now, it is fine" I am dismissing her as a viable option for their health. No dietary advice, no tips, nothing. Even if it were the low fat ADA dogma, I would have been more satisfied.

I am also going on a huge supplement binge at the local market for after they have labs drawn. It should be fun.

Ranting, and feeling good this AM!

Bad idea  Anyway, getting back on track and trying to learn from the mistake and not beat myself up for it.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-21T19:24:18+0200

From CT-7:

_

Once leptin enters and binds to its receptors, it effects the lateral hypothalamic tracts to immediately send a second messenger signal to the thyroid to signal it to up-regulate thyroid function and efficiency. This specifically is how we can raise our basal metabolic rate when we are leptin sensitive. These coupled events, matched with leptinÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s actions peripherally in muscles, occur at the UCP3 sitesÃ¢â‚¬Â¦Ã¢â‚¬Â¦to burn fat as we sleep at a higher basal metabolic rate. This means electron chain transport does not make ATP as usual. When leptin allows this uncoupling to occur we make heat and not energy from normal metabolism. This means we will burn off our excess calories as pure heat. This is one reason why calories in and calories out argument makes no biologic sense once you understand how leptin works. Humans are built to burn fat at night as we sleep to loose excess weight we donÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t need._

This does not seem to happen with me anymore? I used to wake up having lost weight, but no i seem to jsut wake up and remain the same? I guess if thyorid is on the slower side, this is to be expected?

### L8F — 2012-03-22T13:36:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-4300>

Orange glasses block blue (artificial light) from telling your brain sun, time to wake up. It helps with circadian mismatch of being up late reading a bright screen. Best to dim lights at sundown, and do everything you can to have the dark time before sleep. Thus people wear orange glasses as the cheapest way to block the blue light. Not safe to use for driving or other cause you cannot see properly. Alternate would be to not watch a screen and use natural light like fireplace or candles.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-23T02:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-4766>

I've been a lazy butt about avoiding dairy lately. Twice in the last week I've given in to a craving for HWC with cinnamon stirred in (my idea of an ice cream). I don't know what it is, but I'm eating a lot more fat and totally loving it. Doc told me on FB that I should wait it through as I'm heading into actual winter.

In Melbourne today it is 13 degrees Celsius with cold gusts of wind. I went out shopping in a tee and shorts... couldn't even feel the chill. Last night was the same, all windows open, me naked under a thin cover, husband under a thick woollen quilt, freezing his butt off. I was in fact too warm, so I woke at 3 am and stayed up till 5, sweating. (Note to self: I was probably burning off that tub of HWC... as of CT-7). This morning he called me from work to remind me to turn the heater on... I told him I was warm and cosy and that the kids were dressed warmly. He is really starting to look like a whuzz compared to me, all cold-adapted and strong! LOL

Glam and all... remember discussing how CT was driving our hunger/carb cravings up.... well I see (now that the blog comments are fixed) that Dr Kruse answered my question about this. It is NOT because of more calories/energy being used. It's about estrogen. Go figure. See below.

Lauren says: Dr K, a bunch of us on the forum (the old MDA thread now here) have experienced a huge rise in our carb/sugar cravings since starting CT (and we had none for months prior). Is this normal? Any suggestions? Thanks.

Jack Says:

March 20th, 2012 at 7:40 pm

@lauren This is because of the estrogen released from fat. It will pass. You can shut it down by raising you protein loads. CT-7 gets into why.

I am out of DIM and not sure how long I should be taking it. I have been on it for many months.

My Dr. says that the tenderness in the breasts right before your period is do to the progesterone dropping down.... I think it must be so since I haven't had that at all now with the P I have started however I now have a 3 lb weight gain in 5 days.....this better go away after TOM!!!!

### L8F — 2012-03-24T00:58:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-5210>

I guess my whole conundrum with the E/P ratio is numbers for Estrogen. I am having a hard time nailing down good ranges. I know from basic basic reading that women vary in what feels right to their bodies, and what is high for one woman feels low for another. That being said, what is _TOO HIGH, and what is TOO LOW_??? Anyone have some numbers for me? Lots of my resources don't indicate when in the cycle they are taking numbers (I know it is obvious looking at a graph that the peaks are at specific points, likely different for every woman.) I am really torn wondering if my specific symptoms are from low Estrogen, high estrogen, or combo with low progesterone. Or is it just adrenal-->thyroid intermixed with messed up other hormones. UGH, feel like I know less now than at the start. Or is it the 50 mg of P that I took last night messing with my head and making me foggy?

Also, this functional med guy at PaleoFx, Kalisch I think was his name, was mentioning a DAILY Estrogen saliva monitoring system for the whole month to really get a handle on what is going on. Anyone ever done this? If I had unlimited funds and a little more set schedule, I might go for this.

### vkiernan — 2012-03-24T01:14:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-5214>

> **natural\_girl;4925 wrote:** I have noticed this as well. I don't do the spot CT but in but with blocks of ice....warm up way quick especially my stomach...still 3lbs of weight gain in 5 days though.....hopefully it is TOM and not the progesterone cream..

Per Dr. Reiss -

INCREASED AGITATION, as the result of a higher-than-preferred cortisol level. This happens when progesterone blocks an enzyme called 11-beta-hydroxysteriod, which is meant to convert cortisol to cortisone, the inactive form of cortisol. When the work of 11-beta-hydroxysteriod is blocked, your level of cortisol rises and you experience agitation rather than usual calming effect associated with progesterone.

WATER RETENTION, which can occur when the rise of cortisol over-comes the diurectic effect of progesterone. Progesterone is a strong antagonist of the adrenal hormone aldosterone, which causes water retention. Because progesterone battles against aldosterone, progesterone also works a a natural diuretic. However, sometimes when the significant increase of cortisol within the cell described above occurs, women nonetheless experience water retention, breast pain, and water weight gain. A physician experienced with bioidentical hormones will recongnize this effect and recommend steps for resolution. (So much for any resolution advice). Perhaps the CT baths are raising your cortisol and messing with your hormones to cause this craziness.

Glam, you have had some of these too and were able to reset. But, you are also 20 years younger than me. So do you all think lifting weights could actually help me make better progress. I am not sure with adrenal fatigue how much exercise is ok. Thanks for any input.

I think some exercise can be beneficial for the adrenal provided it is not to strenuous. I am really surprised the reset and CT have not fixed your adrenals, they have done wonders for mine! Did you have a stool test for candida?

### vkiernan — 2012-03-25T00:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-5580>

> **natural\_girl;5469 wrote:** I take Maca daily for adrenals and I think it helps a lot. But the CT & reset have helped tremendously with my circadian rhythms which I believe has helped the adrenals. Honestly, I believe a lot of it is just mind over matter too. I really do. I have a lot of personal & business things going on that I should probably be really stressed about somehow or another I am not. I just know that it will all work out the way it is supposed to on it's own time frame. I just feel like I am the creator of my own destiny....

My adrenals are tanked. My rhythm is below the norm on the first 3 and at the bottom at midnight. My dhea is a 1 and the range 3-10. This is adrenal fatigue, the last stage according to my cortisol results. I believe that is why I am struggling so bad. Surgery does a number on your cortisol and rt3 and I'm sure the bad poison ivy event made it worse. I had it all over my legs and arms and it lasted 2 months. If you have had it you can relate, otherwise you probably don't know how bad it is. I also had many things happen the previous fall like infected bee sting which swelled my arm up, water skiing injury, you know - just a lot of things that wouldn't normally happen. I am not talking mental stress so much and injury stress. Who knows how this really impacts one adrenals but I can't believe it helped in any fashion; but I just know I was in rough physical shape for quite some time.

My goal is to strengthen the adrenals to bring my cortisol up so I have something to run on, keep reducing my estrogen and de-tox my liver, increase my progesterone (maybe she will do it this week) and after all of this hopefully my thyroid does what it needs to do without the help of Armour. If I can do the above, get rid of my Candida, fully reset myself and become cold-adapted - it will be a wonderful year. I am going to ask her what else might be wrong with me since I had no idea all of this other stuff was even an issue. Perhaps she will do more testing just to make sure. I want to fix whatever is wrong. Perhaps the CT baths will be my path to optimal!

### vkiernan — 2012-03-25T22:37:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-5986>

> **natural\_girl;5886 wrote:** You know you probably had all the reactions you did because your adrenals weren't good to begin with....have you read Dr. Wilson's "Adrenal Fatigue: The 21st Century Syndrome"? It is a very good book and I ended up purchasing some of his supplements for awhile, they were pricey but I believe they really did help along with Dr.K's program too. With adrenal fatigue it is so important to change your thinking or you will never get past it.

Yes, I did read it (a compounding pharmacist borrowed it to me) and they have been fatigued since way before 2008. Quite possibly since 2004. My life "was" hell for the most part so I know where it came from. I guess I didn't elaborate on the fact that they have been tanked and the surgery etc. drained them even further (I know, it's all in the details). One small example of af, we were on a hawaiian cruse and I fell asleep at 5 pm in the front row of the luau (sp). Just zonked right out. That same year at x-mas at dh's mom's, I fell asleep sitting on the sofa at 1 in the afternoon. Just thought I was tired from the day before. I was diagnosed in early 2009 by a crazy nutritionist. She put me on this extremely low carb, high protein and low fat diet. I lost weight but I'm sure I didn't stick with it long enough. It was all about keeping blood sugar down which I understand is important but too extreme for sure. If people think the leptin reset is extreme, omg, this was crazy..... I knew I had adrenal fatigue but really didn't pay much attention to it back then. Did the diet for a few months and went on with life.

Over time, my pms got really bad like 2 weeks worth for sure out of every month. I could not pull myself out of the depressed state. Those were really dark days for me. My dh would tick me off (my own fault I'm sure) and I would be mad for 3 days min. Finally one day after years of this getting worse, I woke up and said what the hell is wrong with me. This isn't normal. I sure wish the dh would have told me I was getting so bad. I lived it so I couldn't see it and it was right in front of me. I have not had energy for as long as I can remember about the last 30 years so that wasn't an issue as I thought is was normal. But, thank god for p90x, Mark Sisson and now Jack Kruse I will have a life here at some point. It was a p90x forum that turned me on to primal eating hense Mark and Mark's forum who turned up JK. I will be forever grateful for JK. Even my hubby who doesn't do this stuff does believe in it and has seen the changes. He does not argue ever about anything I try that JK offers up.

I finally feel hope. I love my new dh as of almost 3 years. He is sooo good to me and I hope I can someday return the favor and be a really great happy supportive wife.

Dr. K mentioned only doing the CT and not hc, but I didn't realize that Isocort is a form of cortisol so now I'm not sure I am doing the right thing by taking it. My doc told me to take it so I feel I need to follow her advice. She has only helped me so far and I am feeling better. Although today I am wiped. I went shopping with my sister yesterday and I think it took it out of me. So, what does one do?

I have looked at the supplements. You did them while on the reset? Perhaps I should reconsider them.

You know it is not my thinking except during pms cuz I am so dark. I have a stressful life but I love my life now for the most part. I would like to ditch my 2nd job but that is a tie to my dh's business and gives us the cash to have toys and play with the family. I think once I heal my adrenals I will be good to go as long as I don't overdue it physical or emotionally as I am sooo much better than I have been in years and that was just by eating primally and the reset.

Thanks NG for all of your help over the course of this last year. It is without a doubt the thing that keeps me going. Knowing there people who take the time to respond and offer advice whatever it may be.

As a side note: We have no water today. Pump must have burned out so we have to wait until tomorrow for the well guys to come do their thing cuz you know they tack on another $150 for the service call not to mention the extra hourly rate. I will survive but it is crazy how much you take it for granted.

Sorry for the novel.

Let us know how you go! I've increased rapidly across all exersices, but it has slowed down now, in my 5th week of LHT. tha was expected though. I still have very good progress, and can finally do both a chin-up and pull-up unassisted, thats awesome stuff!

My sleep has been missing since Thursday last week. I sleep very lightly, feel like I'm mostly awake during the night, and I am calm (not jittery or anxious at all), and funnily enough my energy levels are good during the day, I don't crash or feel tired until sunset. Is it CT or something else? I know lots of people have gotten intermittent insomnia since starting CT... me included. Its different form the insomnia I had when my estrogen was too high (that was a very anxious and jittery kind!), but I'm painfully aware I need sleep to recover and restore... and I'm just not getting it.

You have made amazing progress! I think you look absolutely fantastic! I showed my dh your avatar and reminded him of what you started with. We are in awe! ### Glamorama — 2012-03-26T03:00:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-6164>

> **vkiernan;6193 wrote:** Put the first pic you showed us of you sitting on the sofa in front of your computer to your refrigerator or something where you can see it everyday along with your avatar pic. You have made amazing progress! I think you look absolutely fantastic! I showed my dh your avatar and reminded him of what you started with. We are in awe! I am sooo glad you have made such progress; without people like you - people like me might give up. You are an inspiration to keep all us older more broken going!

Aww thanks, that makes me very happy! I think my biggest hurdle is my mentality. I have such a hang-up on the scales that I blatantly ignore any other signs of progress. Old ED rearing its ugly head far back in the dark of my mind. My friend cut sugar and grains 6 weeks ago, and has lost weight, now being several kilos less than me. I was sort of jealous/still-happy-for-her, but the other day she stopped by and showed me her waist... She might weigh less, but it was all fat! Good lesson for me. Scales mean nothing, and she can't lift what I can lift nor can she even do 1/4 of a pull-up. But my progress is the reason she started her journey, and thats a massive bonus.

Seeing my Doc this week to request a few follow-up bloods; B12, T3, T4, reverse T3, estrogen. I also want to request cortisol and possibly testostorone. My stress-levels are through the roof and thats not a good thing long-term (as we all know). When I went to see him 2 months ago he didn't recognise me in the waiting room, how funny is that?! LOL

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-6410>

> **Glamorama;6171 wrote:** Let us know how you go! I've increased rapidly across all exersices, but it has slowed down now, in my 5th week of LHT. tha was expected though. I still have very good progress, and can finally do both a chin-up and pull-up unassisted, thats awesome stuff!
>
>
>
> My sleep has been missing since Thursday last week. I sleep very lightly, feel like I'm mostly awake during the night, and I am calm (not jittery or anxious at all), and funnily enough my energy levels are good during the day, I don't crash or feel tired until sunset. Is it CT or something else? I know lots of people have gotten intermittent insomnia since starting CT... me included. Its different form the insomnia I had when my estrogen was too high (that was a very anxious and jittery kind!), but I'm painfully aware I need sleep to recover and restore... and I'm just not getting it.

Will do... Finishing my CT session right now water is 59 degrees. Need more ice. But need a fridge/freezer that works first! Will head out to the Rec center for my Max LHT session and will report back!

I'm also having the night waking as well... Not sleeping the best either.

### L8F — 2012-03-26T18:21:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-6411>

> **natural\_girl;6445 wrote:** That's great. Where you taking any BHRT?
>
> Sorry, can't remember...

Just DHEA (up to 20 now since it is still low at 118) and Pregnenolone. Also most days Milk Thistle. Now I have started using OTC progesterone cream, but not sure I need it. It is tiny dose anyway...last three days took it. I think for me it was the increase in total cholesterol coupled with addition of DHEA that helped, but that is just my guess....Also when I looked back at old labs from the OB, my day three E used to be way higher, 49, and now it is down to 20s. Day 21 equiv of E is 163....not sure if this is low or high??? Ratio is still off though, so it must be lowish???? Head is tired of guessing on numbers that I really have no idea about!

PS, upped DHEA from 15 to 20 two days ago, sense of wellbeing increased a lot with the jump up. Same as when I first started it, cleared up the brain a bunch, and was happy happy each AM. Also productive.

so we'll see how things go next week...

### Brandy — 2012-03-27T04:08:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-6744>

I've been CTing for about 40 days off and on. Something interesting has been happening...my cycle has gone 38 days compared to 24 days it has been for most of my life. I'll be forty this summer. I've also been taking progesterone, pregn. and DHEA. Like many of you, I've been feeling really hungry with the CT. I've gained weight and have felt bloated. That may have something to do with the 50 billion probiotics I'm taking daily as well. I'm also killing off a parasite with wormwood and rascal. I've had breast tenderness for over a week now, along with the late cycle. Dr. K says it's due to the hypothalmus rewring. I've been feeling pretty frustrated with things because in January, I was my leannest and now I feel bloated, have prolonged sore boobs, grouchy and hungry. I was thinking it might have been the DHEA, but maybe it's something else. It's been good to read the posts...I know now, I'm not going crazy. I still don't feel good, but I'm encouraged others are experiencing some of the same things.

That actually sounds exactly what I went through in the last 5 weeks due to estrogen dominance when I burnt off a lot of fat through CT and then released estrogen into my blood stream! I was so bloated and so late for my TOM, but once TOM started, the bloat went out (with some help from herbal supplements) and I've broken through a stall now.

Insane amounts of energy today! Slept like a log all night (finally!), and found myself spontanously washing and cleaning the fronts of my ktichen-cupboards for the first time in (shame on me) 7 years!!! yes, theyw ere filthy, and no, I never had the stamina or willpower to tackle it in the past. I wonder what changed. B12-supplement? Vit D? CT? I've been through a month of fatigue prior to this... I'm always fascinated when things all of a sudden "feel right".

> **Lyndra;7806 wrote:** How about a pair of support pantyhose? They should provide compression if that's what you're looking for.

I went to the op shop (second hand clothing shop) and they had heaps of spanx-type shorts that come to the knee. I use them for ice packs and they provide compression. I can put ice on my upper and lower legs, both in and out of the bath. Also, if I don't use them, when I'm in the bath, I take blue ice bricks and either make sure they float near the problem areas (so that bit of the water is colder - and my skin gets cherry red) or, for example if it's inner thighs, just press one between my thighs while sitting in the tub!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-29T16:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-8246>

It's so strange, but I haven't considered myself LS because of the remaining hormone issues I need to sort out, but I've naturally decreased my BAB (I used to get 85-90 grams of protein in at breakfast) to now eating 30-45 grams and feeling stuffed. I have also added lunch back in because I feel fasting until supper stresses my adrenals, so all three of my meals are about the same size, with dinner being the heaviest.

I've been kind of a wimp on the CT the last couple of days. I haven't done a bath in almost a week, just continuing with the spot-CT and ending my showers in cold (about 2-3 minutes only). I know Dr. K says CT is cumulative, so I'm hoping even my small habits (which I feel are more sustainable in the long-run then hour baths each day), will help me with my thyroid and adrenals.
