### nuttmegs17 — 2012-03-30T00:06:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-8480>

So this is where the old crew is hanging out these days  I got a little lost when everyone switched over...there are so many interesting threads on this forum, I get a bit ADD and can't really focus. Sorry in advance for the novel, just want to catch up!

I'm doing really well - do not know if its the combo of ldn and CT or what, but i have been feeling much more like my old self again. My energy has been amazing, I have not experienced fatigue (unless warranted, like not getting sleep the night before). I was even able to ride my bike to work 2 times last week! I honestly thought I might not ever be able to do that again so it was really exciting! Its awesome to run errands with my husband and still have energy to go out that night. i feel much more like "me" than I have in a really really long while.

I did recently up my LDN one more time, and may back off of it bc i have been having more trouble sleeping which in turn affects my energy the next day. it may simply be too much, so I'll give it a week to work itself out and then decide. otherwise, to other hashi sufferers, i cannot recommend it enough. I do not know if this is in my head or if if it is even possible, but i have noticed that my food allergies since being on it, are not as severe. I got accidentally gluten-ed the other day and instead of it taking me out for days like it used to, it lasted an hour. Obviously still avoid it like the plague but i think its a really positive sign. Of course, these last several months, i have been taking gut-healing actions so perhaps its finally paying off!

Still CT-ing - try to do the baths (generally up to my waist with ice packs around my arms) around 55-60 degrees avg temps, around 4 times a week ...sometimes do shorter sessions but combine with spot CT-ing at night. I had horrible detox symptons like glam mentioned (estrogen releasing): bloating, horribly swollen/tender breasts, odd food cravings/hunger, etc but after my last period it has gotten A LOT better.

haven't yet gotten on BHT....saw Dr R - originally from the Dzugan Method but recently seperated (**shinjin - do you see Dr R? what do you think of him so far?)**...they didn't put the Sex hormones on my lab slip from last month so I have to wait until Monday (day 21 of my cycle) to get them drawn...he prescribed progesterone but I wasn't comfortable going on it until I had the full picture of all my hormones....some of your experiences with progesterone has me kinda nervous about how i will tolerate it...I will def be starting on pregnelone, but he didnt mention getting on DHEA - said my levels were fine...I will also be getting thyroid tests and a cortisol saliva test next week as well...interested to see how the LDN has affected my antibodies and my other thyroid levels, and also interested to see how my cortisol is doing.

Struggles: body image. even though i FEEL more like my old self, i still do not look like it. It was jarring to feel that great and then to realize I still have "hashi" face, still have bigger arms than normal, still heavier than normal (god, these 15 pounds irk me so much!), haven't really lost any weight since the leptin reset or the CT but am hoping its because my hormones just have to work themselves out..hoping BHT will be the final piece in the puzzle. My husband is very encouraging, tells me i look great, and that i have to give it time..i've only been feeling good for the last few weeks, it will take time for the rest to follow...smart man.

Random question: what causes ice cold hands and feet? i've always had them, although the last few days on vacation - bc of the warm temps possibly -they've been warmer and a bit swollen. My husbands are always warm when mine are freezing...is this something i should look to CT to fix?

Exciting progress: I am currently in the carolinas with a bunch of old college couples and we went whitewater rafting today...water temps were 60 and we had 2 opportunities to get in: 1. to jump off a cliff and into the river, and 2. to get out of the boat and float downstream for a while...I JUMPED at both opportunities when most of the girls HATED it, they could not get out of the water fast enough...after the initial shock, I really enjoyed it...I grinned at my husband and said "see, the CT training is working!" In years past, I would have been one of the girls scrambling out of the water, today I was the last one back it the boat!

Shinjin/Glam - awesome progress! You guys are really rocking these last couple of weeks!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-30T00:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-8488>

> **nuttmegs17;8528 wrote:** Random question: what causes ice cold hands and feet? i've always had them, although the last few days on vacation - bc of the warm temps possibly -they've been warmer and a bit swollen. My husbands are always warm when mine are freezing...is this something i should look to CT to fix?

Way to go! I love that the LDN is working for you. I keep my eye on it but I never really feel "bad" enough now that I'm on thyroid meds. Thanks for the report on it.

The cold hands/feet are often times due to poor circulation. My grandpa had it and so did my mom. My grandpas feet would literally go numb to the point where we had to take his drivers license away because he couldn't tell how hard he was pressing the gas pedal! He didn't like it but he was 91 anyway.

I have the circulation issue and I also have Reynauds. Obviously an epigenetic switch got turned on. Reynauds is an autoimmune and when your hands or feet get cold the extremities also get white and frozen/numb feeling. I'm not sure of the official description but that is what happens to me. I don't put my feet in the CT baths at all. I'm working up to that if I can. I still keep socks and gloves on and even yesterday I shivered through the whole thing and my fingers were frosty cold but not white. I live in Southern California so I had forgotten about Reynauds symptoms until I started CTing. Now if you don't have that you may just have poor circulation and need some something to bring about better blood flow. Nitric Oxide maybe? Anyone??

> It's so good to hear this...I've been feeling yucky with all the bloating and huge breasts, etc. I don't think I've ever been so happy for my cycle to start. There has been some improvement, but I'm not back to myself, yet.

5th week of CT nearly through, and I can say the mayhem is over for now. Once my got my very delayed TOM (on day 35), everything else seemed to fall into place. I peed a million times in 48 hours, and after than life has been a dream. I assume the body did a huge estrogen/toxins dump, and this is why I'm finally sleeping well, have good moods/energy, sore throat gone and my sex-drive is back to normal. The massive strenght-increase in the gym has slowed down a bit (as expected), but still going really well. I also think its because I'm not eating quite enough to fuel real muscle-growth? I average on 100 grams protein daily on the days I don't IF.

Another thing that finally happend: I lost that insane CT-hunger. Its gone. I'm left with a feeling of always being quite full, and IFing seems doable for the first time in many months. So big physical changes, indeed. And this could be my mind playing tricks on me, but I actually think I'm shedding less hair! I used to shed tons of hair every day, but now I just shed some... so here's to hoping.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-30T03:33:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-8543>

> **nuttmegs17;8528 wrote:**
>
>
>
> I'm doing really well - do not know if its the combo of ldn and CT or what, but i have been feeling much more like my old self again. My energy has been amazing, I have not experienced fatigue (unless warranted, like not getting sleep the night before). I was even able to ride my bike to work 2 times last week! I honestly thought I might not ever be able to do that again so it was really exciting! Its awesome to run errands with my husband and still have energy to go out that night. i feel much more like "me" than I have in a really really long while.
>
>
>
> Shinjin/Glam - awesome progress! You guys are really rocking these last couple of weeks!

Welcome back! So happy to hear about your progress, it sounds like you're nearly brand new! Whats LDN?

About the weight-loss thing... its such a super-slow procress. If it was caused by hormones, it will take a while before everything is in working order, and thats when the loss will start happening. I don't think I've ever lost weight as slowly as I've done in the last 10 months, but once I add it up I am quite happy with the numbers, even though I often go 5-8 weeks without any loss at all (and as you all know, it tends to drive me insane). Going by all the great signs you are experiencing, I would think 2012 is lining up to be a pretty good and healthy year for you!

> >

> >
> > The cold hands/feet are often times due to poor circulation. My grandpa had it and so did my mom. My grandpas feet would literally go numb to the point where we had to take his drivers license away because he couldn't tell how hard he was pressing the gas pedal! He didn't like it but he was 91 anyway.
> >
> >
> >
> > I have the circulation issue and I also have Reynauds. Obviously an epigenetic switch got turned on. Reynauds is an autoimmune and when your hands or feet get cold the extremities also get white and frozen/numb feeling. I'm not sure of the official description but that is what happens to me. I don't put my feet in the CT baths at all. I'm working up to that if I can. I still keep socks and gloves on and even yesterday I shivered through the whole thing and my fingers were frosty cold but not white. I live in Southern California so I had forgotten about Reynauds symptoms until I started CTing. Now if you don't have that you may just have poor circulation and need some something to bring about better blood flow. Nitric Oxide maybe? Anyone??
>
>
>
> Hmmm thats interesting. I never thought about reynaulds. i currently take nitric balance (assume it has nitric oxcide in it?) Here's to hoping it pays off. i'm hoping CT will take care of the rest!
>
>
>
> Brandy;8559 wrote:** Nuttmeggs said:
>
>
>
> Still CT-ing - try to do the baths (generally up to my waist with ice packs around my arms) around 55-60 degrees avg temps, around 4 times a week ...sometimes do shorter sessions but combine with spot CT-ing at night. I had horrible detox symptons like glam mentioned (estrogen releasing): bloating, horribly swollen/tender breasts, odd food cravings/hunger, etc but after my last period it has gotten A LOT better
>
>
>
> It's so good to hear this...I've been feeling yucky with all the bloating and huge breasts, etc. I don't think I've ever been so happy for my cycle to start. There has been some improvement, but I'm not back to myself, yet.

Yes, keep doing it -. It def gets worse before it gets better but take those "bumps in the road" as a positive sign that your body is detoxing. It means it working (just keep telling yourself that!). I assume i'll have more bumps bc Dr K said its not a linear process...you get better, then you have a set back, then you have gains again....i just try to remind myself of this when i can

Glamorama;8591 wrote: Welcome back! So happy to hear about your progress, it sounds like you're nearly brand new! Whats LDN?

About the weight-loss thing... its such a super-slow procress. If it was caused by hormones, it will take a while before everything is in working order, and thats when the loss will start happening. I don't think I've ever lost weight as slowly as I've done in the last 10 months, but once I add it up I am quite happy with the numbers, even though I often go 5-8 weeks without any loss at all (and as you all know, it tends to drive me insane). Going by all the great signs you are experiencing, I would think 2012 is lining up to be a pretty good and healthy year for you!

thanks for the encouragement. Super slow is right  I will keep on keeping on.

LDN = low dose naltrexone. It was mentioned to me in passing by Dr K and I was asked to get on it by Chris Kressor as well. It is called Low Dose bc Naltrexone at its normal dosage is around 50mg and is generally used (since the 70s or 80s) to get drug addicts off drugs...somewhere along the line, people figured out that naltrexone at a low does (4.5 or less) does SOMETHING to modulate the immune system...basically, the low dose turns off opiate receptors at night for a couple of hours and when they turn back on its like a super surgue of endorphins goes through the body...many people have reversed symptons of MS, stalled tumor growths, and people with hashi's sometimes go into remission or at least have to back off their thyroid meds. its considered "off label use" bc pharm companies dont even make it available in low doses like that...I have to get it at a compounding pharmacy. It will most likely remain off label bc drug companies see no point in funding clinical trials for a medicine that has barely any side affects even at a high dose...what the point for them?

If anyone reads elana's pantry , she had a recent blog post on how she deals with MS...primal diet, fish oil, etc and LDN! I was happy to see it legitimized there.

So far so good with it. Although, I do think i'll go back to the 2.5 dose for a while.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-30T19:15:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-8846>

I'm not a hormone expert but.....when I was having hot flashes I took a little progesterone and they went away. I have never been a fan of estrogen in my body but always liked the way the progesterone made me feel. I know why now because I am super estrogen dominant. It got worse when a doc I had been seeing gave me Bi-est because I had hot flashes. I took it for three weeks and stopped because they symptoms were all leading to ED. My gut feeling old me this wasn't the right treatment plan for me but I thought the doc new better than me. Now I am in the middle of huge estrogen being released from my body. While I'm taking DIM, milk thistle, and NAC I'm still feeling many symptoms. I say go by your gut feeling until you can get in to see the doc.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-01T04:00:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-9635>

Another question for all you who have experienced estrogen detox and liver detox in general. My CT detox symptoms suddenly went through the roof last night. After an ice bath of 40 minutes, I started to feel nauseas. Not unusual for me. But then the nausea got worse and stopped me from sleeping most of the night. (I kept wondering if it was food poisoning, but I ate nothing different or unusual.) Then this morning, I had a medium-ish breakfast with some hunger and some nausea. And since breakfast (normal foods - meat and fat), I have had multiple bouts of awful diarrhea with no signs of abating. (Sorry for TMI.) I know Dr K has said CT is cumulative and after listening to his TedX talk where one big dose of ice stopped his surgical pain, it's pretty clear that single doses of CT can do a LOT. But do you think this sounds like detox? (I've had other milder detox symptoms ongoing... mild headaches, mild nausea, skin rash, pimples.) I am not hungry for lunch, but realised I've been craving salty and sugary foods and for the first time in months I felt dizzy upon standing, so I've eaten some sauerkraut and taken some salt in water, and some ACV too.

I'm totally OK with things if this is just detox stepping up a notch. But am wondering if this sounds familiar to anyone else.

TOM delayed, bloating, weight gain, moody, acute carb/sugar cravings, hungry a lot of the time, pimples that I never get, wonderful skin texture changing to crappy/patchy and dry with my sleep not only going to crap but has gone from7-8 hrs to 10+. My nose and fingers are kinda chilly too. I seem to be hypersensitive to cold atm. I'm a bit of a wreck!!

Yup, that was me up until about a week ago. Due to time restrictions, I end up mostly spot-CTing, but I do it daily without fail. I squeeze in a bath when I can. My sleep has returned and I have the deepest most wonderful sleep now. Slept from 9 pm till 6 am last night! Fear not, it will pass, but you can speed up the process with some EstroFactors supplements, that was my lifesaver when iw as going through hell with the hormones.

Yesterday I participated in my first fun run. It was the easy course, only 5 kms (instead of 14.5 kms). I thought I was going to walk it all, seeing I don't run or jog EVER! but I actually ended up running the whole distance and loving it! I don't know where the strenght and energy comes from, but we know Doc would say CT.  I went to the gym this morning tod o my triceps/chest WO. Weights I could barely do 8 reps with 7 days ago, were now so easy I had to add heavier dumb bells to my lifts. Its really fascinating... my strenght increases in spurts, but its always incredibly increases, at the rate I'm packing on muscle (and I'm doing it with mostly fat and protein!) I'm worrying I really need to see the fat going too, otherwise I'll just look big! But so far I'm looking smaller. And I can finally open jars by myself. LOL

### indigogirl — 2012-04-23T04:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-17964>

Glamorama , I use a large nasal syringe with a bulb on the end...same principal as a Neti pot. Use a Luke warm saline solution in a Neti pot to get all the icky mucous out of your sinuses. I would also push vitamin C, D3, and also take something like olive leaf extract or oregano oil orally for the infection. Also inhale steam by itself or with some eucalyptus leaves or oil thrown in (boil water in a large pot, turn off the stove, tent towel over the pot and inhale- but be careful not to burn your face...the steam can be very hot).

Feel better!

Ok, ive used my tricks on myself and hubby and works great! You will have to have some honey tho.

Make a warm tea with apple cider vinegar (raw organic with the mother). Put in honeycomb from local to taste. Let the wax melt - eat the chunks on top of tea. 3x a day for 3 days.

Also if you get subsequent ear infection - warm olive oil, steep crushed garlic in it, then drip into ear. This kills ear infections.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-25T23:17:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-18851>

> **Glamorama;18711 wrote:** Good point. When should I retest then? Another thing that baffles me is how well I feel. I assume that if my adrenals were really exhausted by now, I'd feel really fatigued and ill? I don't though.

I guess you retest when you are sure you are done with detoxing. The other spanner in the works, of course, esp re: thyroid/adrenals is that you are becoming cold adapted. So your labs will be matched with warm adapted ranges and even if those are 'optimal' they are not for cold adapted humans. I think this is relatively unknown territory so will be hard to compare apples and oranges (or bananas and beef, as the case may be).

### Seeker — 2012-04-26T17:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-19042>

Help, please!

I'm not post reset but thought I would post here since you all seem to have been doing this longest and have a lot of experience.

Since starting the Leptin rx, I think my hair is falling out at a much greater than normal rate. At first I thought I was imagining it, but I really don't think so anymore. When I grab my hair to put it in a ponytail, it feels much less than it used to. I seem to remember someone mentioning hair loss on some thread I read (maybe the old one on MDA). I'm about 7 weeks into the reset. Have never checked labs. Have lost a couple of pounds but not much. The only markers of leptin sensitivity that i can maybe check off now would be better/deeper sleep and sometimes able to skip meals. No improvement in libido and not sure about sweating since I'm not exercise much anymore. Would like to continue with reset but am becoming a little frustrated with the progress and the hair loss is freaking me out.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-04-26T23:51:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-19146>

> **Seeker;19115 wrote:** Help, please!
>
>
>
> I'm not post reset but thought I would post here since you all seem to have been doing this longest and have a lot of experience.
>
>
>
> Since starting the Leptin rx, I think my hair is falling out at a much greater than normal rate. At first I thought I was imagining it, but I really don't think so anymore. When I grab my hair to put it in a ponytail, it feels much less than it used to. I seem to remember someone mentioning hair loss on some thread I read (maybe the old one on MDA). I'm about 7 weeks into the reset. Have never checked labs. Have lost a couple of pounds but not much. The only markers of leptin sensitivity that i can maybe check off now would be better/deeper sleep and sometimes able to skip meals. No improvement in libido and not sure about sweating since I'm not exercise much anymore. Would like to continue with reset but am becoming a little frustrated with the progress and the hair loss is freaking me out.

Hair loss could be a multitude of things...If I had to guess, I would point to hormones. I'm struggled with the same thing. I probably lost half of my hair (my lowest point was after a shower, and I had not one but two handfuls of hair that fell out!). I was constantly cold -- to the point, where I would cry myself to sleep. No energy, absolutely no libido, lots of stress, etc.

Turns out I have hashimotos, adrenal fatigue and wonky cortisol/hormone levels. I would suggest you get some labs done -- consider testing your thyroid and adrenals. You can order at a discount from canary club. Since I've been tested, I've gotten on t3 and Wilson's Adrenal Rebuilder, as well as supporting supplements. Although I'm not 100% yet, I have bursts of energy and a rise in libido. I have fewer days where I crash and am exhausted, and I have tons of new hair growth!

Keep us posted on your progress. I'm sure others will chime in with different ideas, but this is what has helped me.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-27T00:59:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-19166>

I tested hormones and iron. Nothing in those tests indicated reasons for hair loss, as was good in iron and too high in estrogen.

### ehanner@gmail.com — 2012-05-02T23:16:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-20818>

Sorry for the bother but can someone tell me what MSM is please? I've been doing cold showers and focusing on my stomach and under my arms. It looks like the flab is being absorbed.

### Destiny — 2012-05-03T03:27:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-20870>

> **EHanner;20891 wrote:** Sorry for the bother but can someone tell me what MSM is please? I've been doing cold showers and focusing on my stomach and under my arms. It looks like the flab is being absorbed.

MSM (Methylsulfonylmethane) - is a naturally occurring sulfur compound found within our bodies. It is a safe and natural, assimilable food derived from the ocean. MSM is neither a medicine, drug, chemical, herb, stimulant, synthetic food or additive. It is a member of the sulfur family but should never be confused with sulfa drugs, to which some people are allergic. It is completely safe, odorless, and non-toxic. A 160 pound man has approximately 4 pounds of sulfur as body weight.

### Glamorama — 2012-05-04T01:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-21150>

Officially on my 3rd round of detox here... Cough and phlegm gone, replaced by pimples on forehead and chest, as well as crappy mood. Tipping I'm going through another estrogen dump. Saw naturopath last night, and finally got it through my thick skull that his scales are useless! I was told I've gone from 29% BF to 32% in a month!!! And loss of lean mass by 2 pounds. Yeah right. Last month I was squatting 60 lbs, yesterday I squatted 160 lbs. And by eye measurements, I'm tipping I'm 26-27% BF. I was angry with the stupid scales yesterday, and pretty much gave the poor naturopath a lashing!! Yup estrogen out of control again!

I'm feeling like an emotional wreck today. Fragile, stressed, anxious. Really stressed out. According to the iridiology, my liver is working hard, adrenals are in overdrive, and my cortisol probably too high. Detox is good but also bad. I'm so tired of it, and how it makes me feel on a bad day. I haven't had deep sleep in over a week either, due to the kids. This weekend I'll be doing very little and just focus on getting to bed early.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-05T03:02:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-21472>

If his scale is a bioimpedance measurement, I'd be very skeptical about the values. That's what my home scale has and the numbers are all over the map. Caliper measurements on several occasions say I'm around 20 percent BF but the lowest I've ever seen on my scale is 24 percent. There are days when it will read 30, then I'll step off and back on again, and it shows 26. Hydration levels influence readings a lot and who knows what else. I know I get way too wrapped up in what the scale says in terms of weight but I know the BF readings are just nonsense. You look awesome! Don't let his scales get you wound up.

### Glamorama — 2012-05-05T12:04:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-21563>

> **Lyndra;21548 wrote:** If his scale is a bioimpedance measurement, I'd be very skeptical about the values. That's what my home scale has and the numbers are all over the map. There are days when it will read 30, then I'll step off and back on again, and it shows 26. Hydration levels influence readings a lot and who knows what else. I know I get way too wrapped up in what the scale says in terms of weight but I know the BF readings are just nonsense. You look awesome! Don't let his scales get you wound up.

Yep that's his scales. Effin useless, lol! I've asked my PT to bring in calipers for me, when the naturopath toldmemy dinner affected the results, I lost the plot!!! Lol

### Lyndra — 2012-05-06T20:00:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-21790>

> **Glamorama;21639 wrote:** Yep that's his scales. Effin useless, lol! I've asked my PT to bring in calipers for me, when the naturopath toldmemy dinner affected the results, I lost the plot!!! Lol

I bought some calipers online for about $15 USD. They came with instructions and a chart to help estimate your bodyfat. You'll need a helper to do a few of the measurements (upper arm) but you can easily do most (tummy, thigh, hip) by yourself. Measurements are not affected by recent food/drink consumption and once you get the hang of it, should be fairly accurate and repeatable.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-08T05:41:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-22115>

I keep spewing this useless information all over the place, but no one ever responds, so I shall continue. LOL.

I find calipers to be grossly inaccurate as an estimator of my body fat %. The problem is, it doesn't measure the places I carry fat! I get it on the butt and side of my upper thigh, whereas the calipers measure upper arm, hip, and front of thigh. I used to get 12-13% ratings no matter where I went or who did the measuring, and there is no way I was ever that lean. I was 5'4", 120-130lb, never below a size 7, and by no means a strength goddess, although certainly more fit than most girls my age. I had VERY fatty thighs, just not where they measure!

So there. I don't put much stock in calipers, either.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-leptin-rx-support-thread.106/post-22463>

I want to reach 25 then work my way down.

### Glamorama — 2012-05-09T04:54:20+0300

I hadn't eaten since lunch (more than 9 hours) so I was feeling quite hungry. I ate lots of cheese, and 3-4 strawberries with the cheese. This morning I had to pay the price... dairy does not agree with me at all! I had pimply spots/hives across both thighs (this happend last time I had dairy too), and full-blown seasonal hay-fever attack, lots of sneezing, watery eyes, and snot... I've learnt its simply not worth having dairy when i feel like this for the next 30 hours. Lesson learnt.

Just took my 5th dose of TCM... the sinus headache is gone but green snot keeps coming out. ### Glamorama — 2012-05-20T05:59:32+0300

### Glamorama — 2012-05-21T06:30:54+0300

Thanks. You got me thinking and I realised the following:

8 weeks ago I started the gym. About 7 weeks ago I did my first chin-up and pull-up.

Sinuses started playing up 6 weeks ago (I put it down to detox from CT, and still do)

5 weeks ago I developed intense muscular pain in my neck. 1 accupuncture session and lots of magnesium oil on my neck stopped the pain, but things still arent right.

Fast forward to this week, and I have headaches daily (not just in the sinues, but also abck of my head), and I can't seem to recover from this cold/sinusitis. I think all evidence points towards a neck injury, which in turn caused all the other issues.

Seeing chiro in 4 hours. Boy I can't wait.

### Glamorama — 2012-05-22T01:24:39+0300

Like an enormous burden had been lifted off me. Then I felt a strong heat rising in my entire head. I went home and went to bed early. I had this achey, heavy feeling moving down through my body. So weird!!! Anyway, woke up today feeling very good. No signs of headache or sore jaws/neck. Kneed one more adjustment, and was told I'll be very snotty today as the sinuses will empty out.

The chiro said that gym was not to blame, I probably already had issues inmyneck when I started, and the exercise just made it worse at a faster rate.

# Thread: Brrrr--- mind games; and wrapping up after - same affect?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bethany — 2012-03-15T05:29:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-541>

Does anyone have any good ways to keep your mind off the cold while in the shower?

Also, is it okay to wrap up in your cozy robe right after, or does that blow the whole thing.

Man, I just can't imagine getting into an ice tub.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:39:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-550>

I have toa dmit I find it crazy hard to sit in the cold tub and just wait for 40 min to pass. I have to read and keep myself occupied so i don't think about the cold!

Shower are easier, I like to think about how good this is for my hair and skin! Vain but it works, hehe, and you do get sued to it quite quickly.

### taryn.ourso@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T05:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-565>

> **Bethany;542 wrote:** Does anyone have any good ways to keep your mind off the cold while in the shower?
>
>
>
> Also, is it okay to wrap up in your cozy robe right after, or does that blow the whole thing.
>
>
>
> Man, I just can't imagine getting into an ice tub.

I don't know about the shower, but when I'm in the bath, I keep my laptop close enough to watch a movie or Youtube sometimes. You could listen to some podcasts about Paleo, etc, or whatever else floats your boat.

I think the robe is a good idea. I stay as unclothed as I can right after, but there always comes a point where I feel like it's time to put the robe on. Dr. Kruse mentions that after he comes inside from CT, he puts on a terrycloth robe. I think the main idea is to try not to get too warm too fast...a robe will help you to retain your body heat without applying artificial heat.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-15T05:58:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-569>

> **Glamorama;551 wrote:** I have toa dmit I find it crazy hard to sit in the cold tub and just wait for 40 min to pass. I have to read and keep myself occupied so i don't think about the cold!
>
>
>
> Shower are easier, I like to think about how good this is for my hair and skin! Vain but it works, hehe, and you do get sued to it quite quickly.

I find the baths MUCH easier than the showers. I tried the shower a couple of times and then went to the bath. I now do the baths with ice (though not yet up to 60 pounds of it!) and last 45 minutes. I listen to podcasts or put my laptop on a stool outside of the tub so I can reach it but it can't fall in. It's only the first few minutes that seem really cold. Getting out is another story... I'm still shivering for at least 30 minutes. (I put on clothes but try not to put on too much. Then I try to keep moving!)

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T06:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-572>

> **August;555 wrote:** when I am sitting in the cool water (I've not braved the ice bath yet) . . . I like to visualize . . _well that might not be the exact right word . . feel-ize maybe_ . . . that my body is producing/radiating so much heat that it is actually warming the water around me to a warm, comfortable temperature . . .from all the energy from fat that my body is burning . . . some people believe that visualizations can have physiological effects . . I'm not sure about that . . but it is nice to think warm, toasty thoughts!!!

I visualized my way to birth out a 9.4 pound baby. Yup, it works!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T06:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-601>

I read a book or goof off on my iPhone. I've tried mindfulness and meditation but so far I just can't stick to it. I'm not good at either of those when warm! Reading, especially this blog or its comments really helps the time pass (obviously in a bath). I can't take the cold showers at all. It's like torture.

### DarleenMB — 2012-03-15T06:57:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-603>

This A.M. was my second go at a tepid shower. A big change since I LOVE to stand in a hot shower for 20 minutes at a time. When I was about ready to get out I moved the control over just a smidge and got hit with cold (but not icy full cold) ... and to my surprise it felt GREAT! Much better than the tepid water had been. Now that's just plain weird.

So have you all done the face in the pan thing and moved on to the shower or just skipped right ahead?

I ordered a compression shirt from Amazon today (since there were none to be found in any of the local stores) and am hoping to move to the ice on the chest routine in a week or so.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T08:07:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-622>

Darleen, I skipped straight to spot-CT. But I was raised in Norway!

### primat — 2012-03-15T14:01:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-675>

Don't fear the tub - you might like it more that the showers since you don't constantly have to readjust bodyparts to the cold water. For me, 50F shower is pure terror but a 50F tub is cozy

### bennyha — 2012-03-15T14:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-688>

> **hebsie;682 wrote:** ...this, ice showers just plain suck!...hebs

I agree completely. I find the cold showers to be absolute hell. But doing a cold tub for 35 mins at 50F while listening to sports radio is very easy for me

### Jasmin — 2012-03-15T15:58:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-742>

You could print out everything that Jack has posted inclucive all the questions and answers  I have printet out a lot of it and am surpriced how quick time flyes by.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T16:55:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-795>

I can't stand the freezing cold shower. Much easier for my body to adjust to the tub. I sit in the tub and then start running the water. I like that the cold covers my body gradually. Also, a good book is a MUST for me. The time goes by quite quickly that way.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-16T01:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-1132>

I have my iPod nearby and listen to a podcast of some sort - Jimmy Moore, Chris Kresser, etc. Hearing them talk about issues I'm working on is good motivation to stay in the tub. I also try not to allow myself to shiver too much. The first time I did a cold tub, I tried to read but I was shaking so much it took me several minutes per page.

I do bundle up after my tub, and even then I still shake and shiver for an hour or more. I figure that as I adapt, I'll be able to wear fewer clothes afterwards. I'm willing to take longer to experience the effects. Plus, seeing me shake makes my husband worry.

### HeyDianne — 2012-03-16T02:44:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brrrr-mind-games-and-wrapping-up-after-same-affect.108/post-1192>

Lyndra,

I completely understand the husband worry aspect. The worry coupled with the fact that he has always thought that I have the coldest rear end on the planet makes the family sleep aspect a challenge. Tonight I plan on icing while the one hour show he watches the one show he watches followed by a cold soak, we shall see what the complaints will be. My newest response has been "what, do you want me fat?" and it has been working at the moment.

# Thread: confused abt CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-abt-ct.109/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### optimeyes — 2012-03-15T05:39:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-abt-ct.109/post-549>

I'm in central Florida. We have probably 2 weeks of "cold" weather and then it's just cold mornings, and now we are at 65 degrees around dawn, getting up to 85. The pool is already 78 degrees without the heater. There's really no way

to get cold from my environment  So the best I've been able to do is stop doing super hot showers and today was the first day of "cold" shower (I don't know the temp but it was prob 80 degrees, so not really COLD lol)

So I don't even know if my tiny attempts are going to do a darned thing. I mean, I have been turning the air down from 78 degrees in the house to 75. Before I would be cold at 75 and now I am getting used to it. (We moved from Md to Fl in 2006; before we moved we kept the home temp at 70 degrees, but the electric bill down here gets up into the 500$ range per month even at 78 degree setting... soon it will be hot and I won't be able to afford cranking down to 75!)

From reading Jack's blog, I thought he was saying that people in warm climates would be able to eat more carbs, but those where there were definite non-growing seasons, those people needed to be strict carnivores during the hibernation months?? But then tonight where I was reading, he seemed to be saying if you ate carbs and lived in a warm environment, you were doomed.

I am ready to move! Hahah! Great, people move to Florida to die.

### Garfield — 2012-03-15T05:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-abt-ct.109/post-553>

Jack would say: "Last I heard you could still get ice in Florida."

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T05:45:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-abt-ct.109/post-556>

Jack also said that we all stem from cold adapted mammals or species that developed in the icy cold oceans long before we became land-living, smart hominids. So I think thats the original blueprint for the cold-adapted pathway in our DNA.

>
> to get cold from my environment  So the best I've been able to do is stop doing super hot showers and today was the first day of "cold" shower (I don't know the temp but it was prob 80 degrees, so not really COLD lol)

>
>
> So I don't even know if my tiny attempts are going to do a darned thing. I mean, I have been turning the air down from 78 degrees in the house to 75. Before I would be cold at 75 and now I am getting used to it. (We moved from Md to Fl in 2006; before we moved we kept the home temp at 70 degrees, but the electric bill down here gets up into the 500$ range per month even at 78 degree setting... soon it will be hot and I won't be able to afford cranking down to 75!)
>
>
>
> From reading Jack's blog, I thought he was saying that people in warm climates would be able to eat more carbs, but those where there were definite non-growing seasons, those people needed to be strict carnivores during the hibernation months?? But then tonight where I was reading, he seemed to be saying if you ate carbs and lived in a warm environment, you were doomed.
>
> I am ready to move! Hahah! Great, people move to Florida to die.

I am in a similar situation in Mexico- there is no way to turn down Mother Nature's heat since we don't have air conditioning.

I have quite a lot of ice blocks which I keep in the freezer between CT sessions in the tub. It helps a lot to cool the tap water down but I have no thermometer yet to track how cold it gets. We are in a small town and I guess my best bet might be a pet store in the closest major center, so next trip to Manzanillo I will check for one.

Since I am still overweight I am using coconut oil as well as lots of seafood in my diet. Spring is starting already in Mexico but when we get home to northern BC I will have ice cold creek water to work with- so much easier!

### mumone — 2012-03-16T00:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-abt-ct.109/post-1106>

I hear you...I'm in a subtropical world too....

I run my car aircon on 16 and recirculate,

keep our bed room door shut at night and run the aircon at 16 (its lowest setting) and wear socks , a beanie but no bed covers or pjs (much)

now DH tells me I'm always warm even when I feel like I'm cold, which always makes me think - Awesome I'm burning fat!!

then as they say - get some ice in your bath , and wear as little clothing as possible all the time, and lurk about the freezer room at the shops

### AKMan — 2012-03-16T01:36:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-abt-ct.109/post-1148>

Do you live near the ocean or Gulf? You could start swimming there in the cooler months. Other than that, cold showers, cold baths, ice packs. Drink ice-cold water all day long, chewing the ice as you go. Take advantage of whatever cold comes your way and eat a good, clean paleo low-carb diet.

Good Luck!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Dr K said some where that his bottom half is skinny so he keeps the water warmer and uses ice where he needs it. So do you think spot icing is a good/best idea? Thanks for any help - I have learned so much from all of you

### Glamorama — 2012-03-15T06:28:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-595>

> **caroline;588 wrote:** I have been following/lurking on MDA for a long time. I am wondering about cold bathe vs cold showers vs spot icing. I have really skinny legs and narrow hips with a lot of fat around my middle \[think skinny/fat\] so I have been thinking that putting ice on my middle would be the best.
>

I spot-CT my waist, have done so for a month. It seems to affect fat-reduction all over, but especially in my upper body. My waist is slightly smaller, but thanks to bloating I don't think I'll ever get a flat stomach no matter how much I CT. People seem to reap more benefits to their health by doing full submerges though. The shower is just a helpful tool in getting used to cold... on its own it will never do what spot-CT or submerge can do.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T17:08:11+0200

### BK Davis — 2012-03-15T17:46:27+0200

### ChimpChick — 2012-03-15T18:33:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-875>

Yay! A forum!!

That's just from ice packs, I can't imagine what you tub people can accomplish!

### vkiernan — 2012-03-15T19:10:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-900>

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T19:37:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-916>

Every time I do CT, even just a face dunk, I sleep like a rock that night. People need to know that even if they don't lose a pound or an inch, good sleep is worth its weight in gold. 20 minutes shivering in a tub is much better than 6 hours tossing and turning, and the 8 groggy hours that follow.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-15T19:53:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-928>

benefits I have experienced:

facial skin tighter, very small pores, very clear skin

No allergies (yet) while everyone else is sniffling and sneezing--- I did have lots of sinus drainage, but that seems to have abated.

Sleep is great, deep but I don't always wake up refreshed. It is overall improved though. I have been sleeping with the window open since December (winter here in NJ) and that helped too.

Very little soreness with my training, I'm amateur competitive PL, and my recovery is great. My deadlift has been consistently going up, and that was my iffy lift. All the planets had to line up right, so to speak, for a good lift, and now I am just pulling with ease. And not really feeling it next day. Amazing. There really isn't a way for me to do CT and THEN lift, and honestly, my legs are so numb, I'm not sure that would be too smart, at least with squatting LOL I don't want to unrack 220# and "see what happens" I guess I could try it with bench or dl, but again, I can't really arrange to soak and then train.

My libido has improved, wink wink nudge nudge

Overall mood brighter, more even keel, less apt to anger/stress out

INcreased productivty @ work: cramming in all my reports so I have time to skim blog and forums LOL and @ home, to scurrying around the house tidying up, getting clothes out for next day, etc before it gets dark out. I still use laptop with flux, dont use phone or watch tv after dark.

I will have DH do measurements this Saturday, will be 2 weeks

### Lyndra — 2012-03-16T01:10:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1129>

I am seeing vision improvement (sorry, no pun intended). I had LASIK a dozen years ago but for the last few years, my sight has been fuzzy much of the time. Now I notice how much more clear everything is, especially when driving. I drove last night in the dark and wow, what a difference. In the past, I'd have an occasional good day but this has been a more than a week of sharp eyesight.

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-16T01:22:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1133>

My adrenals feel like they finally woke up.

### AKMan — 2012-03-16T01:29:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1140>

I sleep better. I used to wake every night at 3am and toss-n-turn til alarm went off at 6. Now, I sleep all night and wake up right before the alarm goes off.

I've been one of those "how do you lose the last 10lbs" kinda guys for about 6 months. Well, I found the answer! I've been CT'ing in cold air and some ice-packs on the belly for about 6 weeks and broke a 6 month stall at 172 lbs, now down to 167 (I'm a 5'10/47yo dude).

### Croak — 2012-03-16T02:00:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1166>

> **Lyndra;1130 wrote:** I am seeing vision improvement (sorry, no pun intended). I had LASIK a dozen years ago but for the last few years, my sight has been fuzzy much of the time. Now I notice how much more clear everything is, especially when driving. I drove last night in the dark and wow, what a difference. In the past, I'd have an occasional good day but this has been a more than a week of sharp eyesight.

You know, I've had the same sort of improvement, also had LASIK (2004). My right eye especially. But, I attribute this more to the LR diet than CT.

### Rhoswen — 2012-03-16T06:46:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1311>

1\. My acne is going away and what acne I had at the start of the Leptin RX and CT is healing up VERY quickly, much quicker than I've EVER seen it heal before! It heals up even faster when I do CT and use my Tea Tree oil.

2\. This might be TMI but while my libido hasn't really increased, I find it much easier to climax. I'm a lady, fyi.

3\. Oh, another thing with my skin... I had a pretty deep smile line on the right side of my face. I'm only 27 so I was kinda not too happy to have such a deep line in my face, even if it was a smile/laugh line... But it seems like it's far less deep after doing CT for a couple of weeks. I don't know if my mind is playing tricks on me or not.

4\. My sleep seems to have gotten deeper. I don't wake up in the morning wishing I had more time to lay around in bed, I find it much easier to get myself up and going. I think I also fall asleep faster than I used to.

5\. My place of work is kept fairly cold because we have lots of important machines running that we don't want to over heat.. I used to free constantly at work. I'd have to wear a hoodie under my lab coat (and sometimes I'd put my hood OVER my head even) while I was at my computer. Now, I stay totally warm. Even when I was eating a good Primal diet, there were nights were I'd get chilled. I haven't had this happen since doing CT. Now sometimes I have to run to the sink to cool my sweaty hands down! All the other ladies I work with think I'm crazy. They're cold all the time.

### BK Davis — 2012-03-16T16:01:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1431>

vkiernan, after I read Jack's statement about his major increase in max bench following a long CT session, I decided to see how it would apply to me.

At this time, I don't have the set up or the time to do the long sessions like Jack. I had been doing some excercise (pushups, squats, etc.) before my CT sessions in the shower. I switched, out of curiosity, to doing the exercise after CT.

Even with a shorter duration and different application, I was able to do more with greater ease. I've just begun doing this and am trying to dial it in for me. I can't give you a hard number like jack as I'm using body weight exercise but the effect was immediately noticeable.

I'll report more down the road.

### BK Davis — 2012-03-16T16:18:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1441>

Another thing I've noticed:

Jack mentioned in one of the CT blogs about one's sense of "Well Being" increasing with CT due to ACTH rising along with alpha MSH. This seems to prove true by me.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-16T16:21:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1444>

In close to 6 wks of CT (in the combination of spot CTing around my trunk & baths) is my skin looks younger

I've also notice some reduction in belly fat. I actually tried on my bikini for this summer and I only have a little belly while standing up so I look good in bikini standing up still not optimal while sitting ( still a bit pudgy). But I'm on my way!

Currently sitting in a bath. Trying it earlier vs later and I'll see how it goes

### jayjha1@gmail.com — 2012-03-16T17:28:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1500>

so yesterday - I had 12 calories - yes a whole freakin 12

but it was near painless! seriously - woke up this morning 2.8 lbs less than last week which means that is three weeks in a row a plateau has been broken - this is including a 3 day disaster trip to Vegas

plus my system 'feels' stronger - normally after a Vegas weekend of drinking and dancing all night and tobacco (not me, others) - I'm sick for a week! this time - all good! (knock on wood)

I can't stop talking about this cold stuff with my family! I mean, how hard is it to blast you AC while you drive to work and turn your heater at home off?

Thanks Dr. Kruse !

### AKMan — 2012-03-16T18:46:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1578>

From a study at: [http://ukpmc.ac.uk/abstract/MED/551265](http://http://ukpmc.ac.uk/abstract/MED/551265)

Measurement of energy balance during voluntary overeating in rats unequivocally establishes the quantitative importance of diet-induced thermogenesis in energy balance. Like cold-induced thermogenesis, this form of heat production involves changes in the activity of the sympathetic nervous system and brown adipose tissue which suggest that this tissue may determine metabolic efficiency and resistance to obesity.

All you can see at the link is what I pasted above, you have to subscribe to see the whole study, but it looks it backs up what Dr. K. has said all along--calories become meaningless! It looks to me like adequate stores of brown fat, which you build up CT'ing, also activate when one overeats, meaning you'd burn extra calories as heat and not store it all as fat.

### hazyjane — 2012-03-17T01:09:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1758>

I love reading people's improvements!

I've been doing cold showers, several cold baths/week and nearly daily spot CT'ing.

So far, I've had a reduction in belly fat, my skin is softer/younger looking and I get sleepy much earlier in the evenings (I've been a life-long night owl!). I've increased my kettlebell swings from 30-50 reps in a row right after CTing, as well as improved my pushups. I can now do 10 deep (nose a few inches from the floor) girl pushups- I could never get that low before!

I'm also starting to have warmer body temps, eps. at night. I sleep with less covers and I feel warm, but not hot. I'm not used to feeling so much circulation in my hands and feet. Another nice side effect: my singing voice is clearer, esp. in the higher range!

I've also had more good energy days in a row than I have in a long time and yesterday my brain energy was great! I was actually looking for tasks to accomplish, which is not my normal state of affairs

### Lyndra — 2012-03-17T01:35:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1770>

One new improvement - no IT band pain after a longish bike ride today.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-17T02:54:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1806>

I'm looking for some encouragement tonight as I'm feeling pretty discouraged. It's great reading so many improvements in folks, but I'm wondering if those seeing smaller waistlines and fat reduction were already almost to their body goals before starting CT? Any correlation there? Dr. Kruse has said the more you have to lose, the longer it will take to see benefits of CT (but that the benefits are still happening under the proverbial surface).

I've been doing CT baths and spot-icing for 2.5 weeks and am really not seeing any benefits yet at all. I'm an emotional mess like some kind of pregnant-neverending-PMS thing, sinuses drainage like Niagara Falls which finally has abated, tiny acne-like things popping up in various places on my skin, and periodic fatigue like I'm dragging myself through water (ha). I've been eating primal for 10 weeks, 6 of it have been doing the Leptin Reset, and I've lost no weight to speak of nor any inches. My clothing doesn't really feel any different either. In the last 3 weeks since CT I've been consciously keeping my carbs below 50g a day and really averaging more like 15-30 most days. I"ve kept my CT process under wraps from family and friends because I keep waiting for all the pieces to fall into place so I can then unveil my wonderful new discover of cold therapy and hear them oooh and ahh instead of laugh at me or chastise me for "trying to get hypothermia".

The only benefits I can speak of so far have been not being hungry all the time and better sleep since starting CT, but my sleep wasn't all that bad before. I'm happy to dine on bacon and steak and not be hungry, but where is all this 'effortless' weight loss and increased energy Mark Sisson and Dr. Kruse and everyone else talks about?

I have about 90 lbs to lose and don't expect a couple of cold baths to suddenly make me swimsuit ready, but isn't it reasonable to expect some sort of benefits by now? Something? I'm hypothyroid and perimenopausal - currently working on both, yet CT is supposed to override many of those problems. WHEN??

I think it would be _very helpful_ if those who are seeing results from CTafter a month or less would share with us how long they've been doing CT along with how much fat/weight they have to lose. It might inspire those of us with a longer road ahead to keep things in perspective.

Also, anyone started a measurements diary to record how their body size/composition is changing?

### x_danny_x — 2012-03-17T03:01:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1809>

2.5 weeks is way too early. it generally takes 6 weeks to become cold adaptive and for women it is more. women at the sametime experience more side affects of the CT experience because of the detox.

once you become cold adaptive, which again is like 6 weeks and most likely more for you since you are a woman, then you will start seeing the pounds come off and other benefits such as increase strength and better sleep. 2.5 weeks is too early. hang tuff.

im wondering if CT increases intelligence in people. if so that will be a heavy bonus since i am taking supplements for that.

>
> I have about 90 lbs to lose and don't expect a couple of cold baths to suddenly make me swimsuit ready, but isn't it reasonable to expect some sort of benefits by now? Something? I'm hypothyroid and perimenopausal - currently working on both, yet CT is supposed to override many of those problems. WHEN??
>
>
>
> I think it would be _very helpful_ if those who are seeing results from CTafter a month or less would share with us how long they've been doing CT along with how much fat/weight they have to lose. It might inspire those of us with a longer road ahead to keep things in perspective.
>
>
>
> Also, anyone started a measurements diary to record how their body size/composition is changing?

Hang in there Clabbergirl. I totally relate to the bad day/bad week downturn. I get there often.

I think that progress depends on MANY things. The fact that you're only 10 weeks into primal eating and 6 weeks into the Reset means things are just starting up. Many many people who are seeing great gains from CT have been doing the Reset for a long time and are onto the Post Leptin RX, as well as having done GAPS/primal and dealing to many of the pesky issues well before CT was ever a notion in our frozen brains. People often remind each other that progress isn't linear. Also, Dr K reminds us often that this is on the timeframe of your hypothalamus, not your wishes. From my experience, that can be a long time (or at least seem like it!). And all those detox symptoms are positive signs -things are changing!

Many people do not lose weight initially and I, for one, have found the CT process slow going. I am slow to cold adapt, very slow to warm up and am not feeling hot during the day like many others. I keep at it because I trust this process, but battling through when you're not seeing gains is difficult at best. (Remember, Dr Kruse never said his transformation was effortless. I think it was the opposite. And he was testing himself every 3 months and targeting key issues with specific interventions, something most of us cannot do.)

CT is meant to address those issues you struggle with, but I believe if it were to happen that quickly (as in just a few weeks) someone else would have been onto it before. It is a holy trinity - CT, keto paleo and the circadian cycles - and you also need to be vigilant with the Leptin RX (as well as probably the Leaky Gut RX too.)

It's a LOT to be vigilant with and a lot to keep in mind and it's bloody exhausting. I try to figure that if it took me 45 years to get this way, I have to expect it's going to take a wee while to transform me, no matter how good the methods. (I can't help with a measurement diary - I stay far away from scales and measuring tape!).

### x_danny_x — 2012-03-17T03:27:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1831>

she has been doing it for only 2.5 weeks though. 6 weeks is when it starts for some folks though women generally somewhat longer than 6 weeks for CT to actually start doing something. she is asking a bit too early for result s when she has been doing only the eating part correct with the Ketosis.

### Birdy — 2012-03-17T04:12:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1851>

> **AKMan;1580 wrote:** From a study at: [http://ukpmc.ac.uk/abstract/MED/551265](http://http://ukpmc.ac.uk/abstract/MED/551265)
>
>
>
> Measurement of energy balance during voluntary overeating in rats unequivocally establishes the quantitative importance of diet-induced thermogenesis in energy balance. Like cold-induced thermogenesis, this form of heat production involves changes in the activity of the sympathetic nervous system and brown adipose tissue which suggest that this tissue may determine metabolic efficiency and resistance to obesity.
>
>
>
> All you can see at the link is what I pasted above, you have to subscribe to see the whole study, but it looks it backs up what Dr. K. has said all along--calories become meaningless! It looks to me like adequate stores of brown fat, which you build up CT'ing, also activate when one overeats, meaning you'd burn extra calories as heat and not store it all as fat.

I already get hot and supposedly burn excess calories when I eat an excess. Do you think the CT just makes this even more so?

### Birdy — 2012-03-17T04:15:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-1852>

Kiwi, many of us here had been doing the leptin reset protocol for quite some time and had already lost weight and inches. I don't think that there have been many here like yourself who started CT without first doing the LR, so you are paving the way for others.

Thanks Kiwilauren, you said exactly what was on my mind. I too have 90 pounds to lose and it's been a very slow process for me as well. Not losing much weight. I tend to fluctuate between 8 and 12 pounds and I too keep going because I want to trust this process and I also know that it will take a while to transform. I feel your discouragement but hang in there Clabbergirl! Oh and by the way happy birthday! if i remember seeing that correctly on the stats page. I believe we will all be on an emotional roller coaster during this process at some point in time. Keep it up!

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T19:41:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-2071>

I'm gonna say get your labs done.

LR for women seems to take at least 6-8 weeks for weight loss to happen; CT should super-charge that, but there is an important caveat to this: hormones. Jack calls them the North Star. It seems that goal one should be to get your hormones under control; it's the first domino to fall. If you could get a salivary hormone test to see where you're at, that would be a good starting point. Your first goal is to get your hormones under control. Following the leaky gut Rx is good advice, too, as Jack says it doesn't matter what you eat--it matters what your body absorbs. If you're not absorbing the right nutrients, it's going to be harder for your body to heal itself.

Hang in there, and FIRST THINGS FIRST!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-18T02:41:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-2209>

> **tellmisty;2077 wrote: I'm gonna say get your labs done.
>
>
>
> LR for women seems to take at least 6-8 weeks for weight loss to happen; CT should super-charge that, but there is an important caveat to this: hormones. Jack calls them the North Star. It seems that goal one should be to get your hormones under control; it's the first domino to fall.**

**

Thank you, everyone, for the encouragement.

I did have labs done a week ago - blood test, that is. The results were posted on the MDA forum and I've recopied here:

**DHEA - Sulfate: 68.8 (ref 60.9 - 337)

**Estradiol: 144.2** (follicular phase, 12.5-166) or (ovulation, 85.8 - 498)

Free Testosterone: .2** (ref .0 - 2.2)

Progesterone: .2** (follicular, .2 - 1.5) or (ovulation, .8 - 3) or (luteal, 1.7 - 27)

44 yr-old female. Test done on approx. day 8-12 of my cycle which used to be about 30 days on average. I had a uterine ablation so I no longer have a period and can only guess where I am in it based on the cycle of my daughter who lives with me and PMS symptoms.

I realize I am estrogen dominant and trying to combat this without BHRT (is that even possible?). Hoping CT is the way for that. Since this test I've had about a week of supplementing with pregnenolone (100mg), progesterone (2 pumps a day), and DHEA (25mg) - feeling no difference yet, of course.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-21T18:06:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-3916>

Not seeing any body comp or weight loss signs at all. Considering throwing in the proverbial cold towel as I have been doing my CT baths at night so late that I'm exhausted and they are competing with my bed time. Night after night of sitting in a tub for almost an hour when I could be sleeping is starting to wear on me. So incredibly discouraged.

But in the interest of staying on topic of this thread:

Pros (and really stretching to keep this on topic): **I can see some i**mprovement in my allergies/sinus**, _I think, despite crazy high pollen count right now, but not sure if that's attributable to CT or environmental.

Also, better sleep by way of fewer wake-ups to pee_** _and sometimes waking up just before my alarm, but sleep wasn't terrible to begin with, (this could be attributed to pregnenolone/progesterone supplements that began when starting CT).

Body comp is still the same, maybe even a little more bloated around the middle than before if I get technical. Clothing fitting the same, waist the same size and same fluctuations throughout the day, fat feels and looks the same. I don't weigh myself but a doc visit a week ago showed I'd gained 4 lbs since starting Primal. Yay_. Energy has been up/down between none and 'normal for most folks' - no high energy here. Last 24 hours I have been WIPED out - like all I wanted to do was lay in bed. I've mentally forced myself to function through a job and chores, otherwise I would do nothing but sit. Physical exertion of planting bulbs in an indoor pot and 15 min of light weeding in a flower bed floored my energy and I walked back to the house like I had just run a marathon. I don't know if it's CT, but it sucks and I'm too young to feel like this. It's a shame because I really believe CT holds promise, just maybe not for me.

Are the benefits people are reporting true results, or wishful thinking? I'm beginning to wonder if we're not all seeing nails because we're holding hammers.

DO NOT GIVE UP! It will come. When I do CT, I feel completely exhausted so I know it's working. I know my body is expending the energy.

Get your labs done. Get your body fat measured. Take your measurements. And then do it on blind faith for 6 more weeks. Just the fact that you are such a mess (as well as me and all the other girls on the CT Mayhem thread) tells me that SOMETHING is going on. And don't forget to look at female olympic swimmers. They have AMAZING bodies from being in a pool all day. DO NOT GIVE UP!

I only do the baths every other night for this reason (even though I often want to do them more often). I think a good strategy would be to just slow down a bit and just do what is manageable for you. On the nights I don't bathe, I do face dunks instead, which only takes 10 minutes or so. Perhaps this might be a better alternative to giving up completely. Good luck - I hope you see the progress you are looking for soon!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-21T20:37:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-3986>

Thanks for the encouragement. I am whining and obviously struggling to keep perspective. I haven't been at this a very long time, I must remind myself. I like the idea of committing to it for X amount of weeks, and then face dunks on the nights I don't have time/motivation for a full soak. I've been spot icing on my off days as it is, and with this mounting fatigue, it's given me more time for that at least.

I was all gung-ho when I first started the primal diet and although I haven't been proselytizing, those close to me know what I'm doing, have watched me struggle to find a restaurant/meal that I could eat, seen my kitchen counter become cluttered with supplements they've never heard of, and weathered birthdays and celebrations while keeping their skepticism to themselves in an attempt to be supportive. I've watched my grocery budget increase when there was no extra room, probably doubled my water bill, and when asked what's improved, I can say...maybe _my allergies_? I'll take an improvement in allergies, fair enough. But I had hopes for much more. If I'm eating and living so much healthier, why do I look and feel just as unhealthy or worse???? My effort is a mismatch to my results. This tells me I'm doing something wrong, or maybe Fate hates me and I'm destined to be fat.

I'm definitely not a good representative for a primal diet, Leptin Reset, or CT at this point. Ugh. Sorry for such a downer post. It doesn't help that I keep getting unsolicited dieting advice from a coworker who's practically disappearing on Weight Watchers. Needing strength. Fingers crossed.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-21T20:53:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-3994>

I'm on day 11 of cold baths at 49 F for 45-60 minutes a dunk. I'm noticing some mild appetite suppression at this point. Sleep is better, overall. Strength has been hit and miss so far. Last night was a definite hit: reps on kettle bell shoulder press went from 10 up to 15, for example, easily. It was weird, I wasn't huffing and puffing as much either - like I didn't have to breathe as much. Probably all placebo effect, but who knows. Most noticeable is body composition changes. More definition is always nice, eh?

Yes, just set an amount of time to stick with it and see where it takes you. From what I know of the doctor, I think he would urge you to get your hormones tested, so that you can use that information to tailor your approach to you. The problem with the paleo template is that it then needs to be individualized to each person's specific situation.

Thinking of you and sending you strength!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-21T22:21:54+0200

I have had them tested and I'm estrogen dominant. The problem is convincing my doctor of this and treating it. I've run into a brick wall there and my insurance won't cover BHRT. It's part of the feeling of hopelessness that I keep coming down with.

### nonchalant — 2012-03-21T22:54:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-4058>

I've had a distinct improvement in my feet. I suffered from plantar faciitis for about 8-9 years. Icing was the only thing that really helped the pain, of course.

Going VLC/Paleo eventually eliminated the pain, and I was able to wear flip-flops again, yay!! But when I tried to go barefoot, my feet felt like the bones were spreading out abnormally. Kinda hard to describe. I was very afraid of injuring them again, so I continued to wear shoes everywhere, including the shower.

Now CT appears to be fixing the connective tissue, or whatever holds things in place. When I try going barefoot, my feet feel normal, though the skin is very tender! So every day now I spend a few minutes walking barefoot in my backyard, or on the carpet. Occasionally I walk on a hard floor and I don't feel afraid any more.  Thanks Dr. Kruse!

Anyone else noticed since CT that the veins on their arms are really coming out??

### Susan M. — 2012-03-22T04:23:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-4222>

Yesterday I did my 10th soak, then later in the evenings I iced my arms and waist. I believe that it is finally paying off in the terms of increased energy, after I warm back up of course. I also think I am beginning to see a change in my ribs - as in I can finally see them. I have not lost any weight yet from CTing, but interestingly, I am not thinking about food very often either. I am not really sure what my goals are with CTing, other than reversing osteopenia which I will not know the results of a DEXA scan for a long time yet. I absolutely hate being cold, but the thought of having brittle bones bothers me even more. So, I keep the faith that this process will yield good, and perhaps, unexpected results.

In my first 9 soaks I read my kindle, but yesterday I put on some headsets with some Rod Stewart (kinda loud) and it seemed to be a little more bearable, as in it didn't seem so cold even tho' my lower half was shivering like crazy. I have an earache this evening and I am not so motivated to jump in the tub, so icing it will be. I am beginning to realize that I don't have to soak EVER day. Even if it does take a little longer to adapt, so what? I don't think I will be any better off if I don't.

BTW, for those of you using the Maxi-Ice packs, if you roll one up and put a couple of rubber bands around it, then it is much easier to slip your arm through the tunnel you've made. They stay in place pretty good.

Hang in there everybody! I try to look at this as a great experiment. Looking forward to swimming in a lake this summer. I have'nt played in a lake in many, many years --- because I have never liked cold water. It has been too many years of not having any summer water fun.

Susan M.

### caroline — 2012-03-22T07:52:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-4280>

> **August;562 wrote:** I wanted to start a thread where we could list all the possible benefits of Cold Thermogenesis!
>
>
>
> I'm also wondering if the tightening of loose skin might be a possible benefit . . . or at least the ability to lose fat without the skin getting looser . . . What do you think?

I am noticing that my face is tighter - When I put face cream on it doesn't sink in like it used to - sort of sits on top - never been like that before

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-22T10:46:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-4292>

I have noticed that I am majorly exhausted after a cold soak (and I know I'm not down to temperature yet - not far to go, but definitely not down to temperature), and I am 7 cold soaks, and 13 days of cold showers and face dunks in. I am also finding that I have no appetite any more - food simply does not interest me. I feel very lethargic and don't have much interest in anything at the moment. But I am beginning to be able to drop the layers of clothing and the heating is turned off completely. My sleep is different, and I feel more rested when I get up in the mornings. I've lost 2 inches around the stomach, but no weight. However, I've had several comments asking what I've done to my face as apparently it looks much "younger". I've also noticed that I can lift heavier objects (carrying 2 full coal scuttles and not even thinking about it, rather than one and huffing and puffing), and yesterday I moved a table that I couldn't previously shift AT ALL upstairs without problems. I am also finding that physio for a knee problem is much easier, and that my knee hurts more but feels better overall. Changes are afoot, but whether they are good or bad remains to be seen. I am really thankful for these forums, as otherwise I would think I was going mad!

I am glad I did. It is great to see the actual difference. It had helped with my stomach as well, but I am not sharing those pics!

### Holly — 2012-03-22T17:32:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-benefits-of-cold-thermogenesis-thread.111/post-4439>

I have been doing CT for about 3 weeks now. I have been the laughing stock of my household. Until....my teenage sons' chin-up bar stays up in our doorway. Out of the blue one day, in front of everyone, I just grabbed it and tried to do a chin-up. I easily did 3 in a row! I have been lifting weights for a long time, but have NEVER been able to do a single chin up. This wowed everyone, including me. Now I have 2 teenage sons and 1 husband using the CT and trying to go paleo. Funny.
